verses.esv: There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job, and that man was blameless and upright, one who feared God and turned away from evil.
verses.kjv: THERE was a man in the land of Uz, whose name was Job; and that man was perfect and upright, and one that feared God, and eschewed evil.
verses.bsb: There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job. And this man was blameless and upright, fearing God and shunning evil.

verses.esv: There were born to him seven sons and three daughters.
verses.kjv: And there were born unto him seven sons and three daughters.
verses.bsb: He had seven sons and three daughters,

verses.esv: He possessed 7,000 sheep, 3,000 camels, 500 yoke of oxen, and 500 female donkeys, and very many servants, so that this man was the greatest of all the people of the east.
verses.kjv: His substance also was seven thousand sheep, and three thousand camels, and five hundred yoke of oxen, and five hundred she asses, and a very great household; so that this man was the greatest of all the men of the east.
verses.bsb: and he owned 7,000 sheep, 3,000 camels, 500 yoke of oxen, 500 female donkeys, and a very large number of servants. Job was the greatest man of all the people of the East.

verses.esv: His sons used to go and hold a feast in the house of each one on his day, and they would send and invite their three sisters to eat and drink with them.
verses.kjv: And his sons went and feasted in their houses, every one his day; and sent and called for their three sisters to eat and to drink with them.
verses.bsb: Job’s sons would take turns holding feasts in their homes, and they would invite their three sisters to eat and drink with them.

verses.esv: And when the days of the feast had run their course, Job would send and consecrate them, and he would rise early in the morning and offer burnt offerings according to the number of them all. For Job said, “It may be that my children have sinned, and cursed God in their hearts.” Thus Job did continually.
verses.kjv: And it was so, when the days of their feasting were gone about, that Job sent and sanctified them, and rose up early in the morning, and offered burnt offerings according to the number of them all: for Job said, It may be that my sons have sinned, and cursed God in their hearts. Thus did Job continually.
verses.bsb: And when the days of feasting were over, Job would send for his children to purify them, rising early in the morning to offer burnt offerings for all of them. For Job thought, “Perhaps my children have sinned and cursed God in their hearts.” This was Job’s regular practice.

verses.esv: Now there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD, and Satan also came among them.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the Lord, and Satan came also among them.
verses.bsb: One day the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD, and Satan also came with them.

verses.esv: The LORD said to Satan, “From where have you come?” Satan answered the LORD and said, “From going to and fro on the earth, and from walking up and down on it.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Satan, Whence comest thou? Then Satan answered the Lord, and said, From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.
verses.bsb: “Where have you come from?” said the LORD to Satan.  “From roaming through the earth,” he replied, “and walking back and forth in it.”

verses.esv: And the LORD said to Satan, “Have you considered my servant Job, that there is none like him on the earth, a blameless and upright man, who fears God and turns away from evil?”
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that there is none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that feareth God, and escheweth evil?
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Satan, “Have you considered My servant Job? For there is no one on earth like him, a man who is blameless and upright, who fears God and shuns evil.”

verses.esv: Then Satan answered the LORD and said, “Does Job fear God for no reason?
verses.kjv: Then Satan answered the Lord, and said, Doth Job fear God for nought?
verses.bsb: Satan answered the LORD, “Does Job fear God for nothing?

verses.esv: Have you not put a hedge around him and his house and all that he has, on every side? You have blessed the work of his hands, and his possessions have increased in the land.
verses.kjv: Hast not thou made an hedge about him, and about his house, and about all that he hath on every side? thou hast blessed the work of his hands, and his substance is increased in the land.
verses.bsb: Have You not placed a hedge on every side around him and his household and all that he owns? You have blessed the work of his hands, and his possessions have increased in the land.

verses.esv: But stretch out your hand and touch all that he has, and he will curse you to your face.”
verses.kjv: But put forth thine hand now, and touch all that he hath, and he will curse thee to thy face.
verses.bsb: But stretch out Your hand and strike all that he has, and he will surely curse You to Your face.”

verses.esv: And the LORD said to Satan, “Behold, all that he has is in your hand. Only against him do not stretch out your hand.” So Satan went out from the presence of the LORD.
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Satan, Behold, all that he hath is in thy power; only upon himself put not forth thine hand. So Satan went forth from the presence of the Lord.
verses.bsb: “Very well,” said the LORD to Satan. “Everything he has is in your hands, but you must not lay a hand on the man himself.”  Then Satan went out from the presence of the LORD.

verses.esv: Now there was a day when his sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother's house,
verses.kjv: ¶ And there was a day when his sons and his daughters were eating and drinking wine in their eldest brother’s house:
verses.bsb: One day, while Job’s sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother’s house,

verses.esv: and there came a messenger to Job and said, “The oxen were plowing and the donkeys feeding beside them,
verses.kjv: And there came a messenger unto Job, and said, The oxen were plowing, and the asses feeding beside them:
verses.bsb: a messenger came and reported to Job: “While the oxen were plowing and the donkeys were grazing nearby,

verses.esv: and the Sabeans fell upon them and took them and struck down the servants with the edge of the sword, and I alone have escaped to tell you.”
verses.kjv: And the Sabeans fell upon them, and took them away; yea, they have slain the servants with the edge of the sword; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.
verses.bsb: the Sabeans swooped down and took them away. They put the servants to the sword, and I alone have escaped to tell you!”

verses.esv: While he was yet speaking, there came another and said, “The fire of God fell from heaven and burned up the sheep and the servants and consumed them, and I alone have escaped to tell you.”
verses.kjv: While he was yet speaking, there came also another, and said, The fire of God is fallen from heaven, and hath burned up the sheep, and the servants, and consumed them; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.
verses.bsb: While he was still speaking, another messenger came and reported: “The fire of God fell from heaven. It burned and consumed the sheep and the servants, and I alone have escaped to tell you!”

verses.esv: While he was yet speaking, there came another and said, “The Chaldeans formed three groups and made a raid on the camels and took them and struck down the servants with the edge of the sword, and I alone have escaped to tell you.”
verses.kjv: While he was yet speaking, there came also another, and said, The Chaldeans made out three bands, and fell upon the camels, and have carried them away, yea, and slain the servants with the edge of the sword; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.
verses.bsb: While he was still speaking, another messenger came and reported: “The Chaldeans formed three bands, raided the camels, and took them away. They put the servants to the sword, and I alone have escaped to tell you!”

verses.esv: While he was yet speaking, there came another and said, “Your sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother's house,
verses.kjv: While he was yet speaking, there came also another, and said, Thy sons and thy daughters were eating and drinking wine in their eldest brother’s house:
verses.bsb: While he was still speaking, another messenger came and reported: “Your sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother’s house,

verses.esv: and behold, a great wind came across the wilderness and struck the four corners of the house, and it fell upon the young people, and they are dead, and I alone have escaped to tell you.”
verses.kjv: And, behold, there came a great wind from the wilderness, and smote the four corners of the house, and it fell upon the young men, and they are dead; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.
verses.bsb: when suddenly a mighty wind swept in from the desert and struck the four corners of the house. It collapsed on the young people and they are dead, and I alone have escaped to tell you!”

verses.esv: Then Job arose and tore his robe and shaved his head and fell on the ground and worshiped.
verses.kjv: Then Job arose, and rent his mantle, and shaved his head, and fell down upon the ground, and worshipped,
verses.bsb: Then Job stood up, tore his robe, and shaved his head. He fell to the ground and worshiped,

verses.esv: And he said, “Naked I came from my mother's womb, and naked shall I return. The LORD gave, and the LORD has taken away; blessed be the name of the LORD.”
verses.kjv: And said, Naked came I out of my mother’s womb, and naked shall I return thither: the Lord gave, and the Lord hath taken away; blessed be the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: saying:  “Naked I came from my mother’s womb, and naked I will return. The LORD gave, and the LORD has taken away. Blessed be the name of the LORD.”

verses.esv: In all this Job did not sin or charge God with wrong.
verses.kjv: In all this Job sinned not, nor charged God foolishly.
verses.bsb: In all this, Job did not sin or charge God with wrongdoing.

verses.esv: Again there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD, and Satan also came among them to present himself before the LORD.
verses.kjv: AGAIN there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the Lord, and Satan came also among them to present himself before the Lord.
verses.bsb: On another day the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD, and Satan also came with them to present himself before Him.

verses.esv: And the LORD said to Satan, “From where have you come?” Satan answered the LORD and said, “From going to and fro on the earth, and from walking up and down on it.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Satan, From whence comest thou? And Satan answered the Lord, and said, From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.
verses.bsb: “Where have you come from?” said the LORD to Satan.  “From roaming through the earth,” he replied, “and walking back and forth in it.”

verses.esv: And the LORD said to Satan, “Have you considered my servant Job, that there is none like him on the earth, a blameless and upright man, who fears God and turns away from evil? He still holds fast his integrity, although you incited me against him to destroy him without reason.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that there is none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that feareth God, and escheweth evil? and still he holdeth fast his integrity, although thou movedst me against him, to destroy him without cause.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Satan, “Have you considered My servant Job? For there is no one on earth like him, a man who is blameless and upright, who fears God and shuns evil. He still retains his integrity, even though you incited Me against him to ruin him without cause.”

verses.esv: Then Satan answered the LORD and said, “Skin for skin! All that a man has he will give for his life.
verses.kjv: And Satan answered the Lord, and said, Skin for skin, yea, all that a man hath will he give for his life.
verses.bsb: “Skin for skin!” Satan replied. “A man will give up all he owns in exchange for his life.

verses.esv: But stretch out your hand and touch his bone and his flesh, and he will curse you to your face.”
verses.kjv: But put forth thine hand now, and touch his bone and his flesh, and he will curse thee to thy face.
verses.bsb: But stretch out Your hand and strike his flesh and bones, and he will surely curse You to Your face.”

verses.esv: And the LORD said to Satan, “Behold, he is in your hand; only spare his life.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Satan, Behold, he is in thine hand; but save his life.
verses.bsb: “Very well,” said the LORD to Satan. “He is in your hands, but you must spare his life.”

verses.esv: So Satan went out from the presence of the LORD and struck Job with loathsome sores from the sole of his foot to the crown of his head.
verses.kjv: ¶ So went Satan forth from the presence of the Lord, and smote Job with sore boils from the sole of his foot unto his crown.
verses.bsb: So Satan went out from the presence of the LORD and infected Job with terrible boils from the soles of his feet to the crown of his head.

verses.esv: And he took a piece of broken pottery with which to scrape himself while he sat in the ashes.
verses.kjv: And he took him a potsherd to scrape himself withal; and he sat down among the ashes.
verses.bsb: And Job took a piece of broken pottery to scrape himself as he sat among the ashes.

verses.esv: Then his wife said to him, “Do you still hold fast your integrity? Curse God and die.”
verses.kjv: ¶ Then said his wife unto him, Dost thou still retain thine integrity? curse God, and die.
verses.bsb: Then Job’s wife said to him, “Do you still retain your integrity? Curse God and die!”

verses.esv: But he said to her, “You speak as one of the foolish women would speak. Shall we receive good from God, and shall we not receive evil?” In all this Job did not sin with his lips.
verses.kjv: But he said unto her, Thou speakest as one of the foolish women speaketh. What? shall we receive good at the hand of God, and shall we not receive evil? In all this did not Job sin with his lips.
verses.bsb: “You speak as a foolish woman speaks,” he told her. “Should we accept from God only good and not adversity?”  In all this, Job did not sin in what he said.

verses.esv: Now when Job's three friends heard of all this evil that had come upon him, they came each from his own place, Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite. They made an appointment together to come to show him sympathy and comfort him.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now when Job’s three friends heard of all this evil that was come upon him, they came every one from his own place; Eliphaz the Temanite, and Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite: for they had made an appointment together to come to mourn with him and to comfort him.
verses.bsb: Now when Job’s three friends—Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite—heard about all this adversity that had come upon him, each of them came from his home, and they met together to go and sympathize with Job and comfort him.

verses.esv: And when they saw him from a distance, they did not recognize him. And they raised their voices and wept, and they tore their robes and sprinkled dust on their heads toward heaven.
verses.kjv: And when they lifted up their eyes afar off, and knew him not, they lifted up their voice, and wept; and they rent every one his mantle, and sprinkled dust upon their heads toward heaven.
verses.bsb: When they lifted up their eyes from afar, they could barely recognize Job. They began to weep aloud, and each man tore his robe and threw dust in the air over his head.

verses.esv: And they sat with him on the ground seven days and seven nights, and no one spoke a word to him, for they saw that his suffering was very great.
verses.kjv: So they sat down with him upon the ground seven days and seven nights, and none spake a word unto him: for they saw that his grief was very great.
verses.bsb: Then they sat on the ground with him for seven days and seven nights, but no one spoke a word to him because they saw how intense his suffering was.

verses.esv: After this Job opened his mouth and cursed the day of his birth.
verses.kjv: AFTER this opened Job his mouth, and cursed his day.
verses.bsb: After this, Job opened his mouth and cursed the day of his birth.

verses.esv: And Job said:
verses.kjv: And Job spake, and said,
verses.bsb: And this is what he said:

verses.esv: “Let the day perish on which I was born, and the night that said, ‘A man is conceived.’
verses.kjv: Let the day perish wherein I was born, and the night in which it was said, There is a man child conceived.
verses.bsb: “May the day of my birth perish, and the night it was said, ‘A boy is conceived.’

verses.esv: Let that day be darkness! May God above not seek it, nor light shine upon it.
verses.kjv: Let that day be darkness; let not God regard it from above, neither let the light shine upon it.
verses.bsb: If only that day had turned to darkness! May God above disregard it; may no light shine upon it.

verses.esv: Let gloom and deep darkness claim it. Let clouds dwell upon it; let the blackness of the day terrify it.
verses.kjv: Let darkness and the shadow of death stain it; let a cloud dwell upon it; let the blackness of the day terrify it.
verses.bsb: May darkness and gloom reclaim it, and a cloud settle over it; may the blackness of the day overwhelm it.

verses.esv: That night—let thick darkness seize it! Let it not rejoice among the days of the year; let it not come into the number of the months.
verses.kjv: As for that night, let darkness seize upon it; let it not be joined unto the days of the year, let it not come into the number of the months.
verses.bsb: If only darkness had taken that night away! May it not appear among the days of the year; may it never be entered in any of the months.

verses.esv: Behold, let that night be barren; let no joyful cry enter it.
verses.kjv: Lo, let that night be solitary, let no joyful voice come therein.
verses.bsb: Behold, may that night be barren; may no joyful voice come into it.

verses.esv: Let those curse it who curse the day, who are ready to rouse up Leviathan.
verses.kjv: Let them curse it that curse the day, who are ready to raise up their mourning.
verses.bsb: May it be cursed by those who curse the day— those prepared to rouse Leviathan.

verses.esv: Let the stars of its dawn be dark; let it hope for light, but have none, nor see the eyelids of the morning,
verses.kjv: Let the stars of the twilight thereof be dark; let it look for light, but have none; neither let it see the dawning of the day:
verses.bsb: May its morning stars grow dark; may it wait in vain for daylight; may it not see the breaking of dawn.

verses.esv: because it did not shut the doors of my mother's womb, nor hide trouble from my eyes.
verses.kjv: Because it shut not up the doors of my mother’s womb, nor hid sorrow from mine eyes.
verses.bsb: For that night did not shut the doors of the womb to hide the sorrow from my eyes.

verses.esv: “Why did I not die at birth, come out from the womb and expire?
verses.kjv: Why died I not from the womb? why did I not give up the ghost when I came out of the belly?
verses.bsb: Why did I not perish at birth; why did I not die as I came from the womb?

verses.esv: Why did the knees receive me? Or why the breasts, that I should nurse?
verses.kjv: Why did the knees prevent me? or why the breasts that I should suck?
verses.bsb: Why were there knees to receive me, and breasts that I should be nursed?

verses.esv: For then I would have lain down and been quiet; I would have slept; then I would have been at rest,
verses.kjv: For now should I have lain still and been quiet, I should have slept: then had I been at rest,
verses.bsb: For now I would be lying down in peace; I would be asleep and at rest

verses.esv: with kings and counselors of the earth who rebuilt ruins for themselves,
verses.kjv: With kings and counsellers of the earth, which built desolate places for themselves;
verses.bsb: with kings and counselors of the earth, who built for themselves cities now in ruins,

verses.esv: or with princes who had gold, who filled their houses with silver.
verses.kjv: Or with princes that had gold, who filled their houses with silver:
verses.bsb: or with princes who had gold, who filled their houses with silver.

verses.esv: Or why was I not as a hidden stillborn child, as infants who never see the light?
verses.kjv: Or as an hidden untimely birth I had not been; as infants which never saw light.
verses.bsb: Or why was I not hidden like a stillborn child, like an infant who never sees daylight?

verses.esv: There the wicked cease from troubling, and there the weary are at rest.
verses.kjv: There the wicked cease from troubling; and there the weary be at rest.
verses.bsb: There the wicked cease from raging, and there the weary find rest.

verses.esv: There the prisoners are at ease together; they hear not the voice of the taskmaster.
verses.kjv: There the prisoners rest together; they hear not the voice of the oppressor.
verses.bsb: The captives enjoy their ease; they do not hear the voice of the oppressor.

verses.esv: The small and the great are there, and the slave is free from his master.
verses.kjv: The small and great are there; and the servant is free from his master.
verses.bsb: Both small and great are there, and the slave is freed from his master.

verses.esv: “Why is light given to him who is in misery, and life to the bitter in soul,
verses.kjv: Wherefore is light given to him that is in misery, and life unto the bitter in soul;
verses.bsb: Why is light given to the miserable, and life to the bitter of soul,

verses.esv: who long for death, but it comes not, and dig for it more than for hidden treasures,
verses.kjv: Which long for death, but it cometh not; and dig for it more than for hid treasures;
verses.bsb: who long for death that does not come, and search for it like hidden treasure,

verses.esv: who rejoice exceedingly and are glad when they find the grave?
verses.kjv: Which rejoice exceedingly, and are glad, when they can find the grave?
verses.bsb: who rejoice and greatly exult when they can find the grave?

verses.esv: Why is light given to a man whose way is hidden, whom God has hedged in?
verses.kjv: Why is light given to a man whose way is hid, and whom God hath hedged in?
verses.bsb: Why is life given to a man whose way is hidden, whom God has hedged in?

verses.esv: For my sighing comes instead of my bread, and my groanings are poured out like water.
verses.kjv: For my sighing cometh before I eat, and my roarings are poured out like the waters.
verses.bsb: I sigh when food is put before me, and my groans pour out like water.

verses.esv: For the thing that I fear comes upon me, and what I dread befalls me.
verses.kjv: For the thing which I greatly feared is come upon me, and that which I was afraid of is come unto me.
verses.bsb: For the thing I feared has overtaken me, and what I dreaded has befallen me.

verses.esv: I am not at ease, nor am I quiet; I have no rest, but trouble comes.”
verses.kjv: I was not in safety, neither had I rest, neither was I quiet; yet trouble came.
verses.bsb: I am not at ease or quiet; I have no rest, for trouble has come.”

verses.esv: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
verses.kjv: THEN Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,
verses.bsb: Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied:

verses.esv: “If one ventures a word with you, will you be impatient? Yet who can keep from speaking?
verses.kjv: If we assay to commune with thee, wilt thou be grieved? but who can withhold himself from speaking?
verses.bsb: “If one ventures a word with you, will you be wearied? Yet who can keep from speaking?

verses.esv: Behold, you have instructed many, and you have strengthened the weak hands.
verses.kjv: Behold, thou hast instructed many, and thou hast strengthened the weak hands.
verses.bsb: Surely you have instructed many, and have strengthened their feeble hands.

verses.esv: Your words have upheld him who was stumbling, and you have made firm the feeble knees.
verses.kjv: Thy words have upholden him that was falling, and thou hast strengthened the feeble knees.
verses.bsb: Your words have steadied those who stumbled; you have braced the knees that were buckling.

verses.esv: But now it has come to you, and you are impatient; it touches you, and you are dismayed.
verses.kjv: But now it is come upon thee, and thou faintest; it toucheth thee, and thou art troubled.
verses.bsb: But now trouble has come upon you, and you are weary. It strikes you, and you are dismayed.

verses.esv: Is not your fear of God your confidence, and the integrity of your ways your hope?
verses.kjv: Is not this thy fear, thy confidence, thy hope, and the uprightness of thy ways?
verses.bsb: Is your reverence not your confidence, and the uprightness of your ways your hope?

verses.esv: “Remember: who that was innocent ever perished? Or where were the upright cut off?
verses.kjv: Remember, I pray thee, who ever perished, being innocent? or where were the righteous cut off?
verses.bsb: Consider now, I plead:   Who, being innocent, has ever perished? Or where have the upright been destroyed?

verses.esv: As I have seen, those who plow iniquity and sow trouble reap the same.
verses.kjv: Even as I have seen, they that plow iniquity, and sow wickedness, reap the same.
verses.bsb: As I have observed, those who plow iniquity and those who sow trouble reap the same.

verses.esv: By the breath of God they perish, and by the blast of his anger they are consumed.
verses.kjv: By the blast of God they perish, and by the breath of his nostrils are they consumed.
verses.bsb: By the breath of God they perish, and by the blast of His anger they are consumed.

verses.esv: The roar of the lion, the voice of the fierce lion, the teeth of the young lions are broken.
verses.kjv: The roaring of the lion, and the voice of the fierce lion, and the teeth of the young lions, are broken.
verses.bsb: The lion may roar, and the fierce lion may growl, yet the teeth of the young lions are broken.

verses.esv: The strong lion perishes for lack of prey, and the cubs of the lioness are scattered.
verses.kjv: The old lion perisheth for lack of prey, and the stout lion’s whelps are scattered abroad.
verses.bsb: The old lion perishes for lack of prey, and the cubs of the lioness are scattered.

verses.esv: “Now a word was brought to me stealthily; my ear received the whisper of it.
verses.kjv: Now a thing was secretly brought to me, and mine ear received a little thereof.
verses.bsb: Now a word came to me secretly; my ears caught a whisper of it.

verses.esv: Amid thoughts from visions of the night, when deep sleep falls on men,
verses.kjv: In thoughts from the visions of the night, when deep sleep falleth on men,
verses.bsb: In disquieting visions in the night, when deep sleep falls on men,

verses.esv: dread came upon me, and trembling, which made all my bones shake.
verses.kjv: Fear came upon me, and trembling, which made all my bones to shake.
verses.bsb: fear and trembling came over me and made all my bones shudder.

verses.esv: A spirit glided past my face; the hair of my flesh stood up.
verses.kjv: Then a spirit passed before my face; the hair of my flesh stood up:
verses.bsb: Then a spirit glided past my face, and the hair on my body bristled.

verses.esv: It stood still, but I could not discern its appearance. A form was before my eyes; there was silence, then I heard a voice:
verses.kjv: It stood still, but I could not discern the form thereof: an image was before mine eyes, there was silence, and I heard a voice, saying,
verses.bsb: It stood still, but I could not discern its appearance; a form loomed before my eyes, and I heard a whispering voice:

verses.esv: ‘Can mortal man be in the right before God? Can a man be pure before his Maker?
verses.kjv: Shall mortal man be more just than God? shall a man be more pure than his maker?
verses.bsb: ‘Can a mortal be more righteous than God, or a man more pure than his Maker?

verses.esv: Even in his servants he puts no trust, and his angels he charges with error;
verses.kjv: Behold, he put no trust in his servants; and his angels he charged with folly:
verses.bsb: If God puts no trust in His servants, and He charges His angels with error,

verses.esv: how much more those who dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, who are crushed like the moth.
verses.kjv: How much less in them that dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, which are crushed before the moth?
verses.bsb: how much more those who dwell in houses of clay, whose foundations are in the dust, who can be crushed like a moth!

verses.esv: Between morning and evening they are beaten to pieces; they perish forever without anyone regarding it.
verses.kjv: They are destroyed from morning to evening: they perish for ever without any regarding it.
verses.bsb: They are smashed to pieces from dawn to dusk; unnoticed, they perish forever.

verses.esv: Is not their tent-cord plucked up within them, do they not die, and that without wisdom?’ “Call now; is there anyone who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?
verses.kjv: Doth not their excellency which is in them go away? they die, even without wisdom.
verses.bsb: Are not their tent cords pulled up, so that they die without wisdom?’

verses.esv: Surely vexation kills the fool, and jealousy slays the simple.
verses.kjv: For wrath killeth the foolish man, and envy slayeth the silly one.
verses.bsb: For resentment kills a fool, and envy slays the simple.

verses.esv: I have seen the fool taking root, but suddenly I cursed his dwelling.
verses.kjv: I have seen the foolish taking root: but suddenly I cursed his habitation.
verses.bsb: I have seen a fool taking root, but suddenly his house was cursed.

verses.esv: His children are far from safety; they are crushed in the gate, and there is no one to deliver them.
verses.kjv: His children are far from safety, and they are crushed in the gate, neither is there any to deliver them.
verses.bsb: His sons are far from safety, crushed in court without a defender.

verses.esv: The hungry eat his harvest, and he takes it even out of thorns, and the thirsty pant after his wealth.
verses.kjv: Whose harvest the hungry eateth up, and taketh it even out of the thorns, and the robber swalloweth up their substance.
verses.bsb: The hungry consume his harvest, taking it even from the thorns, and the thirsty pant after his wealth.

verses.esv: For affliction does not come from the dust, nor does trouble sprout from the ground,
verses.kjv: Although affliction cometh not forth of the dust, neither doth trouble spring out of the ground;
verses.bsb: For distress does not spring from the dust, and trouble does not sprout from the ground.

verses.esv: but man is born to trouble as the sparks fly upward.
verses.kjv: Yet man is born unto trouble, as the sparks fly upward.
verses.bsb: Yet man is born to trouble as surely as sparks fly upward.

verses.esv: “As for me, I would seek God, and to God would I commit my cause,
verses.kjv: I would seek unto God, and unto God would I commit my cause:
verses.bsb: However, if I were you, I would appeal to God and lay my cause before Him—

verses.esv: who does great things and unsearchable, marvelous things without number:
verses.kjv: Which doeth great things and unsearchable; marvellous things without number:
verses.bsb: the One who does great and unsearchable things, wonders without number.

verses.esv: he gives rain on the earth and sends waters on the fields;
verses.kjv: Who giveth rain upon the earth, and sendeth waters upon the fields:
verses.bsb: He gives rain to the earth and sends water upon the fields.

verses.esv: he sets on high those who are lowly, and those who mourn are lifted to safety.
verses.kjv: To set up on high those that be low; that those which mourn may be exalted to safety.
verses.bsb: He sets the lowly on high, so that mourners are lifted to safety.

verses.esv: He frustrates the devices of the crafty, so that their hands achieve no success.
verses.kjv: He disappointeth the devices of the crafty, so that their hands cannot perform their enterprise.
verses.bsb: He thwarts the schemes of the crafty, so that their hands find no success.

verses.esv: He catches the wise in their own craftiness, and the schemes of the wily are brought to a quick end.
verses.kjv: He taketh the wise in their own craftiness: and the counsel of the froward is carried headlong.
verses.bsb: He catches the wise in their craftiness, and sweeps away the plans of the cunning.

verses.esv: They meet with darkness in the daytime and grope at noonday as in the night.
verses.kjv: They meet with darkness in the daytime, and grope in the noonday as in the night.
verses.bsb: They encounter darkness by day and grope at noon as in the night.

verses.esv: But he saves the needy from the sword of their mouth and from the hand of the mighty.
verses.kjv: But he saveth the poor from the sword, from their mouth, and from the hand of the mighty.
verses.bsb: He saves the needy from the sword in their mouth and from the clutches of the powerful.

verses.esv: So the poor have hope, and injustice shuts her mouth.
verses.kjv: So the poor hath hope, and iniquity stoppeth her mouth.
verses.bsb: So the poor have hope, and injustice shuts its mouth.

verses.esv: “Behold, blessed is the one whom God reproves; therefore despise not the discipline of the Almighty.
verses.kjv: Behold, happy is the man whom God correcteth: therefore despise not thou the chastening of the Almighty:
verses.bsb: Blessed indeed is the man whom God corrects; so do not despise the discipline of the Almighty.

verses.esv: For he wounds, but he binds up; he shatters, but his hands heal.
verses.kjv: For he maketh sore, and bindeth up: he woundeth, and his hands make whole.
verses.bsb: For He wounds, but He also binds; He strikes, but His hands also heal.

verses.esv: He will deliver you from six troubles; in seven no evil shall touch you.
verses.kjv: He shall deliver thee in six troubles: yea, in seven there shall no evil touch thee.
verses.bsb: He will rescue you from six calamities; no harm will touch you in seven.

verses.esv: In famine he will redeem you from death, and in war from the power of the sword.
verses.kjv: In famine he shall redeem thee from death: and in war from the power of the sword.
verses.bsb: In famine He will redeem you from death, and in battle from the stroke of the sword.

verses.esv: You shall be hidden from the lash of the tongue, and shall not fear destruction when it comes.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt be hid from the scourge of the tongue: neither shalt thou be afraid of destruction when it cometh.
verses.bsb: You will be hidden from the scourge of the tongue, and will not fear havoc when it comes.

verses.esv: At destruction and famine you shall laugh, and shall not fear the beasts of the earth.
verses.kjv: At destruction and famine thou shalt laugh: neither shalt thou be afraid of the beasts of the earth.
verses.bsb: You will laugh at destruction and famine, and need not fear the beasts of the earth.

verses.esv: For you shall be in league with the stones of the field, and the beasts of the field shall be at peace with you.
verses.kjv: For thou shalt be in league with the stones of the field: and the beasts of the field shall be at peace with thee.
verses.bsb: For you will have a covenant with the stones of the field, and the wild animals will be at peace with you.

verses.esv: You shall know that your tent is at peace, and you shall inspect your fold and miss nothing.
verses.kjv: And thou shalt know that thy tabernacle shall be in peace; and thou shalt visit thy habitation, and shalt not sin.
verses.bsb: You will know that your tent is secure, and find nothing amiss when inspecting your home.

verses.esv: You shall know also that your offspring shall be many, and your descendants as the grass of the earth.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt know also that thy seed shall be great, and thine offspring as the grass of the earth.
verses.bsb: You will know that your offspring will be many, your descendants like the grass of the earth.

verses.esv: You shall come to your grave in ripe old age, like a sheaf gathered up in its season.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt come to thy grave in a full age, like as a shock of corn cometh in in his season.
verses.bsb: You will come to the grave in full vigor, like a sheaf of grain gathered in season.

verses.esv: Behold, this we have searched out; it is true. Hear, and know it for your good.”
verses.kjv: Lo this, we have searched it, so it is; hear it, and know thou it for thy good.
verses.bsb: Indeed, we have investigated, and it is true! So hear it and know for yourself.”

verses.esv: Then Job answered and said:
verses.kjv: BUT Job answered and said,
verses.bsb: Then Job replied:

verses.esv: “Oh that my vexation were weighed, and all my calamity laid in the balances!
verses.kjv: Oh that my grief were throughly weighed, and my calamity laid in the balances together!
verses.bsb: “If only my grief could be weighed and placed with my calamity on the scales.

verses.esv: For then it would be heavier than the sand of the sea; therefore my words have been rash.
verses.kjv: For now it would be heavier than the sand of the sea: therefore my words are swallowed up.
verses.bsb: For then it would outweigh the sand of the seas— no wonder my words have been rash.

verses.esv: For the arrows of the Almighty are in me; my spirit drinks their poison; the terrors of God are arrayed against me.
verses.kjv: For the arrows of the Almighty are within me, the poison whereof drinketh up my spirit: the terrors of God do set themselves in array against me.
verses.bsb: For the arrows of the Almighty have pierced me; my spirit drinks in their poison; the terrors of God are arrayed against me.

verses.esv: Does the wild donkey bray when he has grass, or the ox low over his fodder?
verses.kjv: Doth the wild ass bray when he hath grass? or loweth the ox over his fodder?
verses.bsb: Does a wild donkey bray over fresh grass, or an ox low over its fodder?

verses.esv: Can that which is tasteless be eaten without salt, or is there any taste in the juice of the mallow?
verses.kjv: Can that which is unsavoury be eaten without salt? or is there any taste in the white of an egg?
verses.bsb: Is tasteless food eaten without salt, or is there flavor in the white of an egg?

verses.esv: My appetite refuses to touch them; they are as food that is loathsome to me.
verses.kjv: The things that my soul refused to touch are as my sorrowful meat.
verses.bsb: My soul refuses to touch them; they are loathsome food to me.

verses.esv: “Oh that I might have my request, and that God would fulfill my hope,
verses.kjv: Oh that I might have my request; and that God would grant me the thing that I long for!
verses.bsb: If only my request were granted and God would fulfill my hope:

verses.esv: that it would please God to crush me, that he would let loose his hand and cut me off!
verses.kjv: Even that it would please God to destroy me; that he would let loose his hand, and cut me off!
verses.bsb: that God would be willing to crush me, to unleash His hand and cut me off!

verses.esv: This would be my comfort; I would even exult in pain unsparing, for I have not denied the words of the Holy One.
verses.kjv: Then should I yet have comfort; yea, I would harden myself in sorrow: let him not spare; for I have not concealed the words of the Holy One.
verses.bsb: It still brings me comfort, and joy through unrelenting pain, that I have not denied the words of the Holy One.

verses.esv: What is my strength, that I should wait? And what is my end, that I should be patient?
verses.kjv: What is my strength, that I should hope? and what is mine end, that I should prolong my life?
verses.bsb: What strength do I have, that I should still hope? What is my future, that I should be patient?

verses.esv: Is my strength the strength of stones, or is my flesh bronze?
verses.kjv: Is my strength the strength of stones? or is my flesh of brass?
verses.bsb: Is my strength like that of stone, or my flesh made of bronze?

verses.esv: Have I any help in me, when resource is driven from me?
verses.kjv: Is not my help in me? and is wisdom driven quite from me?
verses.bsb: Is there any help within me now that success is driven from me?

verses.esv: “He who withholds kindness from a friend forsakes the fear of the Almighty.
verses.kjv: To him that is afflicted pity should be shewed from his friend; but he forsaketh the fear of the Almighty.
verses.bsb: A despairing man should have the kindness of his friend, even if he forsakes the fear of the Almighty.

verses.esv: My brothers are treacherous as a torrent-bed, as torrential streams that pass away,
verses.kjv: My brethren have dealt deceitfully as a brook, and as the stream of brooks they pass away;
verses.bsb: But my brothers are as faithless as wadis, as seasonal streams that overflow,

verses.esv: which are dark with ice, and where the snow hides itself.
verses.kjv: Which are blackish by reason of the ice, and wherein the snow is hid:
verses.bsb: darkened because of the ice and the inflow of melting snow,

verses.esv: When they melt, they disappear; when it is hot, they vanish from their place.
verses.kjv: What time they wax warm, they vanish: when it is hot, they are consumed out of their place.
verses.bsb: but ceasing in the dry season and vanishing from their channels in the heat.

verses.esv: The caravans turn aside from their course; they go up into the waste and perish.
verses.kjv: The paths of their way are turned aside; they go to nothing, and perish.
verses.bsb: Caravans turn aside from their routes; they go into the wasteland and perish.

verses.esv: The caravans of Tema look, the travelers of Sheba hope.
verses.kjv: The troops of Tema looked, the companies of Sheba waited for them.
verses.bsb: The caravans of Tema look for water; the travelers of Sheba hope to find it.

verses.esv: They are ashamed because they were confident; they come there and are disappointed.
verses.kjv: They were confounded because they had hoped; they came thither, and were ashamed.
verses.bsb: They are confounded because they had hoped; their arrival brings disappointment.

verses.esv: For you have now become nothing; you see my calamity and are afraid.
verses.kjv: For now ye are nothing; ye see my casting down, and are afraid.
verses.bsb: For now you are of no help; you see terror, and you are afraid.

verses.esv: Have I said, ‘Make me a gift’? Or, ‘From your wealth offer a bribe for me’?
verses.kjv: Did I say, Bring unto me? or, Give a reward for me of your substance?
verses.bsb: Have I ever said, ‘Give me something; offer me a bribe from your wealth;

verses.esv: Or, ‘Deliver me from the adversary's hand’? Or, ‘Redeem me from the hand of the ruthless’?
verses.kjv: Or, Deliver me from the enemy’s hand? or, Redeem me from the hand of the mighty?
verses.bsb: deliver me from the hand of the enemy; redeem me from the grasp of the ruthless’?

verses.esv: “Teach me, and I will be silent; make me understand how I have gone astray.
verses.kjv: Teach me, and I will hold my tongue: and cause me to understand wherein I have erred.
verses.bsb: Teach me, and I will be silent. Help me understand how I have erred.

verses.esv: How forceful are upright words! But what does reproof from you reprove?
verses.kjv: How forcible are right words! but what doth your arguing reprove?
verses.bsb: How painful are honest words! But what does your argument prove?

verses.esv: Do you think that you can reprove words, when the speech of a despairing man is wind?
verses.kjv: Do ye imagine to reprove words, and the speeches of one that is desperate, which are as wind?
verses.bsb: Do you intend to correct my words, and treat as wind my cry of despair?

verses.esv: You would even cast lots over the fatherless, and bargain over your friend.
verses.kjv: Yea, ye overwhelm the fatherless, and ye dig a pit for your friend.
verses.bsb: You would even cast lots for an orphan and barter away your friend.

verses.esv: “But now, be pleased to look at me, for I will not lie to your face.
verses.kjv: Now therefore be content, look upon me; for it is evident unto you if I lie.
verses.bsb: But now, please look at me. Would I lie to your face?

verses.esv: Please turn; let no injustice be done. Turn now; my vindication is at stake.
verses.kjv: Return, I pray you, let it not be iniquity; yea, return again, my righteousness is in it.
verses.bsb: Reconsider; do not be unjust. Reconsider, for my righteousness is at stake.

verses.esv: Is there any injustice on my tongue? Cannot my palate discern the cause of calamity?
verses.kjv: Is there iniquity in my tongue? cannot my taste discern perverse things?
verses.bsb: Is there iniquity on my tongue? Can my mouth not discern malice?

verses.esv: “Has not man a hard service on earth, and are not his days like the days of a hired hand?
verses.bsb: “Is not man consigned to labor on earth? Are not his days like those of a hired hand?

verses.esv: Like a slave who longs for the shadow, and like a hired hand who looks for his wages,
verses.kjv: As a servant earnestly desireth the shadow, and as an hireling looketh for the reward of his work:
verses.bsb: Like a slave he longs for shade; like a hireling he waits for his wages.

verses.esv: so I am allotted months of emptiness, and nights of misery are apportioned to me.
verses.kjv: So am I made to possess months of vanity, and wearisome nights are appointed to me.
verses.bsb: So I am allotted months of futility, and nights of misery are appointed me.

verses.esv: When I lie down I say, ‘When shall I arise?’ But the night is long, and I am full of tossing till the dawn.
verses.kjv: When I lie down, I say, When shall I arise, and the night be gone? and I am full of tossings to and fro unto the dawning of the day.
verses.bsb: When I lie down I think: ‘When will I get up?’ But the night drags on, and I toss and turn until dawn.

verses.esv: My flesh is clothed with worms and dirt; my skin hardens, then breaks out afresh.
verses.kjv: My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust; my skin is broken, and become loathsome.
verses.bsb: My flesh is clothed with worms and encrusted with dirt; my skin is cracked and festering.

verses.esv: My days are swifter than a weaver's shuttle and come to their end without hope.
verses.kjv: My days are swifter than a weaver’s shuttle, and are spent without hope.
verses.bsb: My days are swifter than a weaver’s shuttle; they come to an end without hope.

verses.esv: “Remember that my life is a breath; my eye will never again see good.
verses.kjv: O remember that my life is wind: mine eye shall no more see good.
verses.bsb: Remember that my life is but a breath. My eyes will never again see happiness.

verses.esv: The eye of him who sees me will behold me no more; while your eyes are on me, I shall be gone.
verses.kjv: The eye of him that hath seen me shall see me no more: thine eyes are upon me, and I am not.
verses.bsb: The eye that beholds me will no longer see me. You will look for me, but I will be no more.

verses.esv: As the cloud fades and vanishes, so he who goes down to Sheol does not come up;
verses.kjv: As the cloud is consumed and vanisheth away: so he that goeth down to the grave shall come up no more.
verses.bsb: As a cloud vanishes and is gone, so he who goes down to Sheol does not come back up.

verses.esv: he returns no more to his house, nor does his place know him anymore.
verses.kjv: He shall return no more to his house, neither shall his place know him any more.
verses.bsb: He never returns to his house; his place remembers him no more.

verses.esv: “Therefore I will not restrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
verses.kjv: Therefore I will not refrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
verses.bsb: Therefore I will not restrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.

verses.esv: Am I the sea, or a sea monster, that you set a guard over me?
verses.kjv: Am I a sea, or a whale, that thou settest a watch over me?
verses.bsb: Am I the sea, or the monster of the deep, that You must keep me under guard?

verses.esv: When I say, ‘My bed will comfort me, my couch will ease my complaint,’
verses.kjv: When I say, My bed shall comfort me, my couch shall ease my complaint;
verses.bsb: When I think my bed will comfort me and my couch will ease my complaint,

verses.esv: then you scare me with dreams and terrify me with visions,
verses.kjv: Then thou scarest me with dreams, and terrifiest me through visions:
verses.bsb: then You frighten me with dreams and terrify me with visions,

verses.esv: so that I would choose strangling and death rather than my bones.
verses.kjv: So that my soul chooseth strangling, and death rather than my life.
verses.bsb: so that I would prefer strangling and death over my life in this body.

verses.esv: I loathe my life; I would not live forever. Leave me alone, for my days are a breath.
verses.kjv: I loathe it; I would not live alway: let me alone; for my days are vanity.
verses.bsb: I loathe my life! I would not live forever. Leave me alone, for my days are but a breath.

verses.esv: What is man, that you make so much of him, and that you set your heart on him,
verses.kjv: What is man, that thou shouldest magnify him? and that thou shouldest set thine heart upon him?
verses.bsb: What is man that You should exalt him, that You should set Your heart upon him,

verses.esv: visit him every morning and test him every moment?
verses.kjv: And that thou shouldest visit him every morning, and try him every moment?
verses.bsb: that You attend to him every morning, and test him every moment?

verses.esv: How long will you not look away from me, nor leave me alone till I swallow my spit?
verses.kjv: How long wilt thou not depart from me, nor let me alone till I swallow down my spittle?
verses.bsb: Will You never look away from me, or leave me alone to swallow my spittle?

verses.esv: If I sin, what do I do to you, you watcher of mankind? Why have you made me your mark? Why have I become a burden to you?
verses.kjv: I have sinned; what shall I do unto thee, O thou preserver of men? why hast thou set me as a mark against thee, so that I am a burden to myself?
verses.bsb: If I have sinned, what have I done to You, O watcher of mankind? Why have You made me Your target, so that I am a burden to You?

verses.esv: Why do you not pardon my transgression and take away my iniquity? For now I shall lie in the earth; you will seek me, but I shall not be.”
verses.kjv: And why dost thou not pardon my transgression, and take away mine iniquity? for now shall I sleep in the dust; and thou shalt seek me in the morning, but I shall not be.
verses.bsb: Why do You not pardon my transgression and take away my iniquity? For soon I will lie down in the dust; You will seek me, but I will be no more.”

verses.esv: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
verses.kjv: THEN answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,
verses.bsb: Then Bildad the Shuhite replied:

verses.esv: “How long will you say these things, and the words of your mouth be a great wind?
verses.kjv: How long wilt thou speak these things? and how long shall the words of thy mouth be like a strong wind?
verses.bsb: “How long will you go on saying such things? The words of your mouth are a blustering wind.

verses.esv: Does God pervert justice? Or does the Almighty pervert the right?
verses.kjv: Doth God pervert judgment? or doth the Almighty pervert justice?
verses.bsb: Does God pervert justice? Does the Almighty pervert what is right?

verses.esv: If your children have sinned against him, he has delivered them into the hand of their transgression.
verses.kjv: If thy children have sinned against him, and he have cast them away for their transgression;
verses.bsb: When your children sinned against Him, He gave them over to their rebellion.

verses.esv: If you will seek God and plead with the Almighty for mercy,
verses.kjv: If thou wouldest seek unto God betimes, and make thy supplication to the Almighty;
verses.bsb: But if you would earnestly seek God and ask the Almighty for mercy,

verses.esv: if you are pure and upright, surely then he will rouse himself for you and restore your rightful habitation.
verses.kjv: If thou wert pure and upright; surely now he would awake for thee, and make the habitation of thy righteousness prosperous.
verses.bsb: if you are pure and upright, even now He will rouse Himself on your behalf and restore your righteous estate.

verses.esv: And though your beginning was small, your latter days will be very great.
verses.kjv: Though thy beginning was small, yet thy latter end should greatly increase.
verses.bsb: Though your beginnings were modest, your latter days will flourish.

verses.esv: “For inquire, please, of bygone ages, and consider what the fathers have searched out.
verses.kjv: For inquire, I pray thee, of the former age, and prepare thyself to the search of their fathers:
verses.bsb: Please inquire of past generations and consider the discoveries of their fathers.

verses.esv: For we are but of yesterday and know nothing, for our days on earth are a shadow.
verses.kjv: (For we are but of yesterday, and know nothing, because our days upon earth are a shadow:)
verses.bsb: For we were born yesterday and know nothing; our days on earth are but a shadow.

verses.esv: Will they not teach you and tell you and utter words out of their understanding?
verses.kjv: Shall not they teach thee, and tell thee, and utter words out of their heart?
verses.bsb: Will they not teach you and tell you, and speak from their understanding?

verses.esv: “Can papyrus grow where there is no marsh? Can reeds flourish where there is no water?
verses.kjv: Can the rush grow up without mire? can the flag grow without water?
verses.bsb: Does papyrus grow where there is no marsh? Do reeds flourish without water?

verses.esv: While yet in flower and not cut down, they wither before any other plant.
verses.kjv: Whilst it is yet in his greenness, and not cut down, it withereth before any other herb.
verses.bsb: While the shoots are still uncut, they dry up quicker than grass.

verses.esv: Such are the paths of all who forget God; the hope of the godless shall perish.
verses.kjv: So are the paths of all that forget God; and the hypocrite’s hope shall perish:
verses.bsb: Such is the destiny of all who forget God; so the hope of the godless will perish.

verses.esv: His confidence is severed, and his trust is a spider's web.
verses.kjv: Whose hope shall be cut off, and whose trust shall be a spider’s web.
verses.bsb: His confidence is fragile; his security is in a spider’s web.

verses.esv: He leans against his house, but it does not stand; he lays hold of it, but it does not endure.
verses.kjv: He shall lean upon his house, but it shall not stand: he shall hold it fast, but it shall not endure.
verses.bsb: He leans on his web, but it gives way; he holds fast, but it does not endure.

verses.esv: He is a lush plant before the sun, and his shoots spread over his garden.
verses.kjv: He is green before the sun, and his branch shooteth forth in his garden.
verses.bsb: He is a well-watered plant in the sunshine, spreading its shoots over the garden.

verses.esv: His roots entwine the stone heap; he looks upon a house of stones.
verses.kjv: His roots are wrapped about the heap, and seeth the place of stones.
verses.bsb: His roots wrap around the rock heap; he looks for a home among the stones.

verses.esv: If he is destroyed from his place, then it will deny him, saying, ‘I have never seen you.’
verses.kjv: If he destroy him from his place, then it shall deny him, saying, I have not seen thee.
verses.bsb: If he is uprooted from his place, it will disown him, saying, ‘I never saw you.’

verses.esv: Behold, this is the joy of his way, and out of the soil others will spring.
verses.kjv: Behold, this is the joy of his way, and out of the earth shall others grow.
verses.bsb: Surely this is the joy of his way; yet others will spring from the dust.

verses.esv: “Behold, God will not reject a blameless man, nor take the hand of evildoers.
verses.kjv: Behold, God will not cast away a perfect man, neither will he help the evil doers:
verses.bsb: Behold, God does not reject the blameless, nor will He strengthen the hand of evildoers.

verses.esv: He will yet fill your mouth with laughter, and your lips with shouting.
verses.kjv: Till he fill thy mouth with laughing, and thy lips with rejoicing.
verses.bsb: He will yet fill your mouth with laughter, and your lips with a shout of joy.

verses.esv: Those who hate you will be clothed with shame, and the tent of the wicked will be no more.”
verses.kjv: They that hate thee shall be clothed with shame; and the dwelling place of the wicked shall come to nought.
verses.bsb: Your enemies will be clothed in shame, and the tent of the wicked will be no more.”

verses.esv: Then Job answered and said:
verses.kjv: THEN Job answered and said,
verses.bsb: Then Job answered:

verses.esv: “Truly I know that it is so: But how can a man be in the right before God?
verses.kjv: I know it is so of a truth: but how should man be just with God?
verses.bsb: “Yes, I know that it is so, but how can a mortal be righteous before God?

verses.esv: If one wished to contend with him, one could not answer him once in a thousand times.
verses.kjv: If he will contend with him, he cannot answer him one of a thousand.
verses.bsb: If one wished to contend with God, he could not answer Him one time out of a thousand.

verses.esv: He is wise in heart and mighty in strength —who has hardened himself against him, and succeeded?—
verses.kjv: He is wise in heart, and mighty in strength: who hath hardened himself against him, and hath prospered?
verses.bsb: God is wise in heart and mighty in strength. Who has resisted Him and prospered?

verses.esv: he who removes mountains, and they know it not, when he overturns them in his anger,
verses.kjv: Which removeth the mountains, and they know not: which overturneth them in his anger.
verses.bsb: He moves mountains without their knowledge and overturns them in His anger.

verses.esv: who shakes the earth out of its place, and its pillars tremble;
verses.kjv: Which shaketh the earth out of her place, and the pillars thereof tremble.
verses.bsb: He shakes the earth from its place, so that its foundations tremble.

verses.esv: who commands the sun, and it does not rise; who seals up the stars;
verses.kjv: Which commandeth the sun, and it riseth not; and sealeth up the stars.
verses.bsb: He commands the sun not to shine; He seals off the stars.

verses.esv: who alone stretched out the heavens and trampled the waves of the sea;
verses.kjv: Which alone spreadeth out the heavens, and treadeth upon the waves of the sea.
verses.bsb: He alone stretches out the heavens and treads on the waves of the sea.

verses.esv: who made the Bear and Orion, the Pleiades and the chambers of the south;
verses.kjv: Which maketh Arcturus, Orion, and Pleiades, and the chambers of the south.
verses.bsb: He is the Maker of the Bear and Orion, of the Pleiades and the constellations of the south.

verses.esv: who does great things beyond searching out, and marvelous things beyond number.
verses.kjv: Which doeth great things past finding out; yea, and wonders without number.
verses.bsb: He does great things beyond searching out, and wonders without number.

verses.esv: Behold, he passes by me, and I see him not; he moves on, but I do not perceive him.
verses.kjv: Lo, he goeth by me, and I see him not: he passeth on also, but I perceive him not.
verses.bsb: Were He to pass by me, I would not see Him; were He to move, I would not recognize Him.

verses.esv: Behold, he snatches away; who can turn him back? Who will say to him, ‘What are you doing?’
verses.kjv: Behold, he taketh away, who can hinder him? who will say unto him, What doest thou?
verses.bsb: If He takes away, who can stop Him? Who dares to ask Him, ‘What are You doing?’

verses.esv: “God will not turn back his anger; beneath him bowed the helpers of Rahab.
verses.kjv: If God will not withdraw his anger, the proud helpers do stoop under him.
verses.bsb: God does not restrain His anger; the helpers of Rahab cower beneath Him.

verses.esv: How then can I answer him, choosing my words with him?
verses.kjv: How much less shall I answer him, and choose out my words to reason with him?
verses.bsb: How then can I answer Him or choose my arguments against Him?

verses.esv: Though I am in the right, I cannot answer him; I must appeal for mercy to my accuser.
verses.kjv: Whom, though I were righteous, yet would I not answer, but I would make supplication to my judge.
verses.bsb: For even if I were right, I could not answer. I could only beg my Judge for mercy.

verses.esv: If I summoned him and he answered me, I would not believe that he was listening to my voice.
verses.kjv: If I had called, and he had answered me; yet would I not believe that he had hearkened unto my voice.
verses.bsb: If I summoned Him and He answered me, I do not believe He would listen to my voice.

verses.esv: For he crushes me with a tempest and multiplies my wounds without cause;
verses.kjv: For he breaketh me with a tempest, and multiplieth my wounds without cause.
verses.bsb: For He would crush me with a tempest and multiply my wounds without cause.

verses.esv: he will not let me get my breath, but fills me with bitterness.
verses.kjv: He will not suffer me to take my breath, but filleth me with bitterness.
verses.bsb: He does not let me catch my breath, but overwhelms me with bitterness.

verses.esv: If it is a contest of strength, behold, he is mighty! If it is a matter of justice, who can summon him?
verses.kjv: If I speak of strength, lo, he is strong: and if of judgment, who shall set me a time to plead?
verses.bsb: If it is a matter of strength, He is indeed mighty! If it is a matter of justice, who can summon Him?

verses.esv: Though I am in the right, my own mouth would condemn me; though I am blameless, he would prove me perverse.
verses.kjv: If I justify myself, mine own mouth shall condemn me: if I say, I am perfect, it shall also prove me perverse.
verses.bsb: Even if I were righteous, my mouth would condemn me; if I were blameless, it would declare me guilty.

verses.esv: I am blameless; I regard not myself; I loathe my life.
verses.kjv: Though I were perfect, yet would I not know my soul: I would despise my life.
verses.bsb: Though I am blameless, I have no concern for myself; I despise my own life.

verses.esv: It is all one; therefore I say, ‘He destroys both the blameless and the wicked.’
verses.kjv: This is one thing, therefore I said it, He destroyeth the perfect and the wicked.
verses.bsb: It is all the same, and so I say, ‘He destroys both the blameless and the wicked.’

verses.esv: When disaster brings sudden death, he mocks at the calamity of the innocent.
verses.kjv: If the scourge slay suddenly, he will laugh at the trial of the innocent.
verses.bsb: When the scourge brings sudden death, He mocks the despair of the innocent.

verses.esv: The earth is given into the hand of the wicked; he covers the faces of its judges— if it is not he, who then is it?
verses.kjv: The earth is given into the hand of the wicked: he covereth the faces of the judges thereof; if not, where, and who is he?
verses.bsb: The earth is given into the hand of the wicked; He blindfolds its judges. If it is not He, then who is it?

verses.esv: “My days are swifter than a runner; they flee away; they see no good.
verses.kjv: Now my days are swifter than a post: they flee away, they see no good.
verses.bsb: My days are swifter than a runner; they flee without seeing good.

verses.esv: They go by like skiffs of reed, like an eagle swooping on the prey.
verses.kjv: They are passed away as the swift ships: as the eagle that hasteth to the prey.
verses.bsb: They sweep by like boats of papyrus, like an eagle swooping down on its prey.

verses.esv: If I say, ‘I will forget my complaint, I will put off my sad face, and be of good cheer,’
verses.kjv: If I say, I will forget my complaint, I will leave off my heaviness, and comfort myself:
verses.bsb: If I were to say, ‘I will forget my complaint and change my expression and smile,’

verses.esv: I become afraid of all my suffering, for I know you will not hold me innocent.
verses.kjv: I am afraid of all my sorrows, I know that thou wilt not hold me innocent.
verses.bsb: I would still dread all my sufferings; I know that You will not acquit me.

verses.esv: I shall be condemned; why then do I labor in vain?
verses.kjv: If I be wicked, why then labour I in vain?
verses.bsb: Since I am already found guilty, why should I labor in vain?

verses.esv: If I wash myself with snow and cleanse my hands with lye,
verses.kjv: If I wash myself with snow water, and make my hands never so clean;
verses.bsb: If I should wash myself with snow and cleanse my hands with lye,

verses.esv: yet you will plunge me into a pit, and my own clothes will abhor me.
verses.kjv: Yet shalt thou plunge me in the ditch, and mine own clothes shall abhor me.
verses.bsb: then You would plunge me into the pit, and even my own clothes would despise me.

verses.esv: For he is not a man, as I am, that I might answer him, that we should come to trial together.
verses.kjv: For he is not a man, as I am, that I should answer him, and we should come together in judgment.
verses.bsb: For He is not a man like me, that I can answer Him, that we can take each other to court.

verses.esv: There is no arbiter between us, who might lay his hand on us both.
verses.kjv: Neither is there any daysman betwixt us, that might lay his hand upon us both.
verses.bsb: Nor is there a mediator between us, to lay his hand upon us both.

verses.esv: Let him take his rod away from me, and let not dread of him terrify me.
verses.kjv: Let him take his rod away from me, and let not his fear terrify me:
verses.bsb: Let Him remove His rod from me, so that His terror will no longer frighten me.

verses.esv: Then I would speak without fear of him, for I am not so in myself.
verses.kjv: Then would I speak, and not fear him; but it is not so with me.
verses.bsb: Then I would speak without fear of Him. But as it is, I am on my own.

verses.esv: “I loathe my life; I will give free utterance to my complaint; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
verses.kjv: MY soul is weary of my life; I will leave my complaint upon myself; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
verses.bsb: “I loathe my own life; I will express my complaint and speak in the bitterness of my soul.

verses.esv: I will say to God, Do not condemn me; let me know why you contend against me.
verses.kjv: I will say unto God, Do not condemn me; shew me wherefore thou contendest with me.
verses.bsb: I will say to God: Do not condemn me! Let me know why You prosecute me.

verses.esv: Does it seem good to you to oppress, to despise the work of your hands and favor the designs of the wicked?
verses.kjv: Is it good unto thee that thou shouldest oppress, that thou shouldest despise the work of thine hands, and shine upon the counsel of the wicked?
verses.bsb: Does it please You to oppress me, to reject the work of Your hands and favor the schemes of the wicked?

verses.esv: Have you eyes of flesh? Do you see as man sees?
verses.kjv: Hast thou eyes of flesh? or seest thou as man seeth?
verses.bsb: Do You have eyes of flesh? Do You see as man sees?

verses.esv: Are your days as the days of man, or your years as a man's years,
verses.kjv: Are thy days as the days of man? are thy years as man’s days,
verses.bsb: Are Your days like those of a mortal, or Your years like those of a man,

verses.esv: that you seek out my iniquity and search for my sin,
verses.kjv: That thou inquirest after mine iniquity, and searchest after my sin?
verses.bsb: that You should seek my iniquity and search out my sin—

verses.esv: although you know that I am not guilty, and there is none to deliver out of your hand?
verses.kjv: Thou knowest that I am not wicked; and there is none that can deliver out of thine hand.
verses.bsb: though You know that I am not guilty, and there is no deliverance from Your hand?

verses.esv: Your hands fashioned and made me, and now you have destroyed me altogether.
verses.kjv: Thine hands have made me and fashioned me together round about; yet thou dost destroy me.
verses.bsb: Your hands shaped me and altogether formed me. Would You now turn and destroy me?

verses.esv: Remember that you have made me like clay; and will you return me to the dust?
verses.kjv: Remember, I beseech thee, that thou hast made me as the clay; and wilt thou bring me into dust again?
verses.bsb: Please remember that You molded me like clay. Would You now return me to dust?

verses.esv: Did you not pour me out like milk and curdle me like cheese?
verses.kjv: Hast thou not poured me out as milk, and curdled me like cheese?
verses.bsb: Did You not pour me out like milk, and curdle me like cheese?

verses.esv: You clothed me with skin and flesh, and knit me together with bones and sinews.
verses.kjv: Thou hast clothed me with skin and flesh, and hast fenced me with bones and sinews.
verses.bsb: You clothed me with skin and flesh, and knit me together with bones and sinews.

verses.esv: You have granted me life and steadfast love, and your care has preserved my spirit.
verses.kjv: Thou hast granted me life and favour, and thy visitation hath preserved my spirit.
verses.bsb: You have granted me life and loving devotion, and Your care has preserved my spirit.

verses.esv: Yet these things you hid in your heart; I know that this was your purpose.
verses.kjv: And these things hast thou hid in thine heart: I know that this is with thee.
verses.bsb: Yet You concealed these things in Your heart, and I know that this was in Your mind:

verses.esv: If I sin, you watch me and do not acquit me of my iniquity.
verses.kjv: If I sin, then thou markest me, and thou wilt not acquit me from mine iniquity.
verses.bsb: If I sinned, You would take note, and would not acquit me of my iniquity.

verses.esv: If I am guilty, woe to me! If I am in the right, I cannot lift up my head, for I am filled with disgrace and look on my affliction.
verses.kjv: If I be wicked, woe unto me; and if I be righteous, yet will I not lift up my head. I am full of confusion; therefore see thou mine affliction;
verses.bsb: If I am guilty, woe to me! And even if I am righteous, I cannot lift my head. I am full of shame and aware of my affliction.

verses.esv: And were my head lifted up, you would hunt me like a lion and again work wonders against me.
verses.kjv: For it increaseth. Thou huntest me as a fierce lion: and again thou shewest thyself marvellous upon me.
verses.bsb: Should I hold my head high, You would hunt me like a lion, and again display Your power against me.

verses.esv: You renew your witnesses against me and increase your vexation toward me; you bring fresh troops against me.
verses.kjv: Thou renewest thy witnesses against me, and increasest thine indignation upon me; changes and war are against me.
verses.bsb: You produce new witnesses against me and multiply Your anger toward me. Hardships assault me in wave after wave.

verses.esv: “Why did you bring me out from the womb? Would that I had died before any eye had seen me
verses.kjv: Wherefore then hast thou brought me forth out of the womb? Oh that I had given up the ghost, and no eye had seen me!
verses.bsb: Why then did You bring me from the womb? Oh, that I had died, and no eye had seen me!

verses.esv: and were as though I had not been, carried from the womb to the grave.
verses.kjv: I should have been as though I had not been; I should have been carried from the womb to the grave.
verses.bsb: If only I had never come to be, but had been carried from the womb to the grave.

verses.esv: Are not my days few? Then cease, and leave me alone, that I may find a little cheer
verses.kjv: Are not my days few? cease then, and let me alone, that I may take comfort a little,
verses.bsb: Are my days not few? Withdraw from me, that I may have a little comfort,

verses.esv: before I go—and I shall not return— to the land of darkness and deep shadow,
verses.kjv: Before I go whence I shall not return, even to the land of darkness and the shadow of death;
verses.bsb: before I go—never to return— to a land of darkness and gloom,

verses.esv: the land of gloom like thick darkness, like deep shadow without any order, where light is as thick darkness.”
verses.kjv: A land of darkness, as darkness itself; and of the shadow of death, without any order, and where the light is as darkness.
verses.bsb: to a land of utter darkness, of deep shadow and disorder, where even the light is like darkness.”

verses.esv: Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:
verses.kjv: THEN answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said,
verses.bsb: Then Zophar the Naamathite replied:

verses.esv: “Should a multitude of words go unanswered, and a man full of talk be judged right?
verses.kjv: Should not the multitude of words be answered? and should a man full of talk be justified?
verses.bsb: “Should this stream of words go unanswered and such a speaker be vindicated?

verses.esv: Should your babble silence men, and when you mock, shall no one shame you?
verses.kjv: Should thy lies make men hold their peace? and when thou mockest, shall no man make thee ashamed?
verses.bsb: Should your babbling put others to silence? Will you scoff without rebuke?

verses.esv: For you say, ‘My doctrine is pure, and I am clean in God's eyes.’
verses.kjv: For thou hast said, My doctrine is pure, and I am clean in thine eyes.
verses.bsb: You have said, ‘My doctrine is sound, and I am pure in Your sight.’

verses.esv: But oh, that God would speak and open his lips to you,
verses.kjv: But oh that God would speak, and open his lips against thee;
verses.bsb: But if only God would speak and open His lips against you,

verses.esv: and that he would tell you the secrets of wisdom! For he is manifold in understanding. Know then that God exacts of you less than your guilt deserves.
verses.kjv: And that he would shew thee the secrets of wisdom, that they are double to that which is! Know therefore that God exacteth of thee less than thine iniquity deserveth.
verses.bsb: and disclose to you the secrets of wisdom, for true wisdom has two sides. Know then that God exacts from you less than your iniquity deserves.

verses.esv: “Can you find out the deep things of God? Can you find out the limit of the Almighty?
verses.kjv: Canst thou by searching find out God? canst thou find out the Almighty unto perfection?
verses.bsb: Can you fathom the deep things of God or discover the limits of the Almighty?

verses.esv: It is higher than heaven—what can you do? Deeper than Sheol—what can you know?
verses.kjv: It is as high as heaven; what canst thou do? deeper than hell; what canst thou know?
verses.bsb: They are higher than the heavens—what can you do? They are deeper than Sheol—what can you know?

verses.esv: Its measure is longer than the earth and broader than the sea.
verses.kjv: The measure thereof is longer than the earth, and broader than the sea.
verses.bsb: Their measure is longer than the earth and wider than the sea.

verses.esv: If he passes through and imprisons and summons the court, who can turn him back?
verses.kjv: If he cut off, and shut up, or gather together, then who can hinder him?
verses.bsb: If He comes along to imprison you, or convenes a court, who can stop Him?

verses.esv: For he knows worthless men; when he sees iniquity, will he not consider it?
verses.kjv: For he knoweth vain men: he seeth wickedness also; will he not then consider it?
verses.bsb: Surely He knows the deceit of men. If He sees iniquity, does He not take note?

verses.esv: But a stupid man will get understanding when a wild donkey's colt is born a man!
verses.kjv: For vain man would be wise, though man be born like a wild ass’s colt.
verses.bsb: But a witless man can no more become wise than the colt of a wild donkey can be born a man!

verses.esv: “If you prepare your heart, you will stretch out your hands toward him.
verses.kjv: If thou prepare thine heart, and stretch out thine hands toward him;
verses.bsb: As for you, if you direct your heart and lift up your hands to Him,

verses.esv: If iniquity is in your hand, put it far away, and let not injustice dwell in your tents.
verses.kjv: If iniquity be in thine hand, put it far away, and let not wickedness dwell in thy tabernacles.
verses.bsb: if you put away the iniquity in your hand, and allow no injustice to dwell in your tents,

verses.esv: Surely then you will lift up your face without blemish; you will be secure and will not fear.
verses.kjv: For then shalt thou lift up thy face without spot; yea, thou shalt be stedfast, and shalt not fear:
verses.bsb: then indeed you will lift up your face without shame; you will stand firm and unafraid.

verses.esv: You will forget your misery; you will remember it as waters that have passed away.
verses.kjv: Because thou shalt forget thy misery, and remember it as waters that pass away:
verses.bsb: For you will forget your misery, recalling it only as waters gone by.

verses.esv: And your life will be brighter than the noonday; its darkness will be like the morning.
verses.kjv: And thine age shall be clearer than the noonday; thou shalt shine forth, thou shalt be as the morning.
verses.bsb: Your life will be brighter than noonday; its darkness will be like the morning.

verses.esv: And you will feel secure, because there is hope; you will look around and take your rest in security.
verses.kjv: And thou shalt be secure, because there is hope; yea, thou shalt dig about thee, and thou shalt take thy rest in safety.
verses.bsb: You will be secure, because there is hope, and you will look around and lie down in safety.

verses.esv: You will lie down, and none will make you afraid; many will court your favor.
verses.kjv: Also thou shalt lie down, and none shall make thee afraid; yea, many shall make suit unto thee.
verses.bsb: You will lie down without fear, and many will court your favor.

verses.esv: But the eyes of the wicked will fail; all way of escape will be lost to them, and their hope is to breathe their last.”
verses.kjv: But the eyes of the wicked shall fail, and they shall not escape, and their hope shall be as the giving up of the ghost.
verses.bsb: But the eyes of the wicked will fail, and escape will elude them; they will hope for their last breath.”

verses.esv: Then Job answered and said:
verses.kjv: AND Job answered and said,
verses.bsb: Then Job answered:

verses.esv: “No doubt you are the people, and wisdom will die with you.
verses.kjv: No doubt but ye are the people, and wisdom shall die with you.
verses.bsb: “Truly then you are the people with whom wisdom itself will die!
