verses.esv: But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Who does not know such things as these?
verses.kjv: But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you: yea, who knoweth not such things as these?
verses.bsb: But I also have a mind; I am not inferior to you. Who does not know such things as these?

verses.esv: I am a laughingstock to my friends; I, who called to God and he answered me, a just and blameless man, am a laughingstock.
verses.kjv: I am as one mocked of his neighbour, who calleth upon God, and he answereth him: the just upright man is laughed to scorn.
verses.bsb: I am a laughingstock to my friends, though I called on God, and He answered. The righteous and upright man is a laughingstock.

verses.esv: In the thought of one who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune; it is ready for those whose feet slip.
verses.kjv: He that is ready to slip with his feet is as a lamp despised in the thought of him that is at ease.
verses.bsb: The one at ease scorns misfortune as the fate of those whose feet are slipping.

verses.esv: The tents of robbers are at peace, and those who provoke God are secure, who bring their god in their hand.
verses.kjv: The tabernacles of robbers prosper, and they that provoke God are secure; into whose hand God bringeth abundantly.
verses.bsb: The tents of robbers are safe, and those who provoke God are secure— those who carry their god in their hands.

verses.esv: “But ask the beasts, and they will teach you; the birds of the heavens, and they will tell you;
verses.kjv: But ask now the beasts, and they shall teach thee; and the fowls of the air, and they shall tell thee:
verses.bsb: But ask the animals, and they will instruct you; ask the birds of the air, and they will tell you.

verses.esv: or the bushes of the earth, and they will teach you; and the fish of the sea will declare to you.
verses.kjv: Or speak to the earth, and it shall teach thee: and the fishes of the sea shall declare unto thee.
verses.bsb: Or speak to the earth, and it will teach you; let the fish of the sea inform you.

verses.esv: Who among all these does not know that the hand of the LORD has done this?
verses.kjv: Who knoweth not in all these that the hand of the Lord hath wrought this?
verses.bsb: Which of all these does not know that the hand of the LORD has done this?

verses.esv: In his hand is the life of every living thing and the breath of all mankind.
verses.kjv: In whose hand is the soul of every living thing, and the breath of all mankind.
verses.bsb: The life of every living thing is in His hand, as well as the breath of all mankind.

verses.esv: Does not the ear test words as the palate tastes food?
verses.kjv: Doth not the ear try words? and the mouth taste his meat?
verses.bsb: Does not the ear test words as the tongue tastes its food?

verses.esv: Wisdom is with the aged, and understanding in length of days.
verses.kjv: With the ancient is wisdom; and in length of days understanding.
verses.bsb: Wisdom is found with the elderly, and understanding comes with long life.

verses.esv: “With God are wisdom and might; he has counsel and understanding.
verses.kjv: With him is wisdom and strength, he hath counsel and understanding.
verses.bsb: Wisdom and strength belong to God; counsel and understanding are His.

verses.esv: If he tears down, none can rebuild; if he shuts a man in, none can open.
verses.kjv: Behold, he breaketh down, and it cannot be built again: he shutteth up a man, and there can be no opening.
verses.bsb: What He tears down cannot be rebuilt; the man He imprisons cannot be released.

verses.esv: If he withholds the waters, they dry up; if he sends them out, they overwhelm the land.
verses.kjv: Behold, he withholdeth the waters, and they dry up: also he sendeth them out, and they overturn the earth.
verses.bsb: If He holds back the waters, they dry up, and if He releases them, they overwhelm the land.

verses.esv: With him are strength and sound wisdom; the deceived and the deceiver are his.
verses.kjv: With him is strength and wisdom: the deceived and the deceiver are his.
verses.bsb: True wisdom and power belong to Him. The deceived and the deceiver are His.

verses.esv: He leads counselors away stripped, and judges he makes fools.
verses.kjv: He leadeth counsellers away spoiled, and maketh the judges fools.
verses.bsb: He leads counselors away barefoot and makes fools of judges.

verses.esv: He looses the bonds of kings and binds a waistcloth on their hips.
verses.kjv: He looseth the bond of kings, and girdeth their loins with a girdle.
verses.bsb: He loosens the bonds placed by kings and fastens a belt around their waists.

verses.esv: He leads priests away stripped and overthrows the mighty.
verses.kjv: He leadeth princes away spoiled, and overthroweth the mighty.
verses.bsb: He leads priests away barefoot and overthrows the established.

verses.esv: He deprives of speech those who are trusted and takes away the discernment of the elders.
verses.kjv: He removeth away the speech of the trusty, and taketh away the understanding of the aged.
verses.bsb: He deprives the trusted of speech and takes away the discernment of elders.

verses.esv: He pours contempt on princes and loosens the belt of the strong.
verses.kjv: He poureth contempt upon princes, and weakeneth the strength of the mighty.
verses.bsb: He pours out contempt on nobles and disarms the mighty.

verses.esv: He uncovers the deeps out of darkness and brings deep darkness to light.
verses.kjv: He discovereth deep things out of darkness, and bringeth out to light the shadow of death.
verses.bsb: He reveals the deep things of darkness and brings deep shadows into light.

verses.esv: He makes nations great, and he destroys them; he enlarges nations, and leads them away.
verses.kjv: He increaseth the nations, and destroyeth them: he enlargeth the nations, and straiteneth them again.
verses.bsb: He makes nations great and destroys them; He enlarges nations, then disperses them.

verses.esv: He takes away understanding from the chiefs of the people of the earth and makes them wander in a trackless waste.
verses.kjv: He taketh away the heart of the chief of the people of the earth, and causeth them to wander in a wilderness where there is no way.
verses.bsb: He deprives the earth’s leaders of reason and makes them wander in a trackless wasteland.

verses.esv: They grope in the dark without light, and he makes them stagger like a drunken man.
verses.kjv: They grope in the dark without light, and he maketh them to stagger like a drunken man.
verses.bsb: They grope in the darkness without light; He makes them stagger like drunkards.

verses.esv: “Behold, my eye has seen all this, my ear has heard and understood it.
verses.kjv: LO, mine eye hath seen all this, mine ear hath heard and understood it.
verses.bsb: “Indeed, my eyes have seen all this; my ears have heard and understood.

verses.esv: What you know, I also know; I am not inferior to you.
verses.kjv: What ye know, the same do I know also: I am not inferior unto you.
verses.bsb: What you know, I also know; I am not inferior to you.

verses.esv: But I would speak to the Almighty, and I desire to argue my case with God.
verses.kjv: Surely I would speak to the Almighty, and I desire to reason with God.
verses.bsb: Yet I desire to speak to the Almighty and argue my case before God.

verses.esv: As for you, you whitewash with lies; worthless physicians are you all.
verses.kjv: But ye are forgers of lies, ye are all physicians of no value.
verses.bsb: You, however, smear with lies; you are all worthless physicians.

verses.esv: Oh that you would keep silent, and it would be your wisdom!
verses.kjv: O that ye would altogether hold your peace! and it should be your wisdom.
verses.bsb: If only you would remain silent; for that would be your wisdom!

verses.esv: Hear now my argument and listen to the pleadings of my lips.
verses.kjv: Hear now my reasoning, and hearken to the pleadings of my lips.
verses.bsb: Hear now my argument, and listen to the plea of my lips.

verses.esv: Will you speak falsely for God and speak deceitfully for him?
verses.kjv: Will ye speak wickedly for God? and talk deceitfully for him?
verses.bsb: Will you speak wickedly on God’s behalf or speak deceitfully for Him?

verses.esv: Will you show partiality toward him? Will you plead the case for God?
verses.kjv: Will ye accept his person? will ye contend for God?
verses.bsb: Would you show Him partiality or argue in His defense?

verses.esv: Will it be well with you when he searches you out? Or can you deceive him, as one deceives a man?
verses.kjv: Is it good that he should search you out? or as one man mocketh another, do ye so mock him?
verses.bsb: Would it be well when He examined you? Could you deceive Him like a man?

verses.esv: He will surely rebuke you if in secret you show partiality.
verses.kjv: He will surely reprove you, if ye do secretly accept persons.
verses.bsb: Surely He would rebuke you if you secretly showed partiality.

verses.esv: Will not his majesty terrify you, and the dread of him fall upon you?
verses.kjv: Shall not his excellency make you afraid? and his dread fall upon you?
verses.bsb: Would His majesty not terrify you? Would the dread of Him not fall upon you?

verses.esv: Your maxims are proverbs of ashes; your defenses are defenses of clay.
verses.kjv: Your remembrances are like unto ashes, your bodies to bodies of clay.
verses.bsb: Your maxims are proverbs of ashes; your defenses are defenses of clay.

verses.esv: “Let me have silence, and I will speak, and let come on me what may.
verses.kjv: Hold your peace, let me alone, that I may speak, and let come on me what will.
verses.bsb: Be silent, and I will speak. Then let come to me what may.

verses.esv: Why should I take my flesh in my teeth and put my life in my hand?
verses.kjv: Wherefore do I take my flesh in my teeth, and put my life in mine hand?
verses.bsb: Why do I put myself at risk and take my life in my own hands?

verses.esv: Though he slay me, I will hope in him; yet I will argue my ways to his face.
verses.kjv: Though he slay me, yet will I trust in him: but I will maintain mine own ways before him.
verses.bsb: Though He slay me, I will hope in Him. I will still defend my ways to His face.

verses.esv: This will be my salvation, that the godless shall not come before him.
verses.kjv: He also shall be my salvation: for an hypocrite shall not come before him.
verses.bsb: Moreover, this will be my salvation, for no godless man can appear before Him.

verses.esv: Keep listening to my words, and let my declaration be in your ears.
verses.kjv: Hear diligently my speech, and my declaration with your ears.
verses.bsb: Listen carefully to my words; let my declaration ring in your ears.

verses.esv: Behold, I have prepared my case; I know that I shall be in the right.
verses.kjv: Behold now, I have ordered my cause; I know that I shall be justified.
verses.bsb: Behold, now that I have prepared my case, I know that I will be vindicated.

verses.esv: Who is there who will contend with me? For then I would be silent and die.
verses.kjv: Who is he that will plead with me? for now, if I hold my tongue, I shall give up the ghost.
verses.bsb: Can anyone indict me? If so, I will be silent and die.

verses.esv: Only grant me two things, then I will not hide myself from your face:
verses.kjv: Only do not two things unto me: then will I not hide myself from thee.
verses.bsb: Only grant these two things to me, so that I need not hide from You:

verses.esv: withdraw your hand far from me, and let not dread of you terrify me.
verses.kjv: Withdraw thine hand far from me: and let not thy dread make me afraid.
verses.bsb: Withdraw Your hand from me, and do not let Your terror frighten me.

verses.esv: Then call, and I will answer; or let me speak, and you reply to me.
verses.kjv: Then call thou, and I will answer: or let me speak, and answer thou me.
verses.bsb: Then call me, and I will answer, or let me speak, and You can reply.

verses.esv: How many are my iniquities and my sins? Make me know my transgression and my sin.
verses.kjv: How many are mine iniquities and sins? make me to know my transgression and my sin.
verses.bsb: How many are my iniquities and sins? Reveal to me my transgression and sin.

verses.esv: Why do you hide your face and count me as your enemy?
verses.kjv: Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and holdest me for thine enemy?
verses.bsb: Why do You hide Your face and consider me as Your enemy?

verses.esv: Will you frighten a driven leaf and pursue dry chaff?
verses.kjv: Wilt thou break a leaf driven to and fro? and wilt thou pursue the dry stubble?
verses.bsb: Would You frighten a windblown leaf? Would You chase after dry chaff?

verses.esv: For you write bitter things against me and make me inherit the iniquities of my youth.
verses.kjv: For thou writest bitter things against me, and makest me to possess the iniquities of my youth.
verses.bsb: For You record bitter accusations against me and bequeath to me the iniquities of my youth.

verses.esv: You put my feet in the stocks and watch all my paths; you set a limit for the soles of my feet.
verses.kjv: Thou puttest my feet also in the stocks, and lookest narrowly unto all my paths; thou settest a print upon the heels of my feet.
verses.bsb: You put my feet in the stocks and stand watch over all my paths; You set a limit for the soles of my feet.

verses.esv: Man wastes away like a rotten thing, like a garment that is moth-eaten.
verses.kjv: And he, as a rotten thing, consumeth, as a garment that is moth eaten.
verses.bsb: So man wastes away like something rotten, like a moth-eaten garment.

verses.esv: “Man who is born of a woman is few of days and full of trouble.
verses.kjv: MAN that is born of a woman is of few days, and full of trouble.
verses.bsb: “Man, who is born of woman, is short of days and full of trouble.

verses.esv: He comes out like a flower and withers; he flees like a shadow and continues not.
verses.kjv: He cometh forth like a flower, and is cut down: he fleeth also as a shadow, and continueth not.
verses.bsb: Like a flower, he comes forth, then withers away; like a fleeting shadow, he does not endure.

verses.esv: And do you open your eyes on such a one and bring me into judgment with you?
verses.kjv: And dost thou open thine eyes upon such an one, and bringest me into judgment with thee?
verses.bsb: Do You open Your eyes to one like this? Will You bring him into judgment before You?

verses.esv: Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? There is not one.
verses.kjv: Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? not one.
verses.bsb: Who can bring out clean from unclean? No one!

verses.esv: Since his days are determined, and the number of his months is with you, and you have appointed his limits that he cannot pass,
verses.kjv: Seeing his days are determined, the number of his months are with thee, thou hast appointed his bounds that he cannot pass;
verses.bsb: Since his days are determined and the number of his months is with You, and since You have set limits that he cannot exceed,

verses.esv: look away from him and leave him alone, that he may enjoy, like a hired hand, his day.
verses.kjv: Turn from him, that he may rest, till he shall accomplish, as an hireling, his day.
verses.bsb: look away from him and let him rest, so he can enjoy his day as a hired hand.

verses.esv: “For there is hope for a tree, if it be cut down, that it will sprout again, and that its shoots will not cease.
verses.kjv: For there is hope of a tree, if it be cut down, that it will sprout again, and that the tender branch thereof will not cease.
verses.bsb: For there is hope for a tree: If it is cut down, it will sprout again, and its tender shoots will not fail.

verses.esv: Though its root grow old in the earth, and its stump die in the soil,
verses.kjv: Though the root thereof wax old in the earth, and the stock thereof die in the ground;
verses.bsb: If its roots grow old in the ground and its stump dies in the soil,

verses.esv: yet at the scent of water it will bud and put out branches like a young plant.
verses.kjv: Yet through the scent of water it will bud, and bring forth boughs like a plant.
verses.bsb: at the scent of water it will bud and put forth twigs like a sapling.

verses.esv: But a man dies and is laid low; man breathes his last, and where is he?
verses.kjv: But man dieth, and wasteth away: yea, man giveth up the ghost, and where is he?
verses.bsb: But a man dies and is laid low; he breathes his last, and where is he?

verses.esv: As waters fail from a lake and a river wastes away and dries up,
verses.kjv: As the waters fail from the sea, and the flood decayeth and drieth up:
verses.bsb: As water disappears from the sea and a river becomes parched and dry,

verses.esv: so a man lies down and rises not again; till the heavens are no more he will not awake or be roused out of his sleep.
verses.kjv: So man lieth down, and riseth not: till the heavens be no more, they shall not awake, nor be raised out of their sleep.
verses.bsb: so a man lies down and does not rise. Until the heavens are no more, he will not be awakened or roused from sleep.

verses.esv: Oh that you would hide me in Sheol, that you would conceal me until your wrath be past, that you would appoint me a set time, and remember me!
verses.kjv: O that thou wouldest hide me in the grave, that thou wouldest keep me secret, until thy wrath be past, that thou wouldest appoint me a set time, and remember me!
verses.bsb: If only You would hide me in Sheol and conceal me until Your anger has passed! If only You would appoint a time for me and then remember me!

verses.esv: If a man dies, shall he live again? All the days of my service I would wait, till my renewal should come.
verses.kjv: If a man die, shall he live again? all the days of my appointed time will I wait, till my change come.
verses.bsb: When a man dies, will he live again? All the days of my hard service I will wait, until my renewal comes.

verses.esv: You would call, and I would answer you; you would long for the work of your hands.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt call, and I will answer thee: thou wilt have a desire to the work of thine hands.
verses.bsb: You will call, and I will answer; You will desire the work of Your hands.

verses.esv: For then you would number my steps; you would not keep watch over my sin;
verses.kjv: For now thou numberest my steps: dost thou not watch over my sin?
verses.bsb: For then You would count my steps, but would not keep track of my sin.

verses.esv: my transgression would be sealed up in a bag, and you would cover over my iniquity.
verses.kjv: My transgression is sealed up in a bag, and thou sewest up mine iniquity.
verses.bsb: My transgression would be sealed in a bag, and You would cover over my iniquity.

verses.esv: “But the mountain falls and crumbles away, and the rock is removed from its place;
verses.kjv: And surely the mountain falling cometh to nought, and the rock is removed out of his place.
verses.bsb: But as a mountain erodes and crumbles and a rock is dislodged from its place,

verses.esv: the waters wear away the stones; the torrents wash away the soil of the earth; so you destroy the hope of man.
verses.kjv: The waters wear the stones: thou washest away the things which grow out of the dust of the earth; and thou destroyest the hope of man.
verses.bsb: as water wears away the stones and torrents wash away the soil, so You destroy a man’s hope.

verses.esv: You prevail forever against him, and he passes; you change his countenance, and send him away.
verses.kjv: Thou prevailest for ever against him, and he passeth: thou changest his countenance, and sendest him away.
verses.bsb: You forever overpower him, and he passes on; You change his countenance and send him away.

verses.esv: His sons come to honor, and he does not know it; they are brought low, and he perceives it not.
verses.kjv: His sons come to honour, and he knoweth it not; and they are brought low, but he perceiveth it not of them.
verses.bsb: If his sons receive honor, he does not know it; if they are brought low, he is unaware.

verses.esv: He feels only the pain of his own body, and he mourns only for himself.”
verses.kjv: But his flesh upon him shall have pain, and his soul within him shall mourn.
verses.bsb: He feels only the pain of his own body and mourns only for himself.”

verses.esv: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
verses.kjv: THEN answered Eliphaz the Temanite, and said,
verses.bsb: Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied:

verses.esv: “Should a wise man answer with windy knowledge, and fill his belly with the east wind?
verses.kjv: Should a wise man utter vain knowledge, and fill his belly with the east wind?
verses.bsb: “Does a wise man answer with empty counsel or fill his belly with the hot east wind?

verses.esv: Should he argue in unprofitable talk, or in words with which he can do no good?
verses.kjv: Should he reason with unprofitable talk? or with speeches wherewith he can do no good?
verses.bsb: Should he argue with useless words or speeches that serve no purpose?

verses.esv: But you are doing away with the fear of God and hindering meditation before God.
verses.kjv: Yea, thou castest off fear, and restrainest prayer before God.
verses.bsb: But you even undermine the fear of God and hinder meditation before Him.

verses.esv: For your iniquity teaches your mouth, and you choose the tongue of the crafty.
verses.kjv: For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity, and thou choosest the tongue of the crafty.
verses.bsb: For your iniquity instructs your mouth, and you choose the language of the crafty.

verses.esv: Your own mouth condemns you, and not I; your own lips testify against you.
verses.kjv: Thine own mouth condemneth thee, and not I: yea, thine own lips testify against thee.
verses.bsb: Your own mouth, not mine, condemns you; your own lips testify against you.

verses.esv: “Are you the first man who was born? Or were you brought forth before the hills?
verses.kjv: Art thou the first man that was born? or wast thou made before the hills?
verses.bsb: Were you the first man ever born? Were you brought forth before the hills?

verses.esv: Have you listened in the council of God? And do you limit wisdom to yourself?
verses.kjv: Hast thou heard the secret of God? and dost thou restrain wisdom to thyself?
verses.bsb: Do you listen in on the council of God or limit wisdom to yourself?

verses.esv: What do you know that we do not know? What do you understand that is not clear to us?
verses.kjv: What knowest thou, that we know not? what understandest thou, which is not in us?
verses.bsb: What do you know that we do not? What do you understand that is not clear to us?

verses.esv: Both the gray-haired and the aged are among us, older than your father.
verses.kjv: With us are both the grayheaded and very aged men, much elder than thy father.
verses.bsb: Both the gray-haired and the aged are on our side— men much older than your father.

verses.esv: Are the comforts of God too small for you, or the word that deals gently with you?
verses.kjv: Are the consolations of God small with thee? is there any secret thing with thee?
verses.bsb: Are the consolations of God not enough for you, even words spoken gently to you?

verses.esv: Why does your heart carry you away, and why do your eyes flash,
verses.kjv: Why doth thine heart carry thee away? and what do thy eyes wink at,
verses.bsb: Why has your heart carried you away, and why do your eyes flash,

verses.esv: that you turn your spirit against God and bring such words out of your mouth?
verses.kjv: That thou turnest thy spirit against God, and lettest such words go out of thy mouth?
verses.bsb: as you turn your spirit against God and pour such words from your mouth?

verses.esv: What is man, that he can be pure? Or he who is born of a woman, that he can be righteous?
verses.kjv: What is man, that he should be clean? and he which is born of a woman, that he should be righteous?
verses.bsb: What is man, that he should be pure, or one born of woman, that he should be righteous?

verses.esv: Behold, God puts no trust in his holy ones, and the heavens are not pure in his sight;
verses.kjv: Behold, he putteth no trust in his saints; yea, the heavens are not clean in his sight.
verses.bsb: If God puts no trust in His holy ones, if even the heavens are not pure in His eyes,

verses.esv: how much less one who is abominable and corrupt, a man who drinks injustice like water!
verses.kjv: How much more abominable and filthy is man, which drinketh iniquity like water?
verses.bsb: how much less man, who is vile and corrupt, who drinks injustice like water?

verses.esv: “I will show you; hear me, and what I have seen I will declare
verses.kjv: I will shew thee, hear me; and that which I have seen I will declare;
verses.bsb: Listen to me and I will inform you. I will describe what I have seen,

verses.esv: (what wise men have told, without hiding it from their fathers,
verses.kjv: Which wise men have told from their fathers, and have not hid it:
verses.bsb: what was declared by wise men and was not concealed from their fathers,

verses.esv: to whom alone the land was given, and no stranger passed among them).
verses.kjv: Unto whom alone the earth was given, and no stranger passed among them.
verses.bsb: to whom alone the land was given when no foreigner passed among them.

verses.esv: The wicked man writhes in pain all his days, through all the years that are laid up for the ruthless.
verses.kjv: The wicked man travaileth with pain all his days, and the number of years is hidden to the oppressor.
verses.bsb: A wicked man writhes in pain all his days; only a few years are reserved for the ruthless.

verses.esv: Dreadful sounds are in his ears; in prosperity the destroyer will come upon him.
verses.kjv: A dreadful sound is in his ears: in prosperity the destroyer shall come upon him.
verses.bsb: Sounds of terror fill his ears; in his prosperity the destroyer attacks him.

verses.esv: He does not believe that he will return out of darkness, and he is marked for the sword.
verses.kjv: He believeth not that he shall return out of darkness, and he is waited for of the sword.
verses.bsb: He despairs of his return from darkness; he is marked for the sword.

verses.esv: He wanders abroad for bread, saying, ‘Where is it?’ He knows that a day of darkness is ready at his hand;
verses.kjv: He wandereth abroad for bread, saying, Where is it? he knoweth that the day of darkness is ready at his hand.
verses.bsb: He wanders about as food for vultures; he knows the day of darkness is at hand.

verses.esv: distress and anguish terrify him; they prevail against him, like a king ready for battle.
verses.kjv: Trouble and anguish shall make him afraid; they shall prevail against him, as a king ready to the battle.
verses.bsb: Distress and anguish terrify him, overwhelming him like a king poised to attack.

verses.esv: Because he has stretched out his hand against God and defies the Almighty,
verses.kjv: For he stretcheth out his hand against God, and strengtheneth himself against the Almighty.
verses.bsb: For he has stretched out his hand against God and has vaunted himself against the Almighty,

verses.esv: running stubbornly against him with a thickly bossed shield;
verses.kjv: He runneth upon him, even on his neck, upon the thick bosses of his bucklers:
verses.bsb: rushing headlong at Him with a thick, studded shield.

verses.esv: because he has covered his face with his fat and gathered fat upon his waist
verses.kjv: Because he covereth his face with his fatness, and maketh collops of fat on his flanks.
verses.bsb: Though his face is covered with fat and his waistline bulges with flesh,

verses.esv: and has lived in desolate cities, in houses that none should inhabit, which were ready to become heaps of ruins;
verses.kjv: And he dwelleth in desolate cities, and in houses which no man inhabiteth, which are ready to become heaps.
verses.bsb: he will dwell in ruined cities, in abandoned houses destined to become rubble.

verses.esv: he will not be rich, and his wealth will not endure, nor will his possessions spread over the earth;
verses.kjv: He shall not be rich, neither shall his substance continue, neither shall he prolong the perfection thereof upon the earth.
verses.bsb: He will no longer be rich; his wealth will not endure. His possessions will not overspread the land.

verses.esv: he will not depart from darkness; the flame will dry up his shoots, and by the breath of his mouth he will depart.
verses.kjv: He shall not depart out of darkness; the flame shall dry up his branches, and by the breath of his mouth shall he go away.
verses.bsb: He will not escape from the darkness; the flame will wither his shoots, and the breath of God’s mouth will carry him away.

verses.esv: Let him not trust in emptiness, deceiving himself, for emptiness will be his payment.
verses.kjv: Let not him that is deceived trust in vanity: for vanity shall be his recompence.
verses.bsb: Let him not deceive himself with trust in emptiness, for emptiness will be his reward.

verses.esv: It will be paid in full before his time, and his branch will not be green.
verses.kjv: It shall be accomplished before his time, and his branch shall not be green.
verses.bsb: It will be paid in full before his time, and his branch will not flourish.

verses.esv: He will shake off his unripe grape like the vine, and cast off his blossom like the olive tree.
verses.kjv: He shall shake off his unripe grape as the vine, and shall cast off his flower as the olive.
verses.bsb: He will be like a vine stripped of its unripe grapes, like an olive tree that sheds its blossoms.

verses.esv: For the company of the godless is barren, and fire consumes the tents of bribery.
verses.kjv: For the congregation of hypocrites shall be desolate, and fire shall consume the tabernacles of bribery.
verses.bsb: For the company of the godless will be barren, and fire will consume the tents of bribery.

verses.esv: They conceive trouble and give birth to evil, and their womb prepares deceit.”
verses.kjv: They conceive mischief, and bring forth vanity, and their belly prepareth deceit.
verses.bsb: They conceive trouble and give birth to evil; their womb is pregnant with deceit.”

verses.esv: Then Job answered and said:
verses.kjv: THEN Job answered and said,
verses.bsb: Then Job answered:

verses.esv: “I have heard many such things; miserable comforters are you all.
verses.kjv: I have heard many such things: miserable comforters are ye all.
verses.bsb: “I have heard many things like these; miserable comforters are you all.

verses.esv: Shall windy words have an end? Or what provokes you that you answer?
verses.kjv: Shall vain words have an end? or what emboldeneth thee that thou answerest?
verses.bsb: Is there no end to your long-winded speeches? What provokes you to continue testifying?

verses.esv: I also could speak as you do, if you were in my place; I could join words together against you and shake my head at you.
verses.kjv: I also could speak as ye do: if your soul were in my soul’s stead, I could heap up words against you, and shake mine head at you.
verses.bsb: I could also speak like you if you were in my place; I could heap up words against you and shake my head at you.

verses.esv: I could strengthen you with my mouth, and the solace of my lips would assuage your pain.
verses.kjv: But I would strengthen you with my mouth, and the moving of my lips should asswage your grief.
verses.bsb: But I would encourage you with my mouth, and the consolation of my lips would bring relief.

verses.esv: “If I speak, my pain is not assuaged, and if I forbear, how much of it leaves me?
verses.kjv: Though I speak, my grief is not asswaged: and though I forbear, what am I eased?
verses.bsb: Even if I speak, my pain is not relieved, and if I hold back, how will it go away?

verses.esv: Surely now God has worn me out; he has made desolate all my company.
verses.kjv: But now he hath made me weary: thou hast made desolate all my company.
verses.bsb: Surely He has now exhausted me; You have devastated all my family.

verses.esv: And he has shriveled me up, which is a witness against me, and my leanness has risen up against me; it testifies to my face.
verses.kjv: And thou hast filled me with wrinkles, which is a witness against me: and my leanness rising up in me beareth witness to my face.
verses.bsb: You have bound me, and it has become a witness; my frailty rises up and testifies against me.

verses.esv: He has torn me in his wrath and hated me; he has gnashed his teeth at me; my adversary sharpens his eyes against me.
verses.kjv: He teareth me in his wrath, who hateth me: he gnasheth upon me with his teeth; mine enemy sharpeneth his eyes upon me.
verses.bsb: His anger has torn me and opposed me; He gnashes His teeth at me. My adversary pierces me with His eyes.

verses.esv: Men have gaped at me with their mouth; they have struck me insolently on the cheek; they mass themselves together against me.
verses.kjv: They have gaped upon me with their mouth; they have smitten me upon the cheek reproachfully; they have gathered themselves together against me.
verses.bsb: They open their mouths against me and strike my cheeks with contempt; they join together against me.

verses.esv: God gives me up to the ungodly and casts me into the hands of the wicked.
verses.kjv: God hath delivered me to the ungodly, and turned me over into the hands of the wicked.
verses.bsb: God has delivered me to unjust men; He has thrown me to the clutches of the wicked.

verses.esv: I was at ease, and he broke me apart; he seized me by the neck and dashed me to pieces; he set me up as his target;
verses.kjv: I was at ease, but he hath broken me asunder: he hath also taken me by my neck, and shaken me to pieces, and set me up for his mark.
verses.bsb: I was at ease, but He shattered me; He seized me by the neck and crushed me. He has set me up as His target;

verses.esv: his archers surround me. He slashes open my kidneys and does not spare; he pours out my gall on the ground.
verses.kjv: His archers compass me round about, he cleaveth my reins asunder, and doth not spare; he poureth out my gall upon the ground.
verses.bsb: His archers surround me. He pierces my kidneys without mercy and spills my gall on the ground.

verses.esv: He breaks me with breach upon breach; he runs upon me like a warrior.
verses.kjv: He breaketh me with breach upon breach, he runneth upon me like a giant.
verses.bsb: He breaks me with wound upon wound; He rushes me like a mighty warrior.

verses.esv: I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin and have laid my strength in the dust.
verses.kjv: I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin, and defiled my horn in the dust.
verses.bsb: I have sewn sackcloth over my skin; I have buried my horn in the dust.

verses.esv: My face is red with weeping, and on my eyelids is deep darkness,
verses.kjv: My face is foul with weeping, and on my eyelids is the shadow of death;
verses.bsb: My face is red with weeping, and deep shadows ring my eyes;

verses.esv: although there is no violence in my hands, and my prayer is pure.
verses.kjv: Not for any injustice in mine hands: also my prayer is pure.
verses.bsb: yet my hands are free of violence and my prayer is pure.

verses.esv: “O earth, cover not my blood, and let my cry find no resting place.
verses.kjv: O earth, cover not thou my blood, and let my cry have no place.
verses.bsb: O earth, do not cover my blood; may my cry for help never be laid to rest.

verses.esv: Even now, behold, my witness is in heaven, and he who testifies for me is on high.
verses.kjv: Also now, behold, my witness is in heaven, and my record is on high.
verses.bsb: Even now my witness is in heaven, and my advocate is on high.

verses.esv: My friends scorn me; my eye pours out tears to God,
verses.kjv: My friends scorn me: but mine eye poureth out tears unto God.
verses.bsb: My friends are my scoffers as my eyes pour out tears to God.

verses.esv: that he would argue the case of a man with God, as a son of man does with his neighbor.
verses.kjv: O that one might plead for a man with God, as a man pleadeth for his neighbour!
verses.bsb: Oh, that a man might plead with God as he pleads with his neighbor!

verses.esv: For when a few years have come I shall go the way from which I shall not return.
verses.kjv: When a few years are come, then I shall go the way whence I shall not return.
verses.bsb: For when only a few years are past I will go the way of no return.

verses.esv: “My spirit is broken; my days are extinct; the graveyard is ready for me.
verses.kjv: MY breath is corrupt, my days are extinct, the graves are ready for me.
verses.bsb: “My spirit is broken; my days are extinguished; the grave awaits me.

verses.esv: Surely there are mockers about me, and my eye dwells on their provocation.
verses.kjv: Are there not mockers with me? and doth not mine eye continue in their provocation?
verses.bsb: Surely mockers surround me, and my eyes must gaze at their rebellion.

verses.esv: “Lay down a pledge for me with you; who is there who will put up security for me?
verses.kjv: Lay down now, put me in a surety with thee; who is he that will strike hands with me?
verses.bsb: Give me, I pray, the pledge You demand. Who else will be my guarantor?

verses.esv: Since you have closed their hearts to understanding, therefore you will not let them triumph.
verses.kjv: For thou hast hid their heart from understanding: therefore shalt thou not exalt them.
verses.bsb: You have closed their minds to understanding; therefore You will not exalt them.

verses.esv: He who informs against his friends to get a share of their property— the eyes of his children will fail.
verses.kjv: He that speaketh flattery to his friends, even the eyes of his children shall fail.
verses.bsb: If a man denounces his friends for a price, the eyes of his children will fail.

verses.esv: “He has made me a byword of the peoples, and I am one before whom men spit.
verses.kjv: He hath made me also a byword of the people; and aforetime I was as a tabret.
verses.bsb: He has made me a byword among the people, a man in whose face they spit.

verses.esv: My eye has grown dim from vexation, and all my members are like a shadow.
verses.kjv: Mine eye also is dim by reason of sorrow, and all my members are as a shadow.
verses.bsb: My eyes have grown dim with grief, and my whole body is but a shadow.

verses.esv: The upright are appalled at this, and the innocent stirs himself up against the godless.
verses.kjv: Upright men shall be astonied at this, and the innocent shall stir up himself against the hypocrite.
verses.bsb: The upright are appalled at this, and the innocent are stirred against the godless.

verses.esv: Yet the righteous holds to his way, and he who has clean hands grows stronger and stronger.
verses.kjv: The righteous also shall hold on his way, and he that hath clean hands shall be stronger and stronger.
verses.bsb: Yet a righteous one holds to his way, and the one with clean hands grows stronger.

verses.esv: But you, come on again, all of you, and I shall not find a wise man among you.
verses.kjv: But as for you all, do ye return, and come now: for I cannot find one wise man among you.
verses.bsb: But come back and try again, all of you. For I will not find a wise man among you.

verses.esv: My days are past; my plans are broken off, the desires of my heart.
verses.kjv: My days are past, my purposes are broken off, even the thoughts of my heart.
verses.bsb: My days have passed; my plans are broken off— even the desires of my heart.

verses.esv: They make night into day: ‘The light,’ they say, ‘is near to the darkness.’
verses.kjv: They change the night into day: the light is short because of darkness.
verses.bsb: They have turned night into day, making light seem near in the face of darkness.

verses.esv: If I hope for Sheol as my house, if I make my bed in darkness,
verses.kjv: If I wait, the grave is mine house: I have made my bed in the darkness.
verses.bsb: If I look for Sheol as my home, if I spread out my bed in darkness,

verses.esv: if I say to the pit, ‘You are my father,’ and to the worm, ‘My mother,’ or ‘My sister,’
verses.kjv: I have said to corruption, Thou art my father: to the worm, Thou art my mother, and my sister.
verses.bsb: and say to corruption, ‘You are my father,’ and to the worm, ‘My mother,’ or ‘My sister,’

verses.esv: where then is my hope? Who will see my hope?
verses.kjv: And where is now my hope? as for my hope, who shall see it?
verses.bsb: where then is my hope? Who can see any hope for me?

verses.esv: Will it go down to the bars of Sheol? Shall we descend together into the dust?”
verses.kjv: They shall go down to the bars of the pit, when our rest together is in the dust.
verses.bsb: Will it go down to the gates of Sheol? Will we go down together into the dust?”

verses.esv: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
verses.kjv: THEN answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,
verses.bsb: Then Bildad the Shuhite replied:

verses.esv: “How long will you hunt for words? Consider, and then we will speak.
verses.kjv: How long will it be ere ye make an end of words? mark, and afterwards we will speak.
verses.bsb: “How long until you end these speeches? Show some sense, and then we can talk.

verses.esv: Why are we counted as cattle? Why are we stupid in your sight?
verses.kjv: Wherefore are we counted as beasts, and reputed vile in your sight?
verses.bsb: Why are we regarded as cattle, as stupid in your sight?

verses.esv: You who tear yourself in your anger, shall the earth be forsaken for you, or the rock be removed out of its place?
verses.kjv: He teareth himself in his anger: shall the earth be forsaken for thee? and shall the rock be removed out of his place?
verses.bsb: You who tear yourself in anger— should the earth be forsaken on your account, or the rocks be moved from their place?

verses.esv: “Indeed, the light of the wicked is put out, and the flame of his fire does not shine.
verses.kjv: Yea, the light of the wicked shall be put out, and the spark of his fire shall not shine.
verses.bsb: Indeed, the lamp of the wicked is extinguished; the flame of his fire does not glow.

verses.esv: The light is dark in his tent, and his lamp above him is put out.
verses.kjv: The light shall be dark in his tabernacle, and his candle shall be put out with him.
verses.bsb: The light in his tent grows dark, and the lamp beside him goes out.

verses.esv: His strong steps are shortened, and his own schemes throw him down.
verses.kjv: The steps of his strength shall be straitened, and his own counsel shall cast him down.
verses.bsb: His vigorous stride is shortened, and his own schemes trip him up.

verses.esv: For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he walks on its mesh.
verses.kjv: For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he walketh upon a snare.
verses.bsb: For his own feet lead him into a net, and he wanders into its mesh.

verses.esv: A trap seizes him by the heel; a snare lays hold of him.
verses.kjv: The gin shall take him by the heel, and the robber shall prevail against him.
verses.bsb: A trap seizes his heel; a snare grips him.

verses.esv: A rope is hidden for him in the ground, a trap for him in the path.
verses.kjv: The snare is laid for him in the ground, and a trap for him in the way.
verses.bsb: A noose is hidden in the ground, and a trap lies in his path.

verses.esv: Terrors frighten him on every side, and chase him at his heels.
verses.kjv: Terrors shall make him afraid on every side, and shall drive him to his feet.
verses.bsb: Terrors frighten him on every side and harass his every step.

verses.esv: His strength is famished, and calamity is ready for his stumbling.
verses.kjv: His strength shall be hungerbitten, and destruction shall be ready at his side.
verses.bsb: His strength is depleted, and calamity is ready at his side.

verses.esv: It consumes the parts of his skin; the firstborn of death consumes his limbs.
verses.kjv: It shall devour the strength of his skin: even the firstborn of death shall devour his strength.
verses.bsb: It devours patches of his skin; the firstborn of death devours his limbs.

verses.esv: He is torn from the tent in which he trusted and is brought to the king of terrors.
verses.kjv: His confidence shall be rooted out of his tabernacle, and it shall bring him to the king of terrors.
verses.bsb: He is torn from the shelter of his tent and is marched off to the king of terrors.

verses.esv: In his tent dwells that which is none of his; sulfur is scattered over his habitation.
verses.kjv: It shall dwell in his tabernacle, because it is none of his: brimstone shall be scattered upon his habitation.
verses.bsb: Fire resides in his tent; burning sulfur rains down on his dwelling.

verses.esv: His roots dry up beneath, and his branches wither above.
verses.kjv: His roots shall be dried up beneath, and above shall his branch be cut off.
verses.bsb: The roots beneath him dry up, and the branches above him wither away.

verses.esv: His memory perishes from the earth, and he has no name in the street.
verses.kjv: His remembrance shall perish from the earth, and he shall have no name in the street.
verses.bsb: The memory of him perishes from the earth, and he has no name in the land.

verses.esv: He is thrust from light into darkness, and driven out of the world.
verses.kjv: He shall be driven from light into darkness, and chased out of the world.
verses.bsb: He is driven from light into darkness and is chased from the inhabited world.

verses.esv: He has no posterity or progeny among his people, and no survivor where he used to live.
verses.kjv: He shall neither have son nor nephew among his people, nor any remaining in his dwellings.
verses.bsb: He has no offspring or posterity among his people, no survivor where he once lived.

verses.esv: They of the west are appalled at his day, and horror seizes them of the east.
verses.kjv: They that come after him shall be astonied at his day, as they that went before were affrighted.
verses.bsb: Those in the west are appalled at his fate, while those in the east tremble in horror.

verses.esv: Surely such are the dwellings of the unrighteous, such is the place of him who knows not God.”
verses.kjv: Surely such are the dwellings of the wicked, and this is the place of him that knoweth not God.
verses.bsb: Surely such is the dwelling of the wicked and the place of one who does not know God.”

verses.esv: Then Job answered and said:
verses.kjv: THEN Job answered and said,
verses.bsb: Then Job answered:

verses.esv: “How long will you torment me and break me in pieces with words?
verses.kjv: How long will ye vex my soul, and break me in pieces with words?
verses.bsb: “How long will you torment me and crush me with your words?

verses.esv: These ten times you have cast reproach upon me; are you not ashamed to wrong me?
verses.kjv: These ten times have ye reproached me: ye are not ashamed that ye make yourselves strange to me.
verses.bsb: Ten times now you have reproached me; you shamelessly mistreat me.

verses.esv: And even if it be true that I have erred, my error remains with myself.
verses.kjv: And be it indeed that I have erred, mine error remaineth with myself.
verses.bsb: Even if I have truly gone astray, my error concerns me alone.

verses.esv: If indeed you magnify yourselves against me and make my disgrace an argument against me,
verses.kjv: If indeed ye will magnify yourselves against me, and plead against me my reproach:
verses.bsb: If indeed you would exalt yourselves above me and use my disgrace against me,

verses.esv: know then that God has put me in the wrong and closed his net about me.
verses.kjv: Know now that God hath overthrown me, and hath compassed me with his net.
verses.bsb: then understand that it is God who has wronged me and drawn His net around me.

verses.esv: Behold, I cry out, ‘Violence!’ but I am not answered; I call for help, but there is no justice.
verses.kjv: Behold, I cry out of wrong, but I am not heard: I cry aloud, but there is no judgment.
verses.bsb: Though I cry out, ‘Violence!’ I get no response; though I call for help, there is no justice.

verses.esv: He has walled up my way, so that I cannot pass, and he has set darkness upon my paths.
verses.kjv: He hath fenced up my way that I cannot pass, and he hath set darkness in my paths.
verses.bsb: He has blocked my way so I cannot pass; He has veiled my paths with darkness.

verses.esv: He has stripped from me my glory and taken the crown from my head.
verses.kjv: He hath stripped me of my glory, and taken the crown from my head.
verses.bsb: He has stripped me of my honor and removed the crown from my head.

verses.esv: He breaks me down on every side, and I am gone, and my hope has he pulled up like a tree.
verses.kjv: He hath destroyed me on every side, and I am gone: and mine hope hath he removed like a tree.
verses.bsb: He tears me down on every side until I am gone; He uproots my hope like a tree.

verses.esv: He has kindled his wrath against me and counts me as his adversary.
verses.kjv: He hath also kindled his wrath against me, and he counteth me unto him as one of his enemies.
verses.bsb: His anger burns against me, and He counts me among His enemies.

verses.esv: His troops come on together; they have cast up their siege ramp against me and encamp around my tent.
verses.kjv: His troops come together, and raise up their way against me, and encamp round about my tabernacle.
verses.bsb: His troops advance together; they construct a ramp against me and encamp around my tent.

verses.esv: “He has put my brothers far from me, and those who knew me are wholly estranged from me.
verses.kjv: He hath put my brethren far from me, and mine acquaintance are verily estranged from me.
verses.bsb: He has removed my brothers from me; my acquaintances have abandoned me.

verses.esv: My relatives have failed me, my close friends have forgotten me.
verses.kjv: My kinsfolk have failed, and my familiar friends have forgotten me.
verses.bsb: My kinsmen have failed me, and my friends have forgotten me.

verses.esv: The guests in my house and my maidservants count me as a stranger; I have become a foreigner in their eyes.
verses.kjv: They that dwell in mine house, and my maids, count me for a stranger: I am an alien in their sight.
verses.bsb: My guests and maidservants count me as a stranger; I am a foreigner in their sight.

verses.esv: I call to my servant, but he gives me no answer; I must plead with him with my mouth for mercy.
verses.kjv: I called my servant, and he gave me no answer; I intreated him with my mouth.
verses.bsb: I call for my servant, but he does not answer, though I implore him with my own mouth.

verses.esv: My breath is strange to my wife, and I am a stench to the children of my own mother.
verses.kjv: My breath is strange to my wife, though I intreated for the children’s sake of mine own body.
verses.bsb: My breath is repulsive to my wife, and I am loathsome to my own family.

verses.esv: Even young children despise me; when I rise they talk against me.
verses.kjv: Yea, young children despised me; I arose, and they spake against me.
verses.bsb: Even little boys scorn me; when I appear, they deride me.

verses.esv: All my intimate friends abhor me, and those whom I loved have turned against me.
verses.kjv: All my inward friends abhorred me: and they whom I loved are turned against me.
verses.bsb: All my best friends despise me, and those I love have turned against me.

verses.esv: My bones stick to my skin and to my flesh, and I have escaped by the skin of my teeth.
verses.kjv: My bone cleaveth to my skin and to my flesh, and I am escaped with the skin of my teeth.
verses.bsb: My skin and flesh cling to my bones; I have escaped by the skin of my teeth.

verses.esv: Have mercy on me, have mercy on me, O you my friends, for the hand of God has touched me!
verses.kjv: Have pity upon me, have pity upon me, O ye my friends; for the hand of God hath touched me.
verses.bsb: Have pity on me, my friends, have pity, for the hand of God has struck me.

verses.esv: Why do you, like God, pursue me? Why are you not satisfied with my flesh?
verses.kjv: Why do ye persecute me as God, and are not satisfied with my flesh?
verses.bsb: Why do you persecute me as God does? Will you never get enough of my flesh?

verses.esv: “Oh that my words were written! Oh that they were inscribed in a book!
verses.kjv: Oh that my words were now written! oh that they were printed in a book!
verses.bsb: I wish that my words were recorded and inscribed in a book,

verses.esv: Oh that with an iron pen and lead they were engraved in the rock forever!
verses.kjv: That they were graven with an iron pen and lead in the rock for ever!
verses.bsb: by an iron stylus on lead, or chiseled in stone forever.

verses.esv: For I know that my Redeemer lives, and at the last he will stand upon the earth.
verses.kjv: For I know that my redeemer liveth, and that he shall stand at the latter day upon the earth:
verses.bsb: But I know that my Redeemer lives, and in the end He will stand upon the earth.

verses.esv: And after my skin has been thus destroyed, yet in my flesh I shall see God,
verses.kjv: And though after my skin worms destroy this body, yet in my flesh shall I see God:
verses.bsb: Even after my skin has been destroyed, yet in my flesh I will see God.

verses.esv: whom I shall see for myself, and my eyes shall behold, and not another. My heart faints within me!
verses.kjv: Whom I shall see for myself, and mine eyes shall behold, and not another; though my reins be consumed within me.
verses.bsb: I will see Him for myself; my eyes will behold Him, and not as a stranger. How my heart yearns within me!

verses.esv: If you say, ‘How we will pursue him!’ and, ‘The root of the matter is found in him,’
verses.kjv: But ye should say, Why persecute we him, seeing the root of the matter is found in me?
verses.bsb: If you say, ‘Let us persecute him, since the root of the matter lies with him,’

verses.esv: be afraid of the sword, for wrath brings the punishment of the sword, that you may know there is a judgment.”
verses.kjv: Be ye afraid of the sword: for wrath bringeth the punishments of the sword, that ye may know there is a judgment.
verses.bsb: then you should fear the sword yourselves, because wrath brings punishment by the sword, so that you may know there is a judgment.”

verses.esv: Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:
verses.kjv: THEN answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said,
verses.bsb: Then Zophar the Naamathite replied:

verses.esv: “Therefore my thoughts answer me, because of my haste within me.
verses.kjv: Therefore do my thoughts cause me to answer, and for this I make haste.
verses.bsb: “So my anxious thoughts compel me to answer, because of the turmoil within me.

verses.esv: I hear censure that insults me, and out of my understanding a spirit answers me.
verses.kjv: I have heard the check of my reproach, and the spirit of my understanding causeth me to answer.
verses.bsb: I have heard a rebuke that insults me, and my understanding prompts a reply.

verses.esv: Do you not know this from of old, since man was placed on earth,
verses.kjv: Knowest thou not this of old, since man was placed upon earth,
verses.bsb: Do you not know that from antiquity, since man was placed on the earth,

verses.esv: that the exulting of the wicked is short, and the joy of the godless but for a moment?
verses.kjv: That the triumphing of the wicked is short, and the joy of the hypocrite but for a moment?
verses.bsb: the triumph of the wicked has been brief and the joy of the godless momentary?

verses.esv: Though his height mount up to the heavens, and his head reach to the clouds,
verses.kjv: Though his excellency mount up to the heavens, and his head reach unto the clouds;
verses.bsb: Though his arrogance reaches the heavens, and his head touches the clouds,

verses.esv: he will perish forever like his own dung; those who have seen him will say, ‘Where is he?’
verses.kjv: Yet he shall perish for ever like his own dung: they which have seen him shall say, Where is he?
verses.bsb: he will perish forever, like his own dung; those who had seen him will ask, ‘Where is he?’

verses.esv: He will fly away like a dream and not be found; he will be chased away like a vision of the night.
verses.kjv: He shall fly away as a dream, and shall not be found: yea, he shall be chased away as a vision of the night.
verses.bsb: He will fly away like a dream, never to be found; he will be chased away like a vision in the night.

verses.esv: The eye that saw him will see him no more, nor will his place any more behold him.
verses.kjv: The eye also which saw him shall see him no more; neither shall his place any more behold him.
verses.bsb: The eye that saw him will see him no more, and his place will no longer behold him.

verses.esv: His children will seek the favor of the poor, and his hands will give back his wealth.
verses.kjv: His children shall seek to please the poor, and his hands shall restore their goods.
verses.bsb: His sons will seek the favor of the poor, for his own hands must return his wealth.

verses.esv: His bones are full of his youthful vigor, but it will lie down with him in the dust.
verses.kjv: His bones are full of the sin of his youth, which shall lie down with him in the dust.
verses.bsb: The youthful vigor that fills his bones will lie down with him in the dust.

verses.esv: “Though evil is sweet in his mouth, though he hides it under his tongue,
verses.kjv: Though wickedness be sweet in his mouth, though he hide it under his tongue;
verses.bsb: Though evil is sweet in his mouth and he conceals it under his tongue,

verses.esv: though he is loath to let it go and holds it in his mouth,
verses.kjv: Though he spare it, and forsake it not; but keep it still within his mouth:
verses.bsb: though he cannot bear to let it go and keeps it in his mouth,

verses.esv: yet his food is turned in his stomach; it is the venom of cobras within him.
verses.kjv: Yet his meat in his bowels is turned, it is the gall of asps within him.
verses.bsb: yet in his stomach his food sours into the venom of cobras within him.

verses.esv: He swallows down riches and vomits them up again; God casts them out of his belly.
verses.kjv: He hath swallowed down riches, and he shall vomit them up again: God shall cast them out of his belly.
verses.bsb: He swallows wealth but vomits it out; God will force it from his stomach.

verses.esv: He will suck the poison of cobras; the tongue of a viper will kill him.
verses.kjv: He shall suck the poison of asps: the viper’s tongue shall slay him.
verses.bsb: He will suck the poison of cobras; the fangs of a viper will kill him.

verses.esv: He will not look upon the rivers, the streams flowing with honey and curds.
verses.kjv: He shall not see the rivers, the floods, the brooks of honey and butter.
verses.bsb: He will not enjoy the streams, the rivers flowing with honey and cream.

verses.esv: He will give back the fruit of his toil and will not swallow it down; from the profit of his trading he will get no enjoyment.
verses.kjv: That which he laboured for shall he restore, and shall not swallow it down: according to his substance shall the restitution be, and he shall not rejoice therein.
verses.bsb: He must return the fruit of his labor without consuming it; he cannot enjoy the profits of his trading.

verses.esv: For he has crushed and abandoned the poor; he has seized a house that he did not build.
verses.kjv: Because he hath oppressed and hath forsaken the poor; because he hath violently taken away an house which he builded not;
verses.bsb: For he has oppressed and forsaken the poor; he has seized houses he did not build.

verses.esv: “Because he knew no contentment in his belly, he will not let anything in which he delights escape him.
verses.kjv: Surely he shall not feel quietness in his belly, he shall not save of that which he desired.
verses.bsb: Because his appetite is never satisfied, he cannot escape with his treasure.

verses.esv: There was nothing left after he had eaten; therefore his prosperity will not endure.
verses.kjv: There shall none of his meat be left; therefore shall no man look for his goods.
verses.bsb: Nothing is left for him to consume; thus his prosperity will not endure.

verses.esv: In the fullness of his sufficiency he will be in distress; the hand of everyone in misery will come against him.
verses.kjv: In the fulness of his sufficiency he shall be in straits: every hand of the wicked shall come upon him.
verses.bsb: In the midst of his plenty, he will be distressed; the full force of misery will come upon him.

verses.esv: To fill his belly to the full, God will send his burning anger against him and rain it upon him into his body.
verses.kjv: When he is about to fill his belly, God shall cast the fury of his wrath upon him, and shall rain it upon him while he is eating.
verses.bsb: When he has filled his stomach, God will vent His fury upon him, raining it down on him as he eats.

verses.esv: He will flee from an iron weapon; a bronze arrow will strike him through.
verses.kjv: He shall flee from the iron weapon, and the bow of steel shall strike him through.
verses.bsb: Though he flees from an iron weapon, a bronze-tipped arrow will pierce him.

verses.esv: It is drawn forth and comes out of his body; the glittering point comes out of his gallbladder; terrors come upon him.
verses.kjv: It is drawn, and cometh out of the body; yea, the glittering sword cometh out of his gall: terrors are upon him.
verses.bsb: It is drawn out of his back, the gleaming point from his liver. Terrors come over him.

verses.esv: Utter darkness is laid up for his treasures; a fire not fanned will devour him; what is left in his tent will be consumed.
verses.kjv: All darkness shall be hid in his secret places: a fire not blown shall consume him; it shall go ill with him that is left in his tabernacle.
verses.bsb: Total darkness is reserved for his treasures. A fire unfanned will consume him and devour what is left in his tent.

verses.esv: The heavens will reveal his iniquity, and the earth will rise up against him.
verses.kjv: The heaven shall reveal his iniquity; and the earth shall rise up against him.
verses.bsb: The heavens will expose his iniquity, and the earth will rise up against him.

verses.esv: The possessions of his house will be carried away, dragged off in the day of God's wrath.
verses.kjv: The increase of his house shall depart, and his goods shall flow away in the day of his wrath.
verses.bsb: The possessions of his house will be removed, flowing away on the day of God’s wrath.

verses.esv: This is the wicked man's portion from God, the heritage decreed for him by God.”
verses.kjv: This is the portion of a wicked man from God, and the heritage appointed unto him by God.
verses.bsb: This is the wicked man’s portion from God, the inheritance God has appointed him.”

verses.esv: Then Job answered and said:
verses.kjv: BUT Job answered and said,
verses.bsb: Then Job answered:

verses.esv: “Keep listening to my words, and let this be your comfort.
verses.kjv: Hear diligently my speech, and let this be your consolations.
verses.bsb: “Listen carefully to my words; let this be your consolation to me.

verses.esv: Bear with me, and I will speak, and after I have spoken, mock on.
verses.kjv: Suffer me that I may speak; and after that I have spoken, mock on.
verses.bsb: Bear with me while I speak; then, after I have spoken, you may go on mocking.

verses.esv: As for me, is my complaint against man? Why should I not be impatient?
verses.kjv: As for me, is my complaint to man? and if it were so, why should not my spirit be troubled?
verses.bsb: Is my complaint against a man? Then why should I not be impatient?

verses.esv: Look at me and be appalled, and lay your hand over your mouth.
verses.kjv: Mark me, and be astonished, and lay your hand upon your mouth.
verses.bsb: Look at me and be appalled; put your hand over your mouth.

verses.esv: When I remember, I am dismayed, and shuddering seizes my flesh.
verses.kjv: Even when I remember I am afraid, and trembling taketh hold on my flesh.
verses.bsb: When I remember, terror takes hold, and my body trembles in horror.

verses.esv: Why do the wicked live, reach old age, and grow mighty in power?
verses.kjv: Wherefore do the wicked live, become old, yea, are mighty in power?
verses.bsb: Why do the wicked live on, growing old and increasing in power?

verses.esv: Their offspring are established in their presence, and their descendants before their eyes.
verses.kjv: Their seed is established in their sight with them, and their offspring before their eyes.
verses.bsb: Their descendants are established around them, and their offspring before their eyes.

verses.esv: Their houses are safe from fear, and no rod of God is upon them.
verses.kjv: Their houses are safe from fear, neither is the rod of God upon them.
verses.bsb: Their homes are safe from fear; no rod of punishment from God is upon them.

verses.esv: Their bull breeds without fail; their cow calves and does not miscarry.
verses.kjv: Their bull gendereth, and faileth not; their cow calveth, and casteth not her calf.
verses.bsb: Their bulls breed without fail; their cows bear calves and do not miscarry.

verses.esv: They send out their little boys like a flock, and their children dance.
verses.kjv: They send forth their little ones like a flock, and their children dance.
verses.bsb: They send forth their little ones like a flock; their children skip about,

verses.esv: They sing to the tambourine and the lyre and rejoice to the sound of the pipe.
verses.kjv: They take the timbrel and harp, and rejoice at the sound of the organ.
verses.bsb: singing to the tambourine and lyre and making merry at the sound of the flute.

verses.esv: They spend their days in prosperity, and in peace they go down to Sheol.
verses.kjv: They spend their days in wealth, and in a moment go down to the grave.
verses.bsb: They spend their days in prosperity and go down to Sheol in peace.

verses.esv: They say to God, ‘Depart from us! We do not desire the knowledge of your ways.
verses.kjv: Therefore they say unto God, Depart from us; for we desire not the knowledge of thy ways.
verses.bsb: Yet they say to God: ‘Leave us alone! For we have no desire to know Your ways.

verses.esv: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? And what profit do we get if we pray to him?’
verses.kjv: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? and what profit should we have, if we pray unto him?
verses.bsb: Who is the Almighty, that we should serve Him, and what would we gain if we pray to Him?’

verses.esv: Behold, is not their prosperity in their hand? The counsel of the wicked is far from me.
verses.kjv: Lo, their good is not in their hand: the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
verses.bsb: Still, their prosperity is not in their own hands, so I stay far from the counsel of the wicked.

verses.esv: “How often is it that the lamp of the wicked is put out? That their calamity comes upon them? That God distributes pains in his anger?
verses.kjv: How oft is the candle of the wicked put out! and how oft cometh their destruction upon them! God distributeth sorrows in his anger.
verses.bsb: How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? Does disaster come upon them? Does God, in His anger, apportion destruction?

verses.esv: That they are like straw before the wind, and like chaff that the storm carries away?
verses.kjv: They are as stubble before the wind, and as chaff that the storm carrieth away.
verses.bsb: Are they like straw before the wind, like chaff swept away by a storm?

verses.esv: You say, ‘God stores up their iniquity for their children.’ Let him pay it out to them, that they may know it.
verses.kjv: God layeth up his iniquity for his children: he rewardeth him, and he shall know it.
verses.bsb: It is said that God lays up one’s punishment for his children. Let God repay the man himself, so he will know it.

verses.esv: Let their own eyes see their destruction, and let them drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
verses.kjv: His eyes shall see his destruction, and he shall drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
verses.bsb: Let his eyes see his own destruction; let him drink for himself the wrath of the Almighty.

verses.esv: For what do they care for their houses after them, when the number of their months is cut off?
verses.kjv: For what pleasure hath he in his house after him, when the number of his months is cut off in the midst?
verses.bsb: For what does he care about his household after him, when the number of his months has run out?

verses.esv: Will any teach God knowledge, seeing that he judges those who are on high?
verses.kjv: Shall any teach God knowledge? seeing he judgeth those that are high.
verses.bsb: Can anyone teach knowledge to God, since He judges those on high?

verses.esv: One dies in his full vigor, being wholly at ease and secure,
verses.kjv: One dieth in his full strength, being wholly at ease and quiet.
verses.bsb: One man dies full of vigor, completely secure and at ease.

verses.esv: his pails full of milk and the marrow of his bones moist.
verses.kjv: His breasts are full of milk, and his bones are moistened with marrow.
verses.bsb: His body is well nourished, and his bones are rich with marrow.

verses.esv: Another dies in bitterness of soul, never having tasted of prosperity.
verses.kjv: And another dieth in the bitterness of his soul, and never eateth with pleasure.
verses.bsb: Yet another man dies in the bitterness of his soul, having never tasted prosperity.

verses.esv: They lie down alike in the dust, and the worms cover them.
verses.kjv: They shall lie down alike in the dust, and the worms shall cover them.
verses.bsb: But together they lie down in the dust, and worms cover them both.

verses.esv: “Behold, I know your thoughts and your schemes to wrong me.
verses.kjv: Behold, I know your thoughts, and the devices which ye wrongfully imagine against me.
verses.bsb: Behold, I know your thoughts full well, the schemes by which you would wrong me.

verses.esv: For you say, ‘Where is the house of the prince? Where is the tent in which the wicked lived?’
verses.kjv: For ye say, Where is the house of the prince? and where are the dwelling places of the wicked?
verses.bsb: For you say, ‘Where now is the nobleman’s house, and where are the tents in which the wicked dwell?’

verses.esv: Have you not asked those who travel the roads, and do you not accept their testimony
verses.kjv: Have ye not asked them that go by the way? and do ye not know their tokens,
verses.bsb: Have you never asked those who travel the roads? Do you not accept their reports?

verses.esv: that the evil man is spared in the day of calamity, that he is rescued in the day of wrath?
verses.kjv: That the wicked is reserved to the day of destruction? they shall be brought forth to the day of wrath.
verses.bsb: Indeed, the evil man is spared from the day of calamity, delivered from the day of wrath.

verses.esv: Who declares his way to his face, and who repays him for what he has done?
verses.kjv: Who shall declare his way to his face? and who shall repay him what he hath done?
verses.bsb: Who denounces his behavior to his face? Who repays him for what he has done?

verses.esv: When he is carried to the grave, watch is kept over his tomb.
verses.kjv: Yet shall he be brought to the grave, and shall remain in the tomb.
verses.bsb: He is carried to the grave, and watch is kept over his tomb.

verses.esv: The clods of the valley are sweet to him; all mankind follows after him, and those who go before him are innumerable.
verses.kjv: The clods of the valley shall be sweet unto him, and every man shall draw after him, as there are innumerable before him.
verses.bsb: The clods of the valley are sweet to him; everyone follows behind him, and those before him are without number.

verses.esv: How then will you comfort me with empty nothings? There is nothing left of your answers but falsehood.”
verses.kjv: How then comfort ye me in vain, seeing in your answers there remaineth falsehood?
verses.bsb: So how can you comfort me with empty words? For your answers remain full of falsehood.”

verses.esv: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
verses.kjv: THEN Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,
verses.bsb: Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied:

verses.esv: “Can a man be profitable to God? Surely he who is wise is profitable to himself.
verses.kjv: Can a man be profitable unto God, as he that is wise may be profitable unto himself?
verses.bsb: “Can a man be of use to God? Can even a wise man benefit Him?

verses.esv: Is it any pleasure to the Almighty if you are in the right, or is it gain to him if you make your ways blameless?
verses.kjv: Is it any pleasure to the Almighty, that thou art righteous? or is it gain to him, that thou makest thy ways perfect?
verses.bsb: Does it delight the Almighty that you are righteous? Does He profit if your ways are blameless?

verses.esv: Is it for your fear of him that he reproves you and enters into judgment with you?
verses.kjv: Will he reprove thee for fear of thee? will he enter with thee into judgment?
verses.bsb: Is it for your reverence that He rebukes you and enters into judgment against you?

verses.esv: Is not your evil abundant? There is no end to your iniquities.
verses.kjv: Is not thy wickedness great? and thine iniquities infinite?
verses.bsb: Is not your wickedness great? Are not your iniquities endless?

verses.esv: For you have exacted pledges of your brothers for nothing and stripped the naked of their clothing.
verses.kjv: For thou hast taken a pledge from thy brother for nought, and stripped the naked of their clothing.
verses.bsb: For you needlessly demanded security from your brothers and deprived the naked of their clothing.

verses.esv: You have given no water to the weary to drink, and you have withheld bread from the hungry.
verses.kjv: Thou hast not given water to the weary to drink, and thou hast withholden bread from the hungry.
verses.bsb: You gave no water to the weary and withheld food from the famished,

verses.esv: The man with power possessed the land, and the favored man lived in it.
verses.kjv: But as for the mighty man, he had the earth; and the honourable man dwelt in it.
verses.bsb: while the land belonged to a mighty man, and a man of honor lived on it.

verses.esv: You have sent widows away empty, and the arms of the fatherless were crushed.
verses.kjv: Thou hast sent widows away empty, and the arms of the fatherless have been broken.
verses.bsb: You sent widows away empty-handed, and the strength of the fatherless was crushed.

verses.esv: Therefore snares are all around you, and sudden terror overwhelms you,
verses.kjv: Therefore snares are round about thee, and sudden fear troubleth thee;
verses.bsb: Therefore snares surround you, and sudden peril terrifies you;

verses.esv: or darkness, so that you cannot see, and a flood of water covers you.
verses.kjv: Or darkness, that thou canst not see; and abundance of waters cover thee.
verses.bsb: it is so dark you cannot see, and a flood of water covers you.

verses.esv: “Is not God high in the heavens? See the highest stars, how lofty they are!
verses.kjv: Is not God in the height of heaven? and behold the height of the stars, how high they are!
verses.bsb: Is not God as high as the heavens? Look at the highest stars, how lofty they are!

verses.esv: But you say, ‘What does God know? Can he judge through the deep darkness?
verses.kjv: And thou sayest, How doth God know? can he judge through the dark cloud?
verses.bsb: Yet you say: ‘What does God know? Does He judge through thick darkness?

verses.esv: Thick clouds veil him, so that he does not see, and he walks on the vault of heaven.’
verses.kjv: Thick clouds are a covering to him, that he seeth not; and he walketh in the circuit of heaven.
verses.bsb: Thick clouds veil Him so He does not see us as He traverses the vault of heaven.’

verses.esv: Will you keep to the old way that wicked men have trod?
verses.kjv: Hast thou marked the old way which wicked men have trodden?
verses.bsb: Will you stay on the ancient path that wicked men have trod?

verses.esv: They were snatched away before their time; their foundation was washed away.
verses.kjv: Which were cut down out of time, whose foundation was overflown with a flood:
verses.bsb: They were snatched away before their time, and their foundations were swept away by a flood.

verses.esv: They said to God, ‘Depart from us,’ and ‘What can the Almighty do to us?’
verses.kjv: Which said unto God, Depart from us: and what can the Almighty do for them?
verses.bsb: They said to God, ‘Depart from us. What can the Almighty do to us?’

verses.esv: Yet he filled their houses with good things— but the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
verses.kjv: Yet he filled their houses with good things: but the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
verses.bsb: But it was He who filled their houses with good things; so I stay far from the counsel of the wicked.

verses.esv: The righteous see it and are glad; the innocent one mocks at them,
verses.kjv: The righteous see it, and are glad: and the innocent laugh them to scorn.
verses.bsb: The righteous see it and are glad; the innocent mock them:

verses.esv: saying, ‘Surely our adversaries are cut off, and what they left the fire has consumed.’
verses.kjv: Whereas our substance is not cut down, but the remnant of them the fire consumeth.
verses.bsb: ‘Surely our foes are destroyed, and fire has consumed their excess.’

verses.esv: “Agree with God, and be at peace; thereby good will come to you.
verses.kjv: Acquaint now thyself with him, and be at peace: thereby good shall come unto thee.
verses.bsb: Reconcile now and be at peace with Him; thereby good will come to you.

verses.esv: Receive instruction from his mouth, and lay up his words in your heart.
verses.kjv: Receive, I pray thee, the law from his mouth, and lay up his words in thine heart.
verses.bsb: Receive instruction from His mouth, and lay up His words in your heart.

verses.esv: If you return to the Almighty you will be built up; if you remove injustice far from your tents,
verses.kjv: If thou return to the Almighty, thou shalt be built up, thou shalt put away iniquity far from thy tabernacles.
verses.bsb: If you return to the Almighty, you will be restored. If you remove injustice from your tents

verses.esv: if you lay gold in the dust, and gold of Ophir among the stones of the torrent-bed,
verses.kjv: Then shalt thou lay up gold as dust, and the gold of Ophir as the stones of the brooks.
verses.bsb: and consign your gold to the dust and the gold of Ophir to the stones of the ravines,

verses.esv: then the Almighty will be your gold and your precious silver.
verses.kjv: Yea, the Almighty shall be thy defence, and thou shalt have plenty of silver.
verses.bsb: then the Almighty will be your gold and the finest silver for you.

verses.esv: For then you will delight yourself in the Almighty and lift up your face to God.
verses.kjv: For then shalt thou have thy delight in the Almighty, and shalt lift up thy face unto God.
verses.bsb: Surely then you will delight in the Almighty and lift up your face to God.

verses.esv: You will make your prayer to him, and he will hear you, and you will pay your vows.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt make thy prayer unto him, and he shall hear thee, and thou shalt pay thy vows.
