verses.bsb: You will pray to Him, and He will hear you, and you will fulfill your vows.

verses.esv: You will decide on a matter, and it will be established for you, and light will shine on your ways.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt also decree a thing, and it shall be established unto thee: and the light shall shine upon thy ways.
verses.bsb: Your decisions will be carried out, and light will shine on your ways.

verses.esv: For when they are humbled you say, ‘It is because of pride’; but he saves the lowly.
verses.kjv: When men are cast down, then thou shalt say, There is lifting up; and he shall save the humble person.
verses.bsb: When men are brought low and you say, ‘Lift them up!’ then He will save the lowly.

verses.esv: He delivers even the one who is not innocent, who will be delivered through the cleanness of your hands.”
verses.kjv: He shall deliver the island of the innocent: and it is delivered by the pureness of thine hands.
verses.bsb: He will deliver even one who is not innocent, rescuing him through the cleanness of your hands.”

verses.esv: Then Job answered and said:
verses.kjv: THEN Job answered and said,
verses.bsb: Then Job answered:

verses.esv: “Today also my complaint is bitter; my hand is heavy on account of my groaning.
verses.kjv: Even to day is my complaint bitter: my stroke is heavier than my groaning.
verses.bsb: “Even today my complaint is bitter. His hand is heavy despite my groaning.

verses.esv: Oh, that I knew where I might find him, that I might come even to his seat!
verses.kjv: Oh that I knew where I might find him! that I might come even to his seat!
verses.bsb: If only I knew where to find Him, so that I could go to His seat.

verses.esv: I would lay my case before him and fill my mouth with arguments.
verses.kjv: I would order my cause before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.
verses.bsb: I would plead my case before Him and fill my mouth with arguments.

verses.esv: I would know what he would answer me and understand what he would say to me.
verses.kjv: I would know the words which he would answer me, and understand what he would say unto me.
verses.bsb: I would learn how He would answer, and consider what He would say.

verses.esv: Would he contend with me in the greatness of his power? No; he would pay attention to me.
verses.kjv: Will he plead against me with his great power? No; but he would put strength in me.
verses.bsb: Would He contend with me in His great power? No, He would certainly take note of me.

verses.esv: There an upright man could argue with him, and I would be acquitted forever by my judge.
verses.kjv: There the righteous might dispute with him; so should I be delivered for ever from my judge.
verses.bsb: Then an upright man could reason with Him, and I would be delivered forever from my Judge.

verses.esv: “Behold, I go forward, but he is not there, and backward, but I do not perceive him;
verses.kjv: Behold, I go forward, but he is not there; and backward, but I cannot perceive him:
verses.bsb: If I go east, He is not there, and if I go west, I cannot find Him.

verses.esv: on the left hand when he is working, I do not behold him; he turns to the right hand, but I do not see him.
verses.kjv: On the left hand, where he doth work, but I cannot behold him: he hideth himself on the right hand, that I cannot see him:
verses.bsb: When He is at work in the north, I cannot behold Him; when He turns to the south, I cannot see Him.

verses.esv: But he knows the way that I take; when he has tried me, I shall come out as gold.
verses.kjv: But he knoweth the way that I take: when he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold.
verses.bsb: Yet He knows the way I have taken; when He has tested me, I will come forth as gold.

verses.esv: My foot has held fast to his steps; I have kept his way and have not turned aside.
verses.kjv: My foot hath held his steps, his way have I kept, and not declined.
verses.bsb: My feet have followed in His tracks; I have kept His way without turning aside.

verses.esv: I have not departed from the commandment of his lips; I have treasured the words of his mouth more than my portion of food.
verses.kjv: Neither have I gone back from the commandment of his lips; I have esteemed the words of his mouth more than my necessary food.
verses.bsb: I have not departed from the command of His lips; I have treasured the words of His mouth more than my daily bread.

verses.esv: But he is unchangeable, and who can turn him back? What he desires, that he does.
verses.kjv: But he is in one mind, and who can turn him? and what his soul desireth, even that he doeth.
verses.bsb: But He is unchangeable, and who can oppose Him? He does what He desires.

verses.esv: For he will complete what he appoints for me, and many such things are in his mind.
verses.kjv: For he performeth the thing that is appointed for me: and many such things are with him.
verses.bsb: For He carries out His decree against me, and He has many such plans.

verses.esv: Therefore I am terrified at his presence; when I consider, I am in dread of him.
verses.kjv: Therefore am I troubled at his presence: when I consider, I am afraid of him.
verses.bsb: Therefore I am terrified in His presence; when I consider this, I fear Him.

verses.esv: God has made my heart faint; the Almighty has terrified me;
verses.kjv: For God maketh my heart soft, and the Almighty troubleth me:
verses.bsb: God has made my heart faint; the Almighty has terrified me.

verses.esv: yet I am not silenced because of the darkness, nor because thick darkness covers my face. “Why are not times of judgment kept by the Almighty, and why do those who know him never see his days?
verses.kjv: Because I was not cut off before the darkness, neither hath he covered the darkness from my face.
verses.bsb: Yet I am not silenced by the darkness, by the thick darkness that covers my face.

verses.esv: Some move landmarks; they seize flocks and pasture them.
verses.kjv: Some remove the landmarks; they violently take away flocks, and feed thereof.
verses.bsb: Men move boundary stones; they pasture stolen flocks.

verses.esv: They drive away the donkey of the fatherless; they take the widow's ox for a pledge.
verses.kjv: They drive away the ass of the fatherless, they take the widow’s ox for a pledge.
verses.bsb: They drive away the donkey of the fatherless and take the widow’s ox in pledge.

verses.esv: They thrust the poor off the road; the poor of the earth all hide themselves.
verses.kjv: They turn the needy out of the way: the poor of the earth hide themselves together.
verses.bsb: They push the needy off the road and force all the poor of the land into hiding.

verses.esv: Behold, like wild donkeys in the desert the poor go out to their toil, seeking game; the wasteland yields food for their children.
verses.kjv: Behold, as wild asses in the desert, go they forth to their work; rising betimes for a prey: the wilderness yieldeth food for them and for their children.
verses.bsb: Indeed, like wild donkeys in the desert, the poor go to work foraging for food; the wasteland is food for their children.

verses.esv: They gather their fodder in the field, and they glean the vineyard of the wicked man.
verses.kjv: They reap every one his corn in the field: and they gather the vintage of the wicked.
verses.bsb: They gather fodder in the fields and glean the vineyards of the wicked.

verses.esv: They lie all night naked, without clothing, and have no covering in the cold.
verses.kjv: They cause the naked to lodge without clothing, that they have no covering in the cold.
verses.bsb: Without clothing, they spend the night naked; they have no covering against the cold.

verses.esv: They are wet with the rain of the mountains and cling to the rock for lack of shelter.
verses.kjv: They are wet with the showers of the mountains, and embrace the rock for want of a shelter.
verses.bsb: Drenched by mountain rains, they huddle against the rocks for want of shelter.

verses.esv: (There are those who snatch the fatherless child from the breast, and they take a pledge against the poor.)
verses.kjv: They pluck the fatherless from the breast, and take a pledge of the poor.
verses.bsb: The fatherless infant is snatched from the breast; the nursing child of the poor is seized for a debt.

verses.esv: They go about naked, without clothing; hungry, they carry the sheaves;
verses.kjv: They cause him to go naked without clothing, and they take away the sheaf from the hungry;
verses.bsb: Without clothing, they wander about naked. They carry the sheaves, but still go hungry.

verses.esv: among the olive rows of the wicked they make oil; they tread the winepresses, but suffer thirst.
verses.kjv: Which make oil within their walls, and tread their winepresses, and suffer thirst.
verses.bsb: They crush olives within their walls; they tread the winepresses, but go thirsty.

verses.esv: From out of the city the dying groan, and the soul of the wounded cries for help; yet God charges no one with wrong.
verses.kjv: Men groan from out of the city, and the soul of the wounded crieth out: yet God layeth not folly to them.
verses.bsb: From the city, men groan, and the souls of the wounded cry out, yet God charges no one with wrongdoing.

verses.esv: “There are those who rebel against the light, who are not acquainted with its ways, and do not stay in its paths.
verses.kjv: They are of those that rebel against the light; they know not the ways thereof, nor abide in the paths thereof.
verses.bsb: Then there are those who rebel against the light, not knowing its ways or staying on its paths.

verses.esv: The murderer rises before it is light, that he may kill the poor and needy, and in the night he is like a thief.
verses.kjv: The murderer rising with the light killeth the poor and needy, and in the night is as a thief.
verses.bsb: When daylight is gone, the murderer rises to kill the poor and needy; in the night he is like a thief.

verses.esv: The eye of the adulterer also waits for the twilight, saying, ‘No eye will see me’; and he veils his face.
verses.kjv: The eye also of the adulterer waiteth for the twilight, saying, No eye shall see me: and disguiseth his face.
verses.bsb: The eye of the adulterer watches for twilight. Thinking, ‘No eye will see me,’ he covers his face.

verses.esv: In the dark they dig through houses; by day they shut themselves up; they do not know the light.
verses.kjv: In the dark they dig through houses, which they had marked for themselves in the daytime: they know not the light.
verses.bsb: In the dark they dig through houses; by day they shut themselves in, never to experience the light.

verses.esv: For deep darkness is morning to all of them; for they are friends with the terrors of deep darkness.
verses.kjv: For the morning is to them even as the shadow of death: if one know them, they are in the terrors of the shadow of death.
verses.bsb: For to them, deep darkness is their morning; surely they are friends with the terrors of darkness!

verses.esv: “You say, ‘Swift are they on the face of the waters; their portion is cursed in the land; no treader turns toward their vineyards.
verses.kjv: He is swift as the waters; their portion is cursed in the earth: he beholdeth not the way of the vineyards.
verses.bsb: They are but foam on the surface of the water; their portion of the land is cursed, so that no one turns toward their vineyards.

verses.esv: Drought and heat snatch away the snow waters; so does Sheol those who have sinned.
verses.kjv: Drought and heat consume the snow waters: so doth the grave those which have sinned.
verses.bsb: As drought and heat consume the melting snow, so Sheol steals those who have sinned.

verses.esv: The womb forgets them; the worm finds them sweet; they are no longer remembered, so wickedness is broken like a tree.’
verses.kjv: The womb shall forget him; the worm shall feed sweetly on him; he shall be no more remembered; and wickedness shall be broken as a tree.
verses.bsb: The womb forgets them; the worm feeds on them; they are remembered no more. So injustice is like a broken tree.

verses.esv: “They wrong the barren, childless woman, and do no good to the widow.
verses.kjv: He evil entreateth the barren that beareth not: and doeth not good to the widow.
verses.bsb: They prey on the barren and childless, and show no kindness to the widow.

verses.esv: Yet God prolongs the life of the mighty by his power; they rise up when they despair of life.
verses.kjv: He draweth also the mighty with his power: he riseth up, and no man is sure of life.
verses.bsb: Yet by His power, God drags away the mighty; though rising up, they have no assurance of life.

verses.esv: He gives them security, and they are supported, and his eyes are upon their ways.
verses.kjv: Though it be given him to be in safety, whereon he resteth; yet his eyes are upon their ways.
verses.bsb: He gives them a sense of security, but His eyes are on their ways.

verses.esv: They are exalted a little while, and then are gone; they are brought low and gathered up like all others; they are cut off like the heads of grain.
verses.kjv: They are exalted for a little while, but are gone and brought low; they are taken out of the way as all other, and cut off as the tops of the ears of corn.
verses.bsb: They are exalted for a moment, then they are gone; they are brought low and gathered up like all others; they are cut off like heads of grain.

verses.esv: If it is not so, who will prove me a liar and show that there is nothing in what I say?”
verses.kjv: And if it be not so now, who will make me a liar, and make my speech nothing worth?
verses.bsb: If this is not so, then who can prove me a liar and reduce my words to nothing?”

verses.esv: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
verses.kjv: THEN answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,
verses.bsb: Then Bildad the Shuhite replied:

verses.esv: “Dominion and fear are with God; he makes peace in his high heaven.
verses.kjv: Dominion and fear are with him, he maketh peace in his high places.
verses.bsb: “Dominion and awe belong to God; He establishes harmony in the heights of heaven.

verses.esv: Is there any number to his armies? Upon whom does his light not arise?
verses.kjv: Is there any number of his armies? and upon whom doth not his light arise?
verses.bsb: Can His troops be numbered? On whom does His light not rise?

verses.esv: How then can man be in the right before God? How can he who is born of woman be pure?
verses.kjv: How then can man be justified with God? or how can he be clean that is born of a woman?
verses.bsb: How then can a man be just before God? How can one born of woman be pure?

verses.esv: Behold, even the moon is not bright, and the stars are not pure in his eyes;
verses.kjv: Behold even to the moon, and it shineth not; yea, the stars are not pure in his sight.
verses.bsb: If even the moon does not shine, and the stars are not pure in His sight,

verses.esv: how much less man, who is a maggot, and the son of man, who is a worm!”
verses.kjv: How much less man, that is a worm? and the son of man, which is a worm?
verses.bsb: how much less man, who is but a maggot, and the son of man, who is but a worm!”

verses.esv: Then Job answered and said:
verses.kjv: BUT Job answered and said,
verses.bsb: Then Job answered:

verses.esv: “How you have helped him who has no power! How you have saved the arm that has no strength!
verses.kjv: How hast thou helped him that is without power? how savest thou the arm that hath no strength?
verses.bsb: “How you have helped the powerless and saved the arm that is feeble!

verses.esv: How you have counseled him who has no wisdom, and plentifully declared sound knowledge!
verses.kjv: How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisdom? and how hast thou plentifully declared the thing as it is?
verses.bsb: How you have counseled the unwise and provided fully sound insight!

verses.esv: With whose help have you uttered words, and whose breath has come out from you?
verses.kjv: To whom hast thou uttered words? and whose spirit came from thee?
verses.bsb: To whom have you uttered these words? And whose spirit spoke through you?

verses.esv: The dead tremble under the waters and their inhabitants.
verses.kjv: Dead things are formed from under the waters, and the inhabitants thereof.
verses.bsb: The dead tremble— those beneath the waters and those who dwell in them.

verses.esv: Sheol is naked before God, and Abaddon has no covering.
verses.kjv: Hell is naked before him, and destruction hath no covering.
verses.bsb: Sheol is naked before God, and Abaddon has no covering.

verses.esv: He stretches out the north over the void and hangs the earth on nothing.
verses.kjv: He stretcheth out the north over the empty place, and hangeth the earth upon nothing.
verses.bsb: He stretches out the north over empty space; He hangs the earth upon nothing.

verses.esv: He binds up the waters in his thick clouds, and the cloud is not split open under them.
verses.kjv: He bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds; and the cloud is not rent under them.
verses.bsb: He wraps up the waters in His clouds, yet the clouds do not burst under their own weight.

verses.esv: He covers the face of the full moon and spreads over it his cloud.
verses.kjv: He holdeth back the face of his throne, and spreadeth his cloud upon it.
verses.bsb: He covers the face of the full moon, spreading over it His cloud.

verses.esv: He has inscribed a circle on the face of the waters at the boundary between light and darkness.
verses.kjv: He hath compassed the waters with bounds, until the day and night come to an end.
verses.bsb: He has inscribed a horizon on the face of the waters at the boundary between light and darkness.

verses.esv: The pillars of heaven tremble and are astounded at his rebuke.
verses.kjv: The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at his reproof.
verses.bsb: The foundations of heaven quake, astounded at His rebuke.

verses.esv: By his power he stilled the sea; by his understanding he shattered Rahab.
verses.kjv: He divideth the sea with his power, and by his understanding he smiteth through the proud.
verses.bsb: By His power He stirred the sea; by His understanding He shattered Rahab.

verses.esv: By his wind the heavens were made fair; his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
verses.kjv: By his spirit he hath garnished the heavens; his hand hath formed the crooked serpent.
verses.bsb: By His breath the skies were cleared; His hand pierced the fleeing serpent.

verses.esv: Behold, these are but the outskirts of his ways, and how small a whisper do we hear of him! But the thunder of his power who can understand?”
verses.kjv: Lo, these are parts of his ways: but how little a portion is heard of him? but the thunder of his power who can understand?
verses.bsb: Indeed, these are but the fringes of His ways; how faint is the whisper we hear of Him! Who then can understand the thunder of His power?”

verses.esv: And Job again took up his discourse, and said:
verses.kjv: MOREOVER Job continued his parable, and said,
verses.bsb: Job continued his discourse:

verses.esv: “As God lives, who has taken away my right, and the Almighty, who has made my soul bitter,
verses.kjv: As God liveth, who hath taken away my judgment; and the Almighty, who hath vexed my soul;
verses.bsb: “As surely as God lives, who has deprived me of justice— the Almighty, who has embittered my soul—

verses.esv: as long as my breath is in me, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils,
verses.kjv: All the while my breath is in me, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils;
verses.bsb: as long as my breath is still within me and the breath of God remains in my nostrils,

verses.esv: my lips will not speak falsehood, and my tongue will not utter deceit.
verses.kjv: My lips shall not speak wickedness, nor my tongue utter deceit.
verses.bsb: my lips will not speak wickedness, and my tongue will not utter deceit.

verses.esv: Far be it from me to say that you are right; till I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
verses.kjv: God forbid that I should justify you: till I die I will not remove mine integrity from me.
verses.bsb: I will never say that you are right; I will maintain my integrity until I die.

verses.esv: I hold fast my righteousness and will not let it go; my heart does not reproach me for any of my days.
verses.kjv: My righteousness I hold fast, and will not let it go: my heart shall not reproach me so long as I live.
verses.bsb: I will cling to my righteousness and never let go. As long as I live, my conscience will not accuse me.

verses.esv: “Let my enemy be as the wicked, and let him who rises up against me be as the unrighteous.
verses.kjv: Let mine enemy be as the wicked, and he that riseth up against me as the unrighteous.
verses.bsb: May my enemy be like the wicked and my opponent like the unjust.

verses.esv: For what is the hope of the godless when God cuts him off, when God takes away his life?
verses.kjv: For what is the hope of the hypocrite, though he hath gained, when God taketh away his soul?
verses.bsb: For what is the hope of the godless when he is cut off, when God takes away his life?

verses.esv: Will God hear his cry when distress comes upon him?
verses.kjv: Will God hear his cry when trouble cometh upon him?
verses.bsb: Will God hear his cry when distress comes upon him?

verses.esv: Will he take delight in the Almighty? Will he call upon God at all times?
verses.kjv: Will he delight himself in the Almighty? will he always call upon God?
verses.bsb: Will he delight in the Almighty? Will he call upon God at all times?

verses.esv: I will teach you concerning the hand of God; what is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
verses.kjv: I will teach you by the hand of God: that which is with the Almighty will I not conceal.
verses.bsb: I will instruct you in the power of God. I will not conceal the ways of the Almighty.

verses.esv: Behold, all of you have seen it yourselves; why then have you become altogether vain?
verses.kjv: Behold, all ye yourselves have seen it; why then are ye thus altogether vain?
verses.bsb: Surely all of you have seen it for yourselves. Why then do you keep up this empty talk?

verses.esv: “This is the portion of a wicked man with God, and the heritage that oppressors receive from the Almighty:
verses.kjv: This is the portion of a wicked man with God, and the heritage of oppressors, which they shall receive of the Almighty.
verses.bsb: This is the wicked man’s portion from God— the heritage the ruthless receive from the Almighty.

verses.esv: If his children are multiplied, it is for the sword, and his descendants have not enough bread.
verses.kjv: If his children be multiplied, it is for the sword: and his offspring shall not be satisfied with bread.
verses.bsb: Though his sons are many, they are destined for the sword; and his offspring will never have enough food.

verses.esv: Those who survive him the pestilence buries, and his widows do not weep.
verses.kjv: Those that remain of him shall be buried in death: and his widows shall not weep.
verses.bsb: His survivors will be buried by the plague, and their widows will not weep for them.

verses.esv: Though he heap up silver like dust, and pile up clothing like clay,
verses.kjv: Though he heap up silver as the dust, and prepare raiment as the clay;
verses.bsb: Though he heaps up silver like dust and piles up a wardrobe like clay,

verses.esv: he may pile it up, but the righteous will wear it, and the innocent will divide the silver.
verses.kjv: He may prepare it, but the just shall put it on, and the innocent shall divide the silver.
verses.bsb: what he lays up, the righteous will wear, and his silver will be divided by the innocent.

verses.esv: He builds his house like a moth's, like a booth that a watchman makes.
verses.kjv: He buildeth his house as a moth, and as a booth that the keeper maketh.
verses.bsb: The house he built is like a moth’s cocoon, like a hut set up by a watchman.

verses.esv: He goes to bed rich, but will do so no more; he opens his eyes, and his wealth is gone.
verses.kjv: The rich man shall lie down, but he shall not be gathered: he openeth his eyes, and he is not.
verses.bsb: He lies down wealthy, but will do so no more; when he opens his eyes, all is gone.

verses.esv: Terrors overtake him like a flood; in the night a whirlwind carries him off.
verses.kjv: Terrors take hold on him as waters, a tempest stealeth him away in the night.
verses.bsb: Terrors overtake him like a flood; a tempest sweeps him away in the night.

verses.esv: The east wind lifts him up and he is gone; it sweeps him out of his place.
verses.kjv: The east wind carrieth him away, and he departeth: and as a storm hurleth him out of his place.
verses.bsb: The east wind carries him away, and he is gone; it sweeps him out of his place.

verses.esv: It hurls at him without pity; he flees from its power in headlong flight.
verses.kjv: For God shall cast upon him, and not spare: he would fain flee out of his hand.
verses.bsb: It hurls itself against him without mercy as he flees headlong from its power.

verses.esv: It claps its hands at him and hisses at him from its place.
verses.kjv: Men shall clap their hands at him, and shall hiss him out of his place.
verses.bsb: It claps its hands at him and hisses him out of his place.

verses.esv: “Surely there is a mine for silver, and a place for gold that they refine.
verses.kjv: SURELY there is a vein for the silver, and a place for gold where they fine it.
verses.bsb: “Surely there is a mine for silver and a place where gold is refined.

verses.esv: Iron is taken out of the earth, and copper is smelted from the ore.
verses.kjv: Iron is taken out of the earth, and brass is molten out of the stone.
verses.bsb: Iron is taken from the earth, and copper is smelted from ore.

verses.esv: Man puts an end to darkness and searches out to the farthest limit the ore in gloom and deep darkness.
verses.kjv: He setteth an end to darkness, and searcheth out all perfection: the stones of darkness, and the shadow of death.
verses.bsb: Man puts an end to the darkness; he probes the farthest recesses for ore in deepest darkness.

verses.esv: He opens shafts in a valley away from where anyone lives; they are forgotten by travelers; they hang in the air, far away from mankind; they swing to and fro.
verses.kjv: The flood breaketh out from the inhabitant; even the waters forgotten of the foot: they are dried up, they are gone away from men.
verses.bsb: Far from human habitation he cuts a shaft in places forgotten by the foot of man. Far from men he dangles and sways.

verses.esv: As for the earth, out of it comes bread, but underneath it is turned up as by fire.
verses.kjv: As for the earth, out of it cometh bread: and under it is turned up as it were fire.
verses.bsb: Food may come from the earth, but from below it is transformed as by fire.

verses.esv: Its stones are the place of sapphires, and it has dust of gold.
verses.kjv: The stones of it are the place of sapphires: and it hath dust of gold.
verses.bsb: Its rocks are the source of sapphires, containing flecks of gold.

verses.esv: “That path no bird of prey knows, and the falcon's eye has not seen it.
verses.kjv: There is a path which no fowl knoweth, and which the vulture’s eye hath not seen:
verses.bsb: No bird of prey knows that path; no falcon’s eye has seen it.

verses.esv: The proud beasts have not trodden it; the lion has not passed over it.
verses.kjv: The lion’s whelps have not trodden it, nor the fierce lion passed by it.
verses.bsb: Proud beasts have never trodden it; no lion has ever prowled over it.

verses.esv: “Man puts his hand to the flinty rock and overturns mountains by the roots.
verses.kjv: He putteth forth his hand upon the rock; he overturneth the mountains by the roots.
verses.bsb: The miner strikes the flint; he overturns mountains at their base.

verses.esv: He cuts out channels in the rocks, and his eye sees every precious thing.
verses.kjv: He cutteth out rivers among the rocks; and his eye seeth every precious thing.
verses.bsb: He hews out channels in the rocks, and his eyes spot every treasure.

verses.esv: He dams up the streams so that they do not trickle, and the thing that is hidden he brings out to light.
verses.kjv: He bindeth the floods from overflowing; and the thing that is hid bringeth he forth to light.
verses.bsb: He stops up the sources of the streams to bring what is hidden to light.

verses.esv: “But where shall wisdom be found? And where is the place of understanding?
verses.kjv: But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?
verses.bsb: But where can wisdom be found, and where does understanding dwell?

verses.esv: Man does not know its worth, and it is not found in the land of the living.
verses.kjv: Man knoweth not the price thereof; neither is it found in the land of the living.
verses.bsb: No man can know its value, nor is it found in the land of the living.

verses.esv: The deep says, ‘It is not in me,’ and the sea says, ‘It is not with me.’
verses.kjv: The depth saith, It is not in me: and the sea saith, It is not with me.
verses.bsb: The ocean depths say, ‘It is not in me,’ while the sea declares, ‘It is not with me.’

verses.esv: It cannot be bought for gold, and silver cannot be weighed as its price.
verses.kjv: It cannot be gotten for gold, neither shall silver be weighed for the price thereof.
verses.bsb: It cannot be bought with gold, nor can its price be weighed out in silver.

verses.esv: It cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir, in precious onyx or sapphire.
verses.kjv: It cannot be valued with the gold of Ophir, with the precious onyx, or the sapphire.
verses.bsb: It cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir, in precious onyx or sapphire.

verses.esv: Gold and glass cannot equal it, nor can it be exchanged for jewels of fine gold.
verses.kjv: The gold and the crystal cannot equal it: and the exchange of it shall not be for jewels of fine gold.
verses.bsb: Neither gold nor crystal can compare to it, nor jewels of fine gold be exchanged for it.

verses.esv: No mention shall be made of coral or of crystal; the price of wisdom is above pearls.
verses.kjv: No mention shall be made of coral, or of pearls: for the price of wisdom is above rubies.
verses.bsb: Coral and quartz are unworthy of mention; the price of wisdom is beyond rubies.

verses.esv: The topaz of Ethiopia cannot equal it, nor can it be valued in pure gold.
verses.kjv: The topaz of Ethiopia shall not equal it, neither shall it be valued with pure gold.
verses.bsb: Topaz from Cush cannot compare to it, nor can it be valued in pure gold.

verses.esv: “From where, then, does wisdom come? And where is the place of understanding?
verses.kjv: Whence then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?
verses.bsb: From where then does wisdom come, and where does understanding dwell?

verses.esv: It is hidden from the eyes of all living and concealed from the birds of the air.
verses.kjv: Seeing it is hid from the eyes of all living, and kept close from the fowls of the air.
verses.bsb: It is hidden from the eyes of every living thing and concealed from the birds of the air.

verses.esv: Abaddon and Death say, ‘We have heard a rumor of it with our ears.’
verses.kjv: Destruction and death say, We have heard the fame thereof with our ears.
verses.bsb: Abaddon and Death say, ‘We have heard a rumor about it.’

verses.esv: “God understands the way to it, and he knows its place.
verses.kjv: God understandeth the way thereof, and he knoweth the place thereof.
verses.bsb: But God understands its way, and He knows its place.

verses.esv: For he looks to the ends of the earth and sees everything under the heavens.
verses.kjv: For he looketh to the ends of the earth, and seeth under the whole heaven;
verses.bsb: For He looks to the ends of the earth and sees everything under the heavens.

verses.esv: When he gave to the wind its weight and apportioned the waters by measure,
verses.kjv: To make the weight for the winds; and he weigheth the waters by measure.
verses.bsb: When God fixed the weight of the wind and measured out the waters,

verses.esv: when he made a decree for the rain and a way for the lightning of the thunder,
verses.kjv: When he made a decree for the rain, and a way for the lightning of the thunder:
verses.bsb: when He set a limit for the rain and a path for the thunderbolt,

verses.esv: then he saw it and declared it; he established it, and searched it out.
verses.kjv: Then did he see it, and declare it; he prepared it, yea, and searched it out.
verses.bsb: then He looked at wisdom and appraised it; He established it and searched it out.

verses.esv: And he said to man, ‘Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom, and to turn away from evil is understanding.’”
verses.kjv: And unto man he said, Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding.
verses.bsb: And He said to man, ‘Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom, and to turn away from evil is understanding.’”

verses.esv: And Job again took up his discourse, and said:
verses.kjv: MOREOVER Job continued his parable, and said,
verses.bsb: And Job continued his discourse:

verses.esv: “Oh, that I were as in the months of old, as in the days when God watched over me,
verses.kjv: Oh that I were as in months past, as in the days when God preserved me;
verses.bsb: “How I long for the months gone by, for the days when God watched over me,

verses.esv: when his lamp shone upon my head, and by his light I walked through darkness,
verses.kjv: When his candle shined upon my head, and when by his light I walked through darkness;
verses.bsb: when His lamp shone above my head, and by His light I walked through the darkness,

verses.esv: as I was in my prime, when the friendship of God was upon my tent,
verses.kjv: As I was in the days of my youth, when the secret of God was upon my tabernacle;
verses.bsb: when I was in my prime, when the friendship of God rested on my tent,

verses.esv: when the Almighty was yet with me, when my children were all around me,
verses.kjv: When the Almighty was yet with me, when my children were about me;
verses.bsb: when the Almighty was still with me and my children were around me,

verses.esv: when my steps were washed with butter, and the rock poured out for me streams of oil!
verses.kjv: When I washed my steps with butter, and the rock poured me out rivers of oil;
verses.bsb: when my steps were bathed in cream and the rock poured out for me streams of oil!

verses.esv: When I went out to the gate of the city, when I prepared my seat in the square,
verses.kjv: When I went out to the gate through the city, when I prepared my seat in the street!
verses.bsb: When I went out to the city gate and took my seat in the public square,

verses.esv: the young men saw me and withdrew, and the aged rose and stood;
verses.kjv: The young men saw me, and hid themselves: and the aged arose, and stood up.
verses.bsb: the young men saw me and withdrew, and the old men rose to their feet.

verses.esv: the princes refrained from talking and laid their hand on their mouth;
verses.kjv: The princes refrained talking, and laid their hand on their mouth.
verses.bsb: The princes refrained from speaking and covered their mouths with their hands.

verses.esv: the voice of the nobles was hushed, and their tongue stuck to the roof of their mouth.
verses.kjv: The nobles held their peace, and their tongue cleaved to the roof of their mouth.
verses.bsb: The voices of the nobles were hushed, and their tongues stuck to the roofs of their mouths.

verses.esv: When the ear heard, it called me blessed, and when the eye saw, it approved,
verses.kjv: When the ear heard me, then it blessed me; and when the eye saw me, it gave witness to me:
verses.bsb: For those who heard me called me blessed, and those who saw me commended me,

verses.esv: because I delivered the poor who cried for help, and the fatherless who had none to help him.
verses.kjv: Because I delivered the poor that cried, and the fatherless, and him that had none to help him.
verses.bsb: because I rescued the poor who cried out and the fatherless who had no helper.

verses.esv: The blessing of him who was about to perish came upon me, and I caused the widow's heart to sing for joy.
verses.kjv: The blessing of him that was ready to perish came upon me: and I caused the widow’s heart to sing for joy.
verses.bsb: The dying man blessed me, and I made the widow’s heart sing for joy.

verses.esv: I put on righteousness, and it clothed me; my justice was like a robe and a turban.
verses.kjv: I put on righteousness, and it clothed me: my judgment was as a robe and a diadem.
verses.bsb: I put on righteousness, and it clothed me; justice was my robe and my turban.

verses.esv: I was eyes to the blind and feet to the lame.
verses.kjv: I was eyes to the blind, and feet was I to the lame.
verses.bsb: I served as eyes to the blind and as feet to the lame.

verses.esv: I was a father to the needy, and I searched out the cause of him whom I did not know.
verses.kjv: I was a father to the poor: and the cause which I knew not I searched out.
verses.bsb: I was a father to the needy, and I took up the case of the stranger.

verses.esv: I broke the fangs of the unrighteous and made him drop his prey from his teeth.
verses.kjv: And I brake the jaws of the wicked, and plucked the spoil out of his teeth.
verses.bsb: I shattered the fangs of the unjust and snatched the prey from his teeth.

verses.esv: Then I thought, ‘I shall die in my nest, and I shall multiply my days as the sand,
verses.kjv: Then I said, I shall die in my nest, and I shall multiply my days as the sand.
verses.bsb: So I thought: ‘I will die in my nest and multiply my days as the sand.

verses.esv: my roots spread out to the waters, with the dew all night on my branches,
verses.kjv: My root was spread out by the waters, and the dew lay all night upon my branch.
verses.bsb: My roots will spread out to the waters, and the dew will rest nightly on my branches.

verses.esv: my glory fresh with me, and my bow ever new in my hand.’
verses.kjv: My glory was fresh in me, and my bow was renewed in my hand.
verses.bsb: My glory is ever new within me, and my bow is renewed in my hand.’

verses.esv: “Men listened to me and waited and kept silence for my counsel.
verses.kjv: Unto me men gave ear, and waited, and kept silence at my counsel.
verses.bsb: Men listened to me with expectation, waiting silently for my counsel.

verses.esv: After I spoke they did not speak again, and my word dropped upon them.
verses.kjv: After my words they spake not again; and my speech dropped upon them.
verses.bsb: After my words, they spoke no more; my speech settled on them like dew.

verses.esv: They waited for me as for the rain, and they opened their mouths as for the spring rain.
verses.kjv: And they waited for me as for the rain; and they opened their mouth wide as for the latter rain.
verses.bsb: They waited for me as for rain and drank in my words like spring showers.

verses.esv: I smiled on them when they had no confidence, and the light of my face they did not cast down.
verses.kjv: If I laughed on them, they believed it not; and the light of my countenance they cast not down.
verses.bsb: If I smiled at them, they did not believe it; the light of my countenance was precious.

verses.esv: I chose their way and sat as chief, and I lived like a king among his troops, like one who comforts mourners. “But now they laugh at me, men who are younger than I, whose fathers I would have disdained to set with the dogs of my flock.
verses.kjv: I chose out their way, and sat chief, and dwelt as a king in the army, as one that comforteth the mourners.
verses.bsb: I chose their course and presided as chief. So I dwelt as a king among his troops, as a comforter of the mourners.

verses.esv: What could I gain from the strength of their hands, men whose vigor is gone?
verses.kjv: Yea, whereto might the strength of their hands profit me, in whom old age was perished?
verses.bsb: What use to me was the strength of their hands, since their vigor had left them?

verses.esv: Through want and hard hunger they gnaw the dry ground by night in waste and desolation;
verses.kjv: For want and famine they were solitary; fleeing into the wilderness in former time desolate and waste.
verses.bsb: Gaunt from poverty and hunger, they gnawed the dry land, and the desolate wasteland by night.

verses.esv: they pick saltwort and the leaves of bushes, and the roots of the broom tree for their food.
verses.kjv: Who cut up mallows by the bushes, and juniper roots for their meat.
verses.bsb: They plucked mallow among the shrubs, and the roots of the broom tree were their food.

verses.esv: They are driven out from human company; they shout after them as after a thief.
verses.kjv: They were driven forth from among men, (they cried after them as after a thief;)
verses.bsb: They were banished from among men, shouted down like thieves,

verses.esv: In the gullies of the torrents they must dwell, in holes of the earth and of the rocks.
verses.kjv: To dwell in the clifts of the valleys, in caves of the earth, and in the rocks.
verses.bsb: so that they lived on the slopes of the wadis, among the rocks and in holes in the ground.

verses.esv: Among the bushes they bray; under the nettles they huddle together.
verses.kjv: Among the bushes they brayed; under the nettles they were gathered together.
verses.bsb: They cried out among the shrubs and huddled beneath the nettles.

verses.esv: A senseless, a nameless brood, they have been whipped out of the land.
verses.kjv: They were children of fools, yea, children of base men: they were viler than the earth.
verses.bsb: A senseless and nameless brood, they were driven off the land.

verses.esv: “And now I have become their song; I am a byword to them.
verses.kjv: And now am I their song, yea, I am their byword.
verses.bsb: And now they mock me in song; I have become a byword among them.

verses.esv: They abhor me; they keep aloof from me; they do not hesitate to spit at the sight of me.
verses.kjv: They abhor me, they flee far from me, and spare not to spit in my face.
verses.bsb: They abhor me and keep far from me; they do not hesitate to spit in my face.

verses.esv: Because God has loosed my cord and humbled me, they have cast off restraint in my presence.
verses.kjv: Because he hath loosed my cord, and afflicted me, they have also let loose the bridle before me.
verses.bsb: Because God has unstrung my bow and afflicted me, they have cast off restraint in my presence.

verses.esv: On my right hand the rabble rise; they push away my feet; they cast up against me their ways of destruction.
verses.kjv: Upon my right hand rise the youth; they push away my feet, and they raise up against me the ways of their destruction.
verses.bsb: The rabble arises at my right; they lay snares for my feet and build siege ramps against me.

verses.esv: They break up my path; they promote my calamity; they need no one to help them.
verses.kjv: They mar my path, they set forward my calamity, they have no helper.
verses.bsb: They tear up my path; they profit from my destruction, with no one to restrain them.

verses.esv: As through a wide breach they come; amid the crash they roll on.
verses.kjv: They came upon me as a wide breaking in of waters: in the desolation they rolled themselves upon me.
verses.bsb: They advance as through a wide breach; through the ruins they keep rolling in.

verses.esv: Terrors are turned upon me; my honor is pursued as by the wind, and my prosperity has passed away like a cloud.
verses.kjv: Terrors are turned upon me: they pursue my soul as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloud.
verses.bsb: Terrors are turned loose against me; they drive away my dignity as by the wind, and my prosperity has passed like a cloud.

verses.esv: “And now my soul is poured out within me; days of affliction have taken hold of me.
verses.kjv: And now my soul is poured out upon me; the days of affliction have taken hold upon me.
verses.bsb: And now my soul is poured out within me; days of affliction grip me.

verses.esv: The night racks my bones, and the pain that gnaws me takes no rest.
verses.kjv: My bones are pierced in me in the night season: and my sinews take no rest.
verses.bsb: Night pierces my bones, and my gnawing pains never rest.

verses.esv: With great force my garment is disfigured; it binds me about like the collar of my tunic.
verses.kjv: By the great force of my disease is my garment changed: it bindeth me about as the collar of my coat.
verses.bsb: With great force He grasps my garment; He seizes me by the collar of my tunic.

verses.esv: God has cast me into the mire, and I have become like dust and ashes.
verses.kjv: He hath cast me into the mire, and I am become like dust and ashes.
verses.bsb: He throws me into the mud, and I have become like dust and ashes.

verses.esv: I cry to you for help and you do not answer me; I stand, and you only look at me.
verses.kjv: I cry unto thee, and thou dost not hear me: I stand up, and thou regardest me not.
verses.bsb: I cry out to You for help, but You do not answer; when I stand up, You merely look at me.

verses.esv: You have turned cruel to me; with the might of your hand you persecute me.
verses.kjv: Thou art become cruel to me: with thy strong hand thou opposest thyself against me.
verses.bsb: You have ruthlessly turned on me; You oppose me with Your strong hand.

verses.esv: You lift me up on the wind; you make me ride on it, and you toss me about in the roar of the storm.
verses.kjv: Thou liftest me up to the wind; thou causest me to ride upon it, and dissolvest my substance.
verses.bsb: You snatch me up into the wind and drive me before it; You toss me about in the storm.

verses.esv: For I know that you will bring me to death and to the house appointed for all living.
verses.kjv: For I know that thou wilt bring me to death, and to the house appointed for all living.
verses.bsb: Yes, I know that You will bring me down to death, to the place appointed for all the living.

verses.esv: “Yet does not one in a heap of ruins stretch out his hand, and in his disaster cry for help?
verses.kjv: Howbeit he will not stretch out his hand to the grave, though they cry in his destruction.
verses.bsb: Yet no one stretches out his hand to a ruined man when he cries for help in his distress.

verses.esv: Did not I weep for him whose day was hard? Was not my soul grieved for the needy?
verses.kjv: Did not I weep for him that was in trouble? was not my soul grieved for the poor?
verses.bsb: Have I not wept for those in trouble? Has my soul not grieved for the needy?

verses.esv: But when I hoped for good, evil came, and when I waited for light, darkness came.
verses.kjv: When I looked for good, then evil came unto me: and when I waited for light, there came darkness.
verses.bsb: But when I hoped for good, evil came; when I looked for light, darkness fell.

verses.esv: My inward parts are in turmoil and never still; days of affliction come to meet me.
verses.kjv: My bowels boiled, and rested not: the days of affliction prevented me.
verses.bsb: I am churning within and cannot rest; days of affliction confront me.

verses.esv: I go about darkened, but not by the sun; I stand up in the assembly and cry for help.
verses.kjv: I went mourning without the sun: I stood up, and I cried in the congregation.
verses.bsb: I go about blackened, but not by the sun. I stand up in the assembly and cry for help.

verses.esv: I am a brother of jackals and a companion of ostriches.
verses.kjv: I am a brother to dragons, and a companion to owls.
verses.bsb: I have become a brother of jackals, a companion of ostriches.

verses.esv: My skin turns black and falls from me, and my bones burn with heat.
verses.kjv: My skin is black upon me, and my bones are burned with heat.
verses.bsb: My skin grows black and peels, and my bones burn with fever.

verses.esv: My lyre is turned to mourning, and my pipe to the voice of those who weep.
verses.kjv: My harp also is turned to mourning, and my organ into the voice of them that weep.
verses.bsb: My harp is tuned to mourning and my flute to the sound of weeping.

verses.esv: “I have made a covenant with my eyes; how then could I gaze at a virgin?
verses.kjv: I MADE a covenant with mine eyes; why then should I think upon a maid?
verses.bsb: “I have made a covenant with my eyes. How then could I gaze with desire at a virgin?

verses.esv: What would be my portion from God above and my heritage from the Almighty on high?
verses.kjv: For what portion of God is there from above? and what inheritance of the Almighty from on high?
verses.bsb: For what is the allotment of God from above, or the heritage from the Almighty on high?

verses.esv: Is not calamity for the unrighteous, and disaster for the workers of iniquity?
verses.kjv: Is not destruction to the wicked? and a strange punishment to the workers of iniquity?
verses.bsb: Does not disaster come to the unjust and calamity to the workers of iniquity?

verses.esv: Does not he see my ways and number all my steps?
verses.kjv: Doth not he see my ways, and count all my steps?
verses.bsb: Does He not see my ways and count my every step?

verses.esv: “If I have walked with falsehood and my foot has hastened to deceit;
verses.kjv: If I have walked with vanity, or if my foot hath hasted to deceit;
verses.bsb: If I have walked in falsehood or my foot has rushed to deceit,

verses.esv: (Let me be weighed in a just balance, and let God know my integrity!)
verses.kjv: Let me be weighed in an even balance, that God may know mine integrity.
verses.bsb: let God weigh me with honest scales, that He may know my integrity.

verses.esv: if my step has turned aside from the way and my heart has gone after my eyes, and if any spot has stuck to my hands,
verses.kjv: If my step hath turned out of the way, and mine heart walked after mine eyes, and if any blot hath cleaved to mine hands;
verses.bsb: If my steps have turned from the path, if my heart has followed my eyes, or if impurity has stuck to my hands,

verses.esv: then let me sow, and another eat, and let what grows for me be rooted out.
verses.kjv: Then let me sow, and let another eat; yea, let my offspring be rooted out.
verses.bsb: then may another eat what I have sown, and may my crops be uprooted.

verses.esv: “If my heart has been enticed toward a woman, and I have lain in wait at my neighbor's door,
verses.kjv: If mine heart have been deceived by a woman, or if I have laid wait at my neighbour’s door;
verses.bsb: If my heart has been enticed by my neighbor’s wife, or I have lurked at his door,

verses.esv: then let my wife grind for another, and let others bow down on her.
verses.kjv: Then let my wife grind unto another, and let others bow down upon her.
verses.bsb: then may my own wife grind grain for another, and may other men sleep with her.

verses.esv: For that would be a heinous crime; that would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges;
verses.kjv: For this is an heinous crime; yea, it is an iniquity to be punished by the judges.
verses.bsb: For that would be a heinous crime, an iniquity to be judged.

verses.esv: for that would be a fire that consumes as far as Abaddon, and it would burn to the root all my increase.
verses.kjv: For it is a fire that consumeth to destruction, and would root out all mine increase.
verses.bsb: For it is a fire that burns down to Abaddon; it would root out my entire harvest.

verses.esv: “If I have rejected the cause of my manservant or my maidservant, when they brought a complaint against me,
verses.kjv: If I did despise the cause of my manservant or of my maidservant, when they contended with me;
verses.bsb: If I have rejected the cause of my manservant or maidservant when they made a complaint against me,

verses.esv: what then shall I do when God rises up? When he makes inquiry, what shall I answer him?
verses.kjv: What then shall I do when God riseth up? and when he visiteth, what shall I answer him?
verses.bsb: what will I do when God rises to judge? How will I answer when called to account?

verses.esv: Did not he who made me in the womb make him? And did not one fashion us in the womb?
verses.kjv: Did not he that made me in the womb make him? and did not one fashion us in the womb?
verses.bsb: Did not He who made me in the womb also make them? Did not the same One form us in the womb?

verses.esv: “If I have withheld anything that the poor desired, or have caused the eyes of the widow to fail,
verses.kjv: If I have withheld the poor from their desire, or have caused the eyes of the widow to fail;
verses.bsb: If I have denied the desires of the poor or allowed the widow’s eyes to fail,

verses.esv: or have eaten my morsel alone, and the fatherless has not eaten of it
verses.kjv: Or have eaten my morsel myself alone, and the fatherless hath not eaten thereof;
verses.bsb: if I have eaten my morsel alone, not sharing it with the fatherless—

verses.esv: (for from my youth the fatherless grew up with me as with a father, and from my mother's womb I guided the widow),
verses.kjv: (For from my youth he was brought up with me, as with a father, and I have guided her from my mother’s womb;)
verses.bsb: though from my youth I reared him as would a father, and from my mother’s womb I guided the widow—

verses.esv: if I have seen anyone perish for lack of clothing, or the needy without covering,
verses.kjv: If I have seen any perish for want of clothing, or any poor without covering;
verses.bsb: if I have seen one perish for lack of clothing, or a needy man without a cloak,

verses.esv: if his body has not blessed me, and if he was not warmed with the fleece of my sheep,
verses.kjv: If his loins have not blessed me, and if he were not warmed with the fleece of my sheep;
verses.bsb: if his heart has not blessed me for warming him with the fleece of my sheep,

verses.esv: if I have raised my hand against the fatherless, because I saw my help in the gate,
verses.kjv: If I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless, when I saw my help in the gate:
verses.bsb: if I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless because I saw that I had support in the gate,

verses.esv: then let my shoulder blade fall from my shoulder, and let my arm be broken from its socket.
verses.kjv: Then let mine arm fall from my shoulder blade, and mine arm be broken from the bone.
verses.bsb: then may my arm fall from my shoulder and be torn from its socket.

verses.esv: For I was in terror of calamity from God, and I could not have faced his majesty.
verses.kjv: For destruction from God was a terror to me, and by reason of his highness I could not endure.
verses.bsb: For calamity from God terrifies me, and His splendor I cannot overpower.

verses.esv: “If I have made gold my trust or called fine gold my confidence,
verses.kjv: If I have made gold my hope, or have said to the fine gold, Thou art my confidence;
verses.bsb: If I have put my trust in gold or called pure gold my security,

verses.esv: if I have rejoiced because my wealth was abundant or because my hand had found much,
verses.kjv: If I rejoiced because my wealth was great, and because mine hand had gotten much;
verses.bsb: if I have rejoiced in my great wealth because my hand had gained so much,

verses.esv: if I have looked at the sun when it shone, or the moon moving in splendor,
verses.kjv: If I beheld the sun when it shined, or the moon walking in brightness;
verses.bsb: if I have beheld the sun in its radiance or the moon moving in splendor,

verses.esv: and my heart has been secretly enticed, and my mouth has kissed my hand,
verses.kjv: And my heart hath been secretly enticed, or my mouth hath kissed my hand:
verses.bsb: so that my heart was secretly enticed and my hand threw a kiss from my mouth,

verses.esv: this also would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges, for I would have been false to God above.
verses.kjv: This also were an iniquity to be punished by the judge: for I should have denied the God that is above.
verses.bsb: this would also be an iniquity to be judged, for I would have denied God on high.

verses.esv: “If I have rejoiced at the ruin of him who hated me, or exulted when evil overtook him
verses.kjv: If I rejoiced at the destruction of him that hated me, or lifted up myself when evil found him:
verses.bsb: If I have rejoiced in my enemy’s ruin, or exulted when evil befell him—

verses.esv: (I have not let my mouth sin by asking for his life with a curse),
verses.kjv: Neither have I suffered my mouth to sin by wishing a curse to his soul.
verses.bsb: I have not allowed my mouth to sin by asking for his life with a curse—

verses.esv: if the men of my tent have not said, ‘Who is there that has not been filled with his meat?’
verses.kjv: If the men of my tabernacle said not, Oh that we had of his flesh! we cannot be satisfied.
verses.bsb: if the men of my house have not said, ‘Who is there who has not had his fill?’—

verses.esv: (the sojourner has not lodged in the street; I have opened my doors to the traveler),
verses.kjv: The stranger did not lodge in the street: but I opened my doors to the traveller.
verses.bsb: but no stranger had to lodge on the street, for my door has been open to the traveler—

verses.esv: if I have concealed my transgressions as others do by hiding my iniquity in my heart,
verses.kjv: If I covered my transgressions as Adam, by hiding mine iniquity in my bosom:
verses.bsb: if I have covered my transgressions like Adam by hiding my guilt in my heart,

verses.esv: because I stood in great fear of the multitude, and the contempt of families terrified me, so that I kept silence, and did not go out of doors—
verses.kjv: Did I fear a great multitude, or did the contempt of families terrify me, that I kept silence, and went not out of the door?
verses.bsb: because I greatly feared the crowds and the contempt of the clans terrified me, so that I kept silent and would not go outside—

verses.esv: Oh, that I had one to hear me! (Here is my signature! Let the Almighty answer me!) Oh, that I had the indictment written by my adversary!
verses.kjv: Oh that one would hear me! behold, my desire is, that the Almighty would answer me, and that mine adversary had written a book.
verses.bsb: (Oh, that I had one to hear me! Here is my signature. Let the Almighty answer me; let my accuser compose an indictment.

verses.esv: Surely I would carry it on my shoulder; I would bind it on me as a crown;
verses.kjv: Surely I would take it upon my shoulder, and bind it as a crown to me.
verses.bsb: Surely I would carry it on my shoulder and wear it like a crown.

verses.esv: I would give him an account of all my steps; like a prince I would approach him.
verses.kjv: I would declare unto him the number of my steps; as a prince would I go near unto him.
verses.bsb: I would give account of all my steps; I would approach Him like a prince.)—

verses.esv: “If my land has cried out against me and its furrows have wept together,
verses.kjv: If my land cry against me, or that the furrows likewise thereof complain;
verses.bsb: if my land cries out against me and its furrows weep together,

verses.esv: if I have eaten its yield without payment and made its owners breathe their last,
verses.kjv: If I have eaten the fruits thereof without money, or have caused the owners thereof to lose their life:
verses.bsb: if I have devoured its produce without payment or broken the spirit of its tenants,

verses.esv: let thorns grow instead of wheat, and foul weeds instead of barley.” The words of Job are ended.
verses.kjv: Let thistles grow instead of wheat, and cockle instead of barley. The words of Job are ended.
verses.bsb: then let briers grow instead of wheat and stinkweed instead of barley.”   Thus conclude the words of Job.

verses.esv: So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
verses.kjv: SO these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
verses.bsb: So these three men stopped answering Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.

verses.esv: Then Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram, burned with anger. He burned with anger at Job because he justified himself rather than God.
verses.kjv: Then was kindled the wrath of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the kindred of Ram: against Job was his wrath kindled, because he justified himself rather than God.
verses.bsb: This kindled the anger of Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram. He burned with anger against Job for justifying himself rather than God,

verses.esv: He burned with anger also at Job's three friends because they had found no answer, although they had declared Job to be in the wrong.
verses.kjv: Also against his three friends was his wrath kindled, because they had found no answer, and yet had condemned Job.
verses.bsb: and he burned with anger against Job’s three friends because they had failed to refute Job, and yet had condemned him.

verses.esv: Now Elihu had waited to speak to Job because they were older than he.
verses.kjv: Now Elihu had waited till Job had spoken, because they were elder than he.
verses.bsb: Now Elihu had waited to speak to Job because the others were older than he.

verses.esv: And when Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of these three men, he burned with anger.
verses.kjv: When Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of these three men, then his wrath was kindled.
verses.bsb: But when he saw that the three men had no further reply, his anger was kindled.

verses.esv: And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said: “I am young in years, and you are aged; therefore I was timid and afraid to declare my opinion to you.
verses.kjv: And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said, I am young, and ye are very old; wherefore I was afraid, and durst not shew you mine opinion.
verses.bsb: So Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite declared:  “I am young in years, while you are old; that is why I was timid and afraid to tell you what I know.

verses.esv: I said, ‘Let days speak, and many years teach wisdom.’
verses.kjv: I said, Days should speak, and multitude of years should teach wisdom.
verses.bsb: I thought that age should speak, and many years should teach wisdom.

verses.esv: But it is the spirit in man, the breath of the Almighty, that makes him understand.
verses.kjv: But there is a spirit in man: and the inspiration of the Almighty giveth them understanding.
verses.bsb: But there is a spirit in a man, the breath of the Almighty, that gives him understanding.

verses.esv: It is not the old who are wise, nor the aged who understand what is right.
verses.kjv: Great men are not always wise: neither do the aged understand judgment.
verses.bsb: It is not only the old who are wise, or the elderly who understand justice.

verses.esv: Therefore I say, ‘Listen to me; let me also declare my opinion.’
verses.kjv: Therefore I said, Hearken to me; I also will shew mine opinion.
verses.bsb: Therefore I say, ‘Listen to me; I too will declare what I know.’

verses.esv: “Behold, I waited for your words, I listened for your wise sayings, while you searched out what to say.
verses.kjv: Behold, I waited for your words; I gave ear to your reasons, whilst ye searched out what to say.
verses.bsb: Indeed, I waited while you spoke; I listened to your reasoning; as you searched for words,

verses.esv: I gave you my attention, and, behold, there was none among you who refuted Job or who answered his words.
verses.kjv: Yea, I attended unto you, and, behold, there was none of you that convinced Job, or that answered his words:
verses.bsb: I paid you full attention. But no one proved Job wrong; not one of you rebutted his arguments.

verses.esv: Beware lest you say, ‘We have found wisdom; God may vanquish him, not a man.’
verses.kjv: Lest ye should say, We have found out wisdom: God thrusteth him down, not man.
verses.bsb: So do not claim, ‘We have found wisdom; let God, not man, refute him.’

verses.esv: He has not directed his words against me, and I will not answer him with your speeches.
verses.kjv: Now he hath not directed his words against me: neither will I answer him with your speeches.
verses.bsb: But Job has not directed his words against me, and I will not answer him with your arguments.

verses.esv: “They are dismayed; they answer no more; they have not a word to say.
verses.kjv: They were amazed, they answered no more: they left off speaking.
verses.bsb: Job’s friends are dismayed, with no more to say; words have escaped them.

verses.esv: And shall I wait, because they do not speak, because they stand there, and answer no more?
verses.kjv: When I had waited, (for they spake not, but stood still, and answered no more;)
verses.bsb: Must I wait, now that they are silent, now that they stand and no longer reply?

verses.esv: I also will answer with my share; I also will declare my opinion.
verses.kjv: I said, I will answer also my part, I also will shew mine opinion.
verses.bsb: I too will answer; yes, I will declare what I know.

verses.esv: For I am full of words; the spirit within me constrains me.
verses.kjv: For I am full of matter, the spirit within me constraineth me.
verses.bsb: For I am full of words, and my spirit within me compels me.

verses.esv: Behold, my belly is like wine that has no vent; like new wineskins ready to burst.
verses.kjv: Behold, my belly is as wine which hath no vent; it is ready to burst like new bottles.
verses.bsb: Behold, my belly is like unvented wine; it is about to burst like a new wineskin.

verses.esv: I must speak, that I may find relief; I must open my lips and answer.
verses.kjv: I will speak, that I may be refreshed: I will open my lips and answer.
verses.bsb: I must speak and find relief; I must open my lips and respond.

verses.esv: I will not show partiality to any man or use flattery toward any person.
verses.kjv: Let me not, I pray you, accept any man’s person, neither let me give flattering titles unto man.
verses.bsb: I will be partial to no one, nor will I flatter any man.

verses.esv: For I do not know how to flatter, else my Maker would soon take me away.
verses.kjv: For I know not to give flattering titles; in so doing my maker would soon take me away.
verses.bsb: For I do not know how to flatter, or my Maker would remove me in an instant.

verses.esv: “But now, hear my speech, O Job, and listen to all my words.
verses.kjv: WHEREFORE, Job, I pray thee, hear my speeches, and hearken to all my words.
verses.bsb: “But now, O Job, hear my speech, and listen to all my words.

verses.esv: Behold, I open my mouth; the tongue in my mouth speaks.
verses.kjv: Behold, now I have opened my mouth, my tongue hath spoken in my mouth.
verses.bsb: Behold, I will open my mouth; my address is on the tip of my tongue.

verses.esv: My words declare the uprightness of my heart, and what my lips know they speak sincerely.
verses.kjv: My words shall be of the uprightness of my heart: and my lips shall utter knowledge clearly.
verses.bsb: My words are from an upright heart, and my lips speak sincerely what I know.

verses.esv: The Spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
verses.kjv: The Spirit of God hath made me, and the breath of the Almighty hath given me life.
verses.bsb: The Spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.

verses.esv: Answer me, if you can; set your words in order before me; take your stand.
verses.kjv: If thou canst answer me, set thy words in order before me, stand up.
verses.bsb: Refute me if you can; prepare your case and confront me.

verses.esv: Behold, I am toward God as you are; I too was pinched off from a piece of clay.
verses.kjv: Behold, I am according to thy wish in God’s stead: I also am formed out of the clay.
verses.bsb: I am just like you before God; I was also formed from clay.

verses.esv: Behold, no fear of me need terrify you; my pressure will not be heavy upon you.
verses.kjv: Behold, my terror shall not make thee afraid, neither shall my hand be heavy upon thee.
verses.bsb: Surely no fear of me should terrify you; nor will my hand be heavy upon you.

verses.esv: “Surely you have spoken in my ears, and I have heard the sound of your words.
verses.kjv: Surely thou hast spoken in mine hearing, and I have heard the voice of thy words, saying,
verses.bsb: Surely you have spoken in my hearing, and I have heard these very words:

verses.esv: You say, ‘I am pure, without transgression; I am clean, and there is no iniquity in me.
verses.kjv: I am clean without transgression, I am innocent; neither is there iniquity in me.
verses.bsb: ‘I am pure, without transgression; I am clean, with no iniquity in me.

verses.esv: Behold, he finds occasions against me, he counts me as his enemy,
verses.kjv: Behold, he findeth occasions against me, he counteth me for his enemy,
verses.bsb: Yet God finds occasions against me; He counts me as His enemy.

verses.esv: he puts my feet in the stocks and watches all my paths.’
verses.kjv: He putteth my feet in the stocks, he marketh all my paths.
verses.bsb: He puts my feet in the stocks; He watches over all my paths.’

verses.esv: “Behold, in this you are not right. I will answer you, for God is greater than man.
verses.kjv: Behold, in this thou art not just: I will answer thee, that God is greater than man.
verses.bsb: Behold, you are not right in this matter. I will answer you, for God is greater than man.

verses.esv: Why do you contend against him, saying, ‘He will answer none of man's words’?
verses.kjv: Why dost thou strive against him? for he giveth not account of any of his matters.
verses.bsb: Why do you complain to Him that He answers nothing a man asks?

verses.esv: For God speaks in one way, and in two, though man does not perceive it.
verses.kjv: For God speaketh once, yea twice, yet man perceiveth it not.
verses.bsb: For God speaks in one way and in another, yet no one notices.

verses.esv: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falls on men, while they slumber on their beds,
verses.kjv: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falleth upon men, in slumberings upon the bed;
verses.bsb: In a dream, in a vision in the night, when deep sleep falls upon men as they slumber on their beds,

verses.esv: then he opens the ears of men and terrifies them with warnings,
verses.kjv: Then he openeth the ears of men, and sealeth their instruction,
verses.bsb: He opens their ears and terrifies them with warnings

verses.esv: that he may turn man aside from his deed and conceal pride from a man;
verses.kjv: That he may withdraw man from his purpose, and hide pride from man.
verses.bsb: to turn a man from wrongdoing and keep him from pride,

verses.esv: he keeps back his soul from the pit, his life from perishing by the sword.
verses.kjv: He keepeth back his soul from the pit, and his life from perishing by the sword.
verses.bsb: to preserve his soul from the Pit and his life from perishing by the sword.

verses.esv: “Man is also rebuked with pain on his bed and with continual strife in his bones,
verses.kjv: He is chastened also with pain upon his bed, and the multitude of his bones with strong pain:
verses.bsb: A man is also chastened on his bed with pain and constant distress in his bones,

verses.esv: so that his life loathes bread, and his appetite the choicest food.
verses.kjv: So that his life abhorreth bread, and his soul dainty meat.
verses.bsb: so that he detests his bread, and his soul loathes his favorite food.

verses.esv: His flesh is so wasted away that it cannot be seen, and his bones that were not seen stick out.
verses.kjv: His flesh is consumed away, that it cannot be seen; and his bones that were not seen stick out.
verses.bsb: His flesh wastes away from sight, and his hidden bones protrude.

verses.esv: His soul draws near the pit, and his life to those who bring death.
verses.kjv: Yea, his soul draweth near unto the grave, and his life to the destroyers.
verses.bsb: He draws near to the Pit, and his life to the messengers of death.

verses.esv: If there be for him an angel, a mediator, one of the thousand, to declare to man what is right for him,
verses.kjv: If there be a messenger with him, an interpreter, one among a thousand, to shew unto man his uprightness:
verses.bsb: Yet if there is a messenger on his side, one mediator in a thousand, to tell a man what is right for him,

verses.esv: and he is merciful to him, and says, ‘Deliver him from going down into the pit; I have found a ransom;
verses.kjv: Then he is gracious unto him, and saith, Deliver him from going down to the pit: I have found a ransom.
verses.bsb: to be gracious to him and say, ‘Spare him from going down to the Pit; I have found his ransom,’

verses.esv: let his flesh become fresh with youth; let him return to the days of his youthful vigor’;
verses.kjv: His flesh shall be fresher than a child’s: he shall return to the days of his youth:
verses.bsb: then his flesh is refreshed like a child’s; he returns to the days of his youth.

verses.esv: then man prays to God, and he accepts him; he sees his face with a shout of joy, and he restores to man his righteousness.
verses.kjv: He shall pray unto God, and he will be favourable unto him: and he shall see his face with joy: for he will render unto man his righteousness.
verses.bsb: He prays to God and finds favor; he sees God’s face and shouts for joy, and God restores His righteousness to that man.

verses.esv: He sings before men and says: ‘I sinned and perverted what was right, and it was not repaid to me.
verses.kjv: He looketh upon men, and if any say, I have sinned, and perverted that which was right, and it profited me not;
verses.bsb: Then he sings before men with these words: ‘I have sinned and perverted what was right; yet I did not get what I deserved.

verses.esv: He has redeemed my soul from going down into the pit, and my life shall look upon the light.’
verses.kjv: He will deliver his soul from going into the pit, and his life shall see the light.
verses.bsb: He redeemed my soul from going down to the Pit, and I will live to see the light.’

verses.esv: “Behold, God does all these things, twice, three times, with a man,
verses.kjv: Lo, all these things worketh God oftentimes with man,
verses.bsb: Behold, all these things God does to a man, two or even three times,

verses.esv: to bring back his soul from the pit, that he may be lighted with the light of life.
verses.kjv: To bring back his soul from the pit, to be enlightened with the light of the living.
verses.bsb: to bring back his soul from the Pit, that he may be enlightened with the light of life.

verses.esv: Pay attention, O Job, listen to me; be silent, and I will speak.
verses.kjv: Mark well, O Job, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I will speak.
verses.bsb: Pay attention, Job, and listen to me; be silent, and I will speak.

verses.esv: If you have any words, answer me; speak, for I desire to justify you.
verses.kjv: If thou hast any thing to say, answer me: speak, for I desire to justify thee.
verses.bsb: But if you have something to say, answer me; speak up, for I would like to vindicate you.

verses.esv: If not, listen to me; be silent, and I will teach you wisdom.”
verses.kjv: If not, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I shall teach thee wisdom.
verses.bsb: But if not, then listen to me; be quiet, and I will teach you wisdom.”

verses.esv: Then Elihu answered and said:
verses.kjv: FURTHERMORE Elihu answered and said,
verses.bsb: Then Elihu continued:

verses.esv: “Hear my words, you wise men, and give ear to me, you who know;
verses.kjv: Hear my words, O ye wise men; and give ear unto me, ye that have knowledge.
verses.bsb: “Hear my words, O wise men; give ear to me, O men of learning.

verses.esv: for the ear tests words as the palate tastes food.
verses.kjv: For the ear trieth words, as the mouth tasteth meat.
verses.bsb: For the ear tests words as the mouth tastes food.

verses.esv: Let us choose what is right; let us know among ourselves what is good.
verses.kjv: Let us choose to us judgment: let us know among ourselves what is good.
verses.bsb: Let us choose for ourselves what is right; let us learn together what is good.

verses.esv: For Job has said, ‘I am in the right, and God has taken away my right;
verses.kjv: For Job hath said, I am righteous: and God hath taken away my judgment.
verses.bsb: For Job has declared, ‘I am righteous, yet God has deprived me of justice.

verses.esv: in spite of my right I am counted a liar; my wound is incurable, though I am without transgression.’
verses.kjv: Should I lie against my right? my wound is incurable without transgression.
verses.bsb: Would I lie about my case? My wound is incurable, though I am without transgression.’

verses.esv: What man is like Job, who drinks up scoffing like water,
verses.kjv: What man is like Job, who drinketh up scorning like water?
verses.bsb: What man is like Job, who drinks up derision like water?

verses.esv: who travels in company with evildoers and walks with wicked men?
verses.kjv: Which goeth in company with the workers of iniquity, and walketh with wicked men.
verses.bsb: He keeps company with evildoers and walks with wicked men.

verses.esv: For he has said, ‘It profits a man nothing that he should take delight in God.’
verses.kjv: For he hath said, It profiteth a man nothing that he should delight himself with God.
verses.bsb: For he has said, ‘It profits a man nothing that he should delight in God.’

verses.esv: “Therefore, hear me, you men of understanding: far be it from God that he should do wickedness, and from the Almighty that he should do wrong.
verses.kjv: Therefore hearken unto me, ye men of understanding: far be it from God, that he should do wickedness; and from the Almighty, that he should commit iniquity.
verses.bsb: Therefore listen to me, O men of understanding. Far be it from God to do wrong, and from the Almighty to act unjustly.

verses.esv: For according to the work of a man he will repay him, and according to his ways he will make it befall him.
verses.kjv: For the work of a man shall he render unto him, and cause every man to find according to his ways.
verses.bsb: For according to a man’s deeds He repays him; according to a man’s ways He brings consequences.

verses.esv: Of a truth, God will not do wickedly, and the Almighty will not pervert justice.
verses.kjv: Yea, surely God will not do wickedly, neither will the Almighty pervert judgment.
verses.bsb: Indeed, it is true that God does not act wickedly, and the Almighty does not pervert justice.

verses.esv: Who gave him charge over the earth, and who laid on him the whole world?
verses.kjv: Who hath given him a charge over the earth? or who hath disposed the whole world?
verses.bsb: Who gave Him charge over the earth? Who appointed Him over the whole world?

verses.esv: If he should set his heart to it and gather to himself his spirit and his breath,
verses.kjv: If he set his heart upon man, if he gather unto himself his spirit and his breath;
verses.bsb: If He were to set His heart to it and withdraw His Spirit and breath,

verses.esv: all flesh would perish together, and man would return to dust.
verses.kjv: All flesh shall perish together, and man shall turn again unto dust.
verses.bsb: all flesh would perish together and mankind would return to the dust.

verses.esv: “If you have understanding, hear this; listen to what I say.
verses.kjv: If now thou hast understanding, hear this: hearken to the voice of my words.
verses.bsb: If you have understanding, hear this; listen to my words.
