verses.esv: Shall one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn him who is righteous and mighty,
verses.kjv: Shall even he that hateth right govern? and wilt thou condemn him that is most just?
verses.bsb: Could one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn the just and mighty One,

verses.esv: who says to a king, ‘Worthless one,’ and to nobles, ‘Wicked man,’
verses.kjv: Is it fit to say to a king, Thou art wicked? and to princes, Ye are ungodly?
verses.bsb: who says to kings, ‘You are worthless!’ and to nobles, ‘You are wicked,’

verses.esv: who shows no partiality to princes, nor regards the rich more than the poor, for they are all the work of his hands?
verses.kjv: How much less to him that accepteth not the persons of princes, nor regardeth the rich more than the poor? for they all are the work of his hands.
verses.bsb: who is not partial to princes and does not favor rich over poor? For they are all the work of His hands.

verses.esv: In a moment they die; at midnight the people are shaken and pass away, and the mighty are taken away by no human hand.
verses.kjv: In a moment shall they die, and the people shall be troubled at midnight, and pass away: and the mighty shall be taken away without hand.
verses.bsb: They die in an instant, in the middle of the night. The people convulse and pass away; the mighty are removed without human hand.

verses.esv: “For his eyes are on the ways of a man, and he sees all his steps.
verses.kjv: For his eyes are upon the ways of man, and he seeth all his goings.
verses.bsb: For His eyes are on the ways of a man, and He sees his every step.

verses.esv: There is no gloom or deep darkness where evildoers may hide themselves.
verses.kjv: There is no darkness, nor shadow of death, where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.
verses.bsb: There is no darkness or deep shadow where the workers of iniquity can hide.

verses.esv: For God has no need to consider a man further, that he should go before God in judgment.
verses.kjv: For he will not lay upon man more than right; that he should enter into judgment with God.
verses.bsb: For God need not examine a man further or have him approach for judgment.

verses.esv: He shatters the mighty without investigation and sets others in their place.
verses.kjv: He shall break in pieces mighty men without number, and set others in their stead.
verses.bsb: He shatters the mighty without inquiry and sets up others in their place.

verses.esv: Thus, knowing their works, he overturns them in the night, and they are crushed.
verses.kjv: Therefore he knoweth their works, and he overturneth them in the night, so that they are destroyed.
verses.bsb: Therefore, He recognizes their deeds; He overthrows them in the night and they are crushed.

verses.esv: He strikes them for their wickedness in a place for all to see,
verses.kjv: He striketh them as wicked men in the open sight of others;
verses.bsb: He strikes them for their wickedness in full view,

verses.esv: because they turned aside from following him and had no regard for any of his ways,
verses.kjv: Because they turned back from him, and would not consider any of his ways:
verses.bsb: because they turned aside from Him and had no regard for any of His ways.

verses.esv: so that they caused the cry of the poor to come to him, and he heard the cry of the afflicted—
verses.kjv: So that they cause the cry of the poor to come unto him, and he heareth the cry of the afflicted.
verses.bsb: They caused the cry of the poor to come before Him, and He heard the outcry of the afflicted.

verses.esv: When he is quiet, who can condemn? When he hides his face, who can behold him, whether it be a nation or a man?—
verses.kjv: When he giveth quietness, who then can make trouble? and when he hideth his face, who then can behold him? whether it be done against a nation, or against a man only:
verses.bsb: But when He remains silent, who can condemn Him? When He hides His face, who can see Him? Yet He watches over both man and nation,

verses.esv: that a godless man should not reign, that he should not ensnare the people.
verses.kjv: That the hypocrite reign not, lest the people be ensnared.
verses.bsb: that godless men should not rule or lay snares for the people.

verses.esv: “For has anyone said to God, ‘I have borne punishment; I will not offend any more;
verses.kjv: Surely it is meet to be said unto God, I have borne chastisement, I will not offend any more:
verses.bsb: Suppose someone says to God, ‘I have endured my punishment; I will offend no more.

verses.esv: teach me what I do not see; if I have done iniquity, I will do it no more’?
verses.kjv: That which I see not teach thou me: if I have done iniquity, I will do no more.
verses.bsb: Teach me what I cannot see; if I have done wrong, I will not do it again.’

verses.esv: Will he then make repayment to suit you, because you reject it? For you must choose, and not I; therefore declare what you know.
verses.kjv: Should it be according to thy mind? he will recompense it, whether thou refuse, or whether thou choose; and not I: therefore speak what thou knowest.
verses.bsb: Should God repay you on your own terms when you have disavowed His? You must choose, not I; so tell me what you know.

verses.esv: Men of understanding will say to me, and the wise man who hears me will say:
verses.kjv: Let men of understanding tell me, and let a wise man hearken unto me.
verses.bsb: Men of understanding will declare to me, and the wise men who hear me will say:

verses.esv: ‘Job speaks without knowledge; his words are without insight.’
verses.kjv: Job hath spoken without knowledge, and his words were without wisdom.
verses.bsb: ‘Job speaks without knowledge; his words lack insight.’

verses.esv: Would that Job were tried to the end, because he answers like wicked men.
verses.kjv: My desire is that Job may be tried unto the end because of his answers for wicked men.
verses.bsb: If only Job were tried to the utmost for answering like a wicked man.

verses.esv: For he adds rebellion to his sin; he claps his hands among us and multiplies his words against God.”
verses.kjv: For he addeth rebellion unto his sin, he clappeth his hands among us, and multiplieth his words against God.
verses.bsb: For he adds rebellion to his sin; he claps his hands among us and multiplies his words against God.”

verses.esv: And Elihu answered and said:
verses.kjv: ELIHU spake moreover, and said,
verses.bsb: And Elihu went on to say:

verses.esv: “Do you think this to be just? Do you say, ‘It is my right before God,’
verses.kjv: Thinkest thou this to be right, that thou saidst, My righteousness is more than God’s?
verses.bsb: “Do you think this is just? You say, ‘I am more righteous than God.’

verses.esv: that you ask, ‘What advantage have I? How am I better off than if I had sinned?’
verses.kjv: For thou saidst, What advantage will it be unto thee? and, What profit shall I have, if I be cleansed from my sin?
verses.bsb: For you ask, ‘What does it profit me, and what benefit do I gain apart from sin?’

verses.esv: I will answer you and your friends with you.
verses.kjv: I will answer thee, and thy companions with thee.
verses.bsb: I will reply to you and to your friends as well.

verses.esv: Look at the heavens, and see; and behold the clouds, which are higher than you.
verses.kjv: Look unto the heavens, and see; and behold the clouds which are higher than thou.
verses.bsb: Look to the heavens and see; gaze at the clouds high above you.

verses.esv: If you have sinned, what do you accomplish against him? And if your transgressions are multiplied, what do you do to him?
verses.kjv: If thou sinnest, what doest thou against him? or if thy transgressions be multiplied, what doest thou unto him?
verses.bsb: If you sin, what do you accomplish against Him? If you multiply your transgressions, what do you do to Him?

verses.esv: If you are righteous, what do you give to him? Or what does he receive from your hand?
verses.kjv: If thou be righteous, what givest thou him? or what receiveth he of thine hand?
verses.bsb: If you are righteous, what do you give Him, or what does He receive from your hand?

verses.esv: Your wickedness concerns a man like yourself, and your righteousness a son of man.
verses.kjv: Thy wickedness may hurt a man as thou art; and thy righteousness may profit the son of man.
verses.bsb: Your wickedness affects only a man like yourself, and your righteousness only a son of man.

verses.esv: “Because of the multitude of oppressions people cry out; they call for help because of the arm of the mighty.
verses.kjv: By reason of the multitude of oppressions they make the oppressed to cry: they cry out by reason of the arm of the mighty.
verses.bsb: Men cry out under great oppression; they plead for relief from the arm of the mighty.

verses.esv: But none says, ‘Where is God my Maker, who gives songs in the night,
verses.kjv: But none saith, Where is God my maker, who giveth songs in the night;
verses.bsb: But no one asks, ‘Where is God my Maker, who gives us songs in the night,

verses.esv: who teaches us more than the beasts of the earth and makes us wiser than the birds of the heavens?’
verses.kjv: Who teacheth us more than the beasts of the earth, and maketh us wiser than the fowls of heaven?
verses.bsb: who teaches us more than the beasts of the earth and makes us wiser than the birds of the air?’

verses.esv: There they cry out, but he does not answer, because of the pride of evil men.
verses.kjv: There they cry, but none giveth answer, because of the pride of evil men.
verses.bsb: There they cry out, but He does not answer, because of the pride of evil men.

verses.esv: Surely God does not hear an empty cry, nor does the Almighty regard it.
verses.kjv: Surely God will not hear vanity, neither will the Almighty regard it.
verses.bsb: Surely God does not listen to empty pleas, and the Almighty does not take note of it.

verses.esv: How much less when you say that you do not see him, that the case is before him, and you are waiting for him!
verses.kjv: Although thou sayest thou shalt not see him, yet judgment is before him; therefore trust thou in him.
verses.bsb: How much less, then, when you say that you do not see Him, that your case is before Him and you must wait for Him,

verses.esv: And now, because his anger does not punish, and he does not take much note of transgression,
verses.kjv: But now, because it is not so, he hath visited in his anger; yet he knoweth it not in great extremity:
verses.bsb: and further, that in His anger He has not punished or taken much notice of folly!

verses.esv: Job opens his mouth in empty talk; he multiplies words without knowledge.”
verses.kjv: Therefore doth Job open his mouth in vain; he multiplieth words without knowledge.
verses.bsb: So Job opens his mouth in vain and multiplies words without knowledge.”

verses.esv: And Elihu continued, and said:
verses.kjv: ELIHU also proceeded, and said,
verses.bsb: And Elihu continued:

verses.esv: “Bear with me a little, and I will show you, for I have yet something to say on God's behalf.
verses.kjv: Suffer me a little, and I will shew thee that I have yet to speak on God’s behalf.
verses.bsb: “Bear with me a little longer, and I will show you that there is more to be said on God’s behalf.

verses.esv: I will get my knowledge from afar and ascribe righteousness to my Maker.
verses.kjv: I will fetch my knowledge from afar, and will ascribe righteousness to my Maker.
verses.bsb: I get my knowledge from afar, and I will ascribe justice to my Maker.

verses.esv: For truly my words are not false; one who is perfect in knowledge is with you.
verses.kjv: For truly my words shall not be false: he that is perfect in knowledge is with thee.
verses.bsb: For truly my words are free of falsehood; one perfect in knowledge is with you.

verses.esv: “Behold, God is mighty, and does not despise any; he is mighty in strength of understanding.
verses.kjv: Behold, God is mighty, and despiseth not any: he is mighty in strength and wisdom.
verses.bsb: Indeed, God is mighty, but He despises no one; He is mighty in strength of understanding.

verses.esv: He does not keep the wicked alive, but gives the afflicted their right.
verses.kjv: He preserveth not the life of the wicked: but giveth right to the poor.
verses.bsb: He does not keep the wicked alive, but He grants justice to the afflicted.

verses.esv: He does not withdraw his eyes from the righteous, but with kings on the throne he sets them forever, and they are exalted.
verses.kjv: He withdraweth not his eyes from the righteous: but with kings are they on the throne; yea, he doth establish them for ever, and they are exalted.
verses.bsb: He does not take His eyes off the righteous, but He enthrones them with kings and exalts them forever.

verses.esv: And if they are bound in chains and caught in the cords of affliction,
verses.kjv: And if they be bound in fetters, and be holden in cords of affliction;
verses.bsb: And if men are bound with chains, caught in cords of affliction,

verses.esv: then he declares to them their work and their transgressions, that they are behaving arrogantly.
verses.kjv: Then he sheweth them their work, and their transgressions that they have exceeded.
verses.bsb: then He tells them their deeds and how arrogantly they have transgressed.

verses.esv: He opens their ears to instruction and commands that they return from iniquity.
verses.kjv: He openeth also their ear to discipline, and commandeth that they return from iniquity.
verses.bsb: He opens their ears to correction and commands that they turn from iniquity.

verses.esv: If they listen and serve him, they complete their days in prosperity, and their years in pleasantness.
verses.kjv: If they obey and serve him, they shall spend their days in prosperity, and their years in pleasures.
verses.bsb: If they obey and serve Him, then they end their days in prosperity and their years in happiness.

verses.esv: But if they do not listen, they perish by the sword and die without knowledge.
verses.kjv: But if they obey not, they shall perish by the sword, and they shall die without knowledge.
verses.bsb: But if they do not obey, then they perish by the sword and die without knowledge.

verses.esv: “The godless in heart cherish anger; they do not cry for help when he binds them.
verses.kjv: But the hypocrites in heart heap up wrath: they cry not when he bindeth them.
verses.bsb: The godless in heart harbor resentment; even when He binds them, they do not cry for help.

verses.esv: They die in youth, and their life ends among the cult prostitutes.
verses.kjv: They die in youth, and their life is among the unclean.
verses.bsb: They die in their youth, among the male shrine prostitutes.

verses.esv: He delivers the afflicted by their affliction and opens their ear by adversity.
verses.kjv: He delivereth the poor in his affliction, and openeth their ears in oppression.
verses.bsb: God rescues the afflicted by their affliction and opens their ears in oppression.

verses.esv: He also allured you out of distress into a broad place where there was no cramping, and what was set on your table was full of fatness.
verses.kjv: Even so would he have removed thee out of the strait into a broad place, where there is no straitness; and that which should be set on thy table should be full of fatness.
verses.bsb: Indeed, He drew you from the jaws of distress to a spacious and broad place, to a table full of richness.

verses.esv: “But you are full of the judgment on the wicked; judgment and justice seize you.
verses.kjv: But thou hast fulfilled the judgment of the wicked: judgment and justice take hold on thee.
verses.bsb: But now you are laden with the judgment due the wicked; judgment and justice have seized you.

verses.esv: Beware lest wrath entice you into scoffing, and let not the greatness of the ransom turn you aside.
verses.kjv: Because there is wrath, beware lest he take thee away with his stroke: then a great ransom cannot deliver thee.
verses.bsb: Be careful that no one lures you with riches; do not let a large bribe lead you astray.

verses.esv: Will your cry for help avail to keep you from distress, or all the force of your strength?
verses.kjv: Will he esteem thy riches? no, not gold, nor all the forces of strength.
verses.bsb: Can your wealth or all your mighty effort keep you from distress?

verses.esv: Do not long for the night, when peoples vanish in their place.
verses.kjv: Desire not the night, when people are cut off in their place.
verses.bsb: Do not long for the night, when people vanish from their homes.

verses.esv: Take care; do not turn to iniquity, for this you have chosen rather than affliction.
verses.kjv: Take heed, regard not iniquity: for this hast thou chosen rather than affliction.
verses.bsb: Be careful not to turn to iniquity, for this you have preferred to affliction.

verses.esv: Behold, God is exalted in his power; who is a teacher like him?
verses.kjv: Behold, God exalteth by his power: who teacheth like him?
verses.bsb: Behold, God is exalted in His power. Who is a teacher like Him?

verses.esv: Who has prescribed for him his way, or who can say, ‘You have done wrong’?
verses.kjv: Who hath enjoined him his way? or who can say, Thou hast wrought iniquity?
verses.bsb: Who has appointed His way for Him, or told Him, ‘You have done wrong’?

verses.esv: “Remember to extol his work, of which men have sung.
verses.kjv: Remember that thou magnify his work, which men behold.
verses.bsb: Remember to magnify His work, which men have praised in song.

verses.esv: All mankind has looked on it; man beholds it from afar.
verses.kjv: Every man may see it; man may behold it afar off.
verses.bsb: All mankind has seen it; men behold it from afar.

verses.esv: Behold, God is great, and we know him not; the number of his years is unsearchable.
verses.kjv: Behold, God is great, and we know him not, neither can the number of his years be searched out.
verses.bsb: Indeed, God is great—beyond our knowledge; the number of His years is unsearchable.

verses.esv: For he draws up the drops of water; they distill his mist in rain,
verses.kjv: For he maketh small the drops of water: they pour down rain according to the vapour thereof:
verses.bsb: For He draws up drops of water which distill the rain from the mist,

verses.esv: which the skies pour down and drop on mankind abundantly.
verses.kjv: Which the clouds do drop and distil upon man abundantly.
verses.bsb: which the clouds pour out and shower abundantly on mankind.

verses.esv: Can anyone understand the spreading of the clouds, the thunderings of his pavilion?
verses.kjv: Also can any understand the spreadings of the clouds, or the noise of his tabernacle?
verses.bsb: Furthermore, who can understand how the clouds spread out, how the thunder roars from His pavilion?

verses.esv: Behold, he scatters his lightning about him and covers the roots of the sea.
verses.kjv: Behold, he spreadeth his light upon it, and covereth the bottom of the sea.
verses.bsb: See how He scatters His lightning around Him and covers the depths of the sea.

verses.esv: For by these he judges peoples; he gives food in abundance.
verses.kjv: For by them judgeth he the people; he giveth meat in abundance.
verses.bsb: For by these He judges the nations and provides food in abundance.

verses.esv: He covers his hands with the lightning and commands it to strike the mark.
verses.kjv: With clouds he covereth the light; and commandeth it not to shine by the cloud that cometh betwixt.
verses.bsb: He fills His hands with lightning and commands it to strike its mark.

verses.esv: Its crashing declares his presence; the cattle also declare that he rises.
verses.kjv: The noise thereof sheweth concerning it, the cattle also concerning the vapour.
verses.bsb: The thunder declares His presence; even the cattle regard the rising storm.

verses.esv: “At this also my heart trembles and leaps out of its place.
verses.kjv: AT this also my heart trembleth, and is moved out of his place.
verses.bsb: “At this my heart also pounds and leaps from its place.

verses.esv: Keep listening to the thunder of his voice and the rumbling that comes from his mouth.
verses.kjv: Hear attentively the noise of his voice, and the sound that goeth out of his mouth.
verses.bsb: Listen closely to the thunder of His voice and the rumbling that comes from His mouth.

verses.esv: Under the whole heaven he lets it go, and his lightning to the corners of the earth.
verses.kjv: He directeth it under the whole heaven, and his lightning unto the ends of the earth.
verses.bsb: He unleashes His lightning beneath the whole sky and sends it to the ends of the earth.

verses.esv: After it his voice roars; he thunders with his majestic voice, and he does not restrain the lightnings when his voice is heard.
verses.kjv: After it a voice roareth: he thundereth with the voice of his excellency; and he will not stay them when his voice is heard.
verses.bsb: Then there comes a roaring sound; He thunders with His majestic voice. He does not restrain the lightning when His voice resounds.

verses.esv: God thunders wondrously with his voice; he does great things that we cannot comprehend.
verses.kjv: God thundereth marvellously with his voice; great things doeth he, which we cannot comprehend.
verses.bsb: God thunders wondrously with His voice; He does great things we cannot comprehend.

verses.esv: For to the snow he says, ‘Fall on the earth,’ likewise to the downpour, his mighty downpour.
verses.kjv: For he saith to the snow, Be thou on the earth; likewise to the small rain, and to the great rain of his strength.
verses.bsb: For He says to the snow, ‘Fall on the earth,’ and to the gentle rain, ‘Pour out a mighty downpour.’

verses.esv: He seals up the hand of every man, that all men whom he made may know it.
verses.kjv: He sealeth up the hand of every man; that all men may know his work.
verses.bsb: He seals up the hand of every man, so that all men may know His work.

verses.esv: Then the beasts go into their lairs, and remain in their dens.
verses.kjv: Then the beasts go into dens, and remain in their places.
verses.bsb: The wild animals enter their lairs; they settle down in their dens.

verses.esv: From its chamber comes the whirlwind, and cold from the scattering winds.
verses.kjv: Out of the south cometh the whirlwind: and cold out of the north.
verses.bsb: The tempest comes from its chamber, and the cold from the driving north winds.

verses.esv: By the breath of God ice is given, and the broad waters are frozen fast.
verses.kjv: By the breath of God frost is given: and the breadth of the waters is straitened.
verses.bsb: By the breath of God the ice is formed and the watery expanses are frozen.

verses.esv: He loads the thick cloud with moisture; the clouds scatter his lightning.
verses.kjv: Also by watering he wearieth the thick cloud: he scattereth his bright cloud:
verses.bsb: He loads the clouds with moisture; He scatters His lightning through them.

verses.esv: They turn around and around by his guidance, to accomplish all that he commands them on the face of the habitable world.
verses.kjv: And it is turned round about by his counsels: that they may do whatsoever he commandeth them upon the face of the world in the earth.
verses.bsb: They swirl about, whirling at His direction, accomplishing all that He commands over the face of all the earth.

verses.esv: Whether for correction or for his land or for love, he causes it to happen.
verses.kjv: He causeth it to come, whether for correction, or for his land, or for mercy.
verses.bsb: Whether for punishment or for His land, He accomplishes this in His loving devotion.

verses.esv: “Hear this, O Job; stop and consider the wondrous works of God.
verses.kjv: Hearken unto this, O Job: stand still, and consider the wondrous works of God.
verses.bsb: Listen to this, O Job; stand still and consider the wonders of God.

verses.esv: Do you know how God lays his command upon them and causes the lightning of his cloud to shine?
verses.kjv: Dost thou know when God disposed them, and caused the light of his cloud to shine?
verses.bsb: Do you know how God dispatches the clouds or makes the lightning flash?

verses.esv: Do you know the balancings of the clouds, the wondrous works of him who is perfect in knowledge,
verses.kjv: Dost thou know the balancings of the clouds, the wondrous works of him which is perfect in knowledge?
verses.bsb: Do you understand how the clouds float, those wonders of Him who is perfect in knowledge?

verses.esv: you whose garments are hot when the earth is still because of the south wind?
verses.kjv: How thy garments are warm, when he quieteth the earth by the south wind?
verses.bsb: You whose clothes get hot when the land lies hushed under the south wind,

verses.esv: Can you, like him, spread out the skies, hard as a cast metal mirror?
verses.kjv: Hast thou with him spread out the sky, which is strong, and as a molten looking glass?
verses.bsb: can you, like Him, spread out the skies to reflect the heat like a mirror of bronze?

verses.esv: Teach us what we shall say to him; we cannot draw up our case because of darkness.
verses.kjv: Teach us what we shall say unto him; for we cannot order our speech by reason of darkness.
verses.bsb: Teach us what we should say to Him; we cannot draw up our case when our faces are in darkness.

verses.esv: Shall it be told him that I would speak? Did a man ever wish that he would be swallowed up?
verses.kjv: Shall it be told him that I speak? if a man speak, surely he shall be swallowed up.
verses.bsb: Should He be told that I want to speak? Would a man ask to be swallowed up?

verses.esv: “And now no one looks on the light when it is bright in the skies, when the wind has passed and cleared them.
verses.kjv: And now men see not the bright light which is in the clouds: but the wind passeth, and cleanseth them.
verses.bsb: Now no one can gaze at the sun when it is bright in the skies after the wind has swept them clean.

verses.esv: Out of the north comes golden splendor; God is clothed with awesome majesty.
verses.kjv: Fair weather cometh out of the north: with God is terrible majesty.
verses.bsb: Out of the north He comes in golden splendor; awesome majesty surrounds Him.

verses.esv: The Almighty—we cannot find him; he is great in power; justice and abundant righteousness he will not violate.
verses.kjv: Touching the Almighty, we cannot find him out: he is excellent in power, and in judgment, and in plenty of justice: he will not afflict.
verses.bsb: The Almighty is beyond our reach; He is exalted in power! In His justice and great righteousness He does not oppress.

verses.esv: Therefore men fear him; he does not regard any who are wise in their own conceit.”
verses.kjv: Men do therefore fear him: he respecteth not any that are wise of heart.
verses.bsb: Therefore, men fear Him, for He is not partial to the wise in heart.”

verses.esv: Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind and said:
verses.kjv: THEN the Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind, and said,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind and said:

verses.esv: “Who is this that darkens counsel by words without knowledge?
verses.kjv: Who is this that darkeneth counsel by words without knowledge?
verses.bsb: “Who is this who obscures My counsel by words without knowledge?

verses.esv: Dress for action like a man; I will question you, and you make it known to me.
verses.kjv: Gird up now thy loins like a man; for I will demand of thee, and answer thou me.
verses.bsb: Now brace yourself like a man; I will question you, and you shall inform Me.

verses.esv: “Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth? Tell me, if you have understanding.
verses.kjv: Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth? declare, if thou hast understanding.
verses.bsb: Where were you when I laid the foundations of the earth? Tell Me, if you have understanding.

verses.esv: Who determined its measurements—surely you know! Or who stretched the line upon it?
verses.kjv: Who hath laid the measures thereof, if thou knowest? or who hath stretched the line upon it?
verses.bsb: Who fixed its measurements? Surely you know! Or who stretched a measuring line across it?

verses.esv: On what were its bases sunk, or who laid its cornerstone,
verses.kjv: Whereupon are the foundations thereof fastened? or who laid the corner stone thereof;
verses.bsb: On what were its foundations set, or who laid its cornerstone,

verses.esv: when the morning stars sang together and all the sons of God shouted for joy?
verses.kjv: When the morning stars sang together, and all the sons of God shouted for joy?
verses.bsb: while the morning stars sang together and all the sons of God shouted for joy?

verses.esv: “Or who shut in the sea with doors when it burst out from the womb,
verses.kjv: Or who shut up the sea with doors, when it brake forth, as if it had issued out of the womb?
verses.bsb: Who enclosed the sea behind doors when it burst forth from the womb,

verses.esv: when I made clouds its garment and thick darkness its swaddling band,
verses.kjv: When I made the cloud the garment thereof, and thick darkness a swaddlingband for it,
verses.bsb: when I made the clouds its garment and thick darkness its blanket,

verses.esv: and prescribed limits for it and set bars and doors,
verses.kjv: And brake up for it my decreed place, and set bars and doors,
verses.bsb: when I fixed its boundaries and set in place its bars and doors,

verses.esv: and said, ‘Thus far shall you come, and no farther, and here shall your proud waves be stayed’?
verses.kjv: And said, Hitherto shalt thou come, but no further: and here shall thy proud waves be stayed?
verses.bsb: and I declared: ‘You may come this far, but no farther; here your proud waves must stop’?

verses.esv: “Have you commanded the morning since your days began, and caused the dawn to know its place,
verses.kjv: Hast thou commanded the morning since thy days; and caused the dayspring to know his place;
verses.bsb: In your days, have you commanded the morning or assigned the dawn its place,

verses.esv: that it might take hold of the skirts of the earth, and the wicked be shaken out of it?
verses.kjv: That it might take hold of the ends of the earth, that the wicked might be shaken out of it?
verses.bsb: that it might spread to the ends of the earth and shake the wicked out of it?

verses.esv: It is changed like clay under the seal, and its features stand out like a garment.
verses.kjv: It is turned as clay to the seal; and they stand as a garment.
verses.bsb: The earth takes shape like clay under a seal; its hills stand out like the folds of a garment.

verses.esv: From the wicked their light is withheld, and their uplifted arm is broken.
verses.kjv: And from the wicked their light is withholden, and the high arm shall be broken.
verses.bsb: Light is withheld from the wicked, and their upraised arm is broken.

verses.esv: “Have you entered into the springs of the sea, or walked in the recesses of the deep?
verses.kjv: Hast thou entered into the springs of the sea? or hast thou walked in the search of the depth?
verses.bsb: Have you journeyed to the vents of the sea or walked in the trenches of the deep?

verses.esv: Have the gates of death been revealed to you, or have you seen the gates of deep darkness?
verses.kjv: Have the gates of death been opened unto thee? or hast thou seen the doors of the shadow of death?
verses.bsb: Have the gates of death been revealed to you? Have you seen the gates of the shadow of death?

verses.esv: Have you comprehended the expanse of the earth? Declare, if you know all this.
verses.kjv: Hast thou perceived the breadth of the earth? declare if thou knowest it all.
verses.bsb: Have you surveyed the extent of the earth? Tell Me, if you know all this.

verses.esv: “Where is the way to the dwelling of light, and where is the place of darkness,
verses.kjv: Where is the way where light dwelleth? and as for darkness, where is the place thereof,
verses.bsb: Where is the way to the home of light? Do you know where darkness resides,

verses.esv: that you may take it to its territory and that you may discern the paths to its home?
verses.kjv: That thou shouldest take it to the bound thereof, and that thou shouldest know the paths to the house thereof?
verses.bsb: so you can lead it back to its border? Do you know the paths to its home?

verses.esv: You know, for you were born then, and the number of your days is great!
verses.kjv: Knowest thou it, because thou wast then born? or because the number of thy days is great?
verses.bsb: Surely you know, for you were already born! And the number of your days is great!

verses.esv: “Have you entered the storehouses of the snow, or have you seen the storehouses of the hail,
verses.kjv: Hast thou entered into the treasures of the snow? or hast thou seen the treasures of the hail,
verses.bsb: Have you entered the storehouses of snow or observed the storehouses of hail,

verses.esv: which I have reserved for the time of trouble, for the day of battle and war?
verses.kjv: Which I have reserved against the time of trouble, against the day of battle and war?
verses.bsb: which I hold in reserve for times of trouble, for the day of war and battle?

verses.esv: What is the way to the place where the light is distributed, or where the east wind is scattered upon the earth?
verses.kjv: By what way is the light parted, which scattereth the east wind upon the earth?
verses.bsb: In which direction is the lightning dispersed, or the east wind scattered over the earth?

verses.esv: “Who has cleft a channel for the torrents of rain and a way for the thunderbolt,
verses.kjv: Who hath divided a watercourse for the overflowing of waters, or a way for the lightning of thunder;
verses.bsb: Who cuts a channel for the flood or clears a path for the thunderbolt,

verses.esv: to bring rain on a land where no man is, on the desert in which there is no man,
verses.kjv: To cause it to rain on the earth, where no man is; on the wilderness, wherein there is no man;
verses.bsb: to bring rain on a barren land, on a desert where no man lives,

verses.esv: to satisfy the waste and desolate land, and to make the ground sprout with grass?
verses.kjv: To satisfy the desolate and waste ground; and to cause the bud of the tender herb to spring forth?
verses.bsb: to satisfy the parched wasteland and make it sprout with tender grass?

verses.esv: “Has the rain a father, or who has begotten the drops of dew?
verses.kjv: Hath the rain a father? or who hath begotten the drops of dew?
verses.bsb: Does the rain have a father? Who has begotten the drops of dew?

verses.esv: From whose womb did the ice come forth, and who has given birth to the frost of heaven?
verses.kjv: Out of whose womb came the ice? and the hoary frost of heaven, who hath gendered it?
verses.bsb: From whose womb does the ice emerge? Who gives birth to the frost from heaven,

verses.esv: The waters become hard like stone, and the face of the deep is frozen.
verses.kjv: The waters are hid as with a stone, and the face of the deep is frozen.
verses.bsb: when the waters become hard as stone and the surface of the deep is frozen?

verses.esv: “Can you bind the chains of the Pleiades or loose the cords of Orion?
verses.kjv: Canst thou bind the sweet influences of Pleiades, or loose the bands of Orion?
verses.bsb: Can you bind the chains of the Pleiades or loosen the belt of Orion?

verses.esv: Can you lead forth the Mazzaroth in their season, or can you guide the Bear with its children?
verses.kjv: Canst thou bring forth Mazzaroth in his season? or canst thou guide Arcturus with his sons?
verses.bsb: Can you bring forth the constellations in their seasons or lead out the Bear and her cubs?

verses.esv: Do you know the ordinances of the heavens? Can you establish their rule on the earth?
verses.kjv: Knowest thou the ordinances of heaven? canst thou set the dominion thereof in the earth?
verses.bsb: Do you know the laws of the heavens? Can you set their dominion over the earth?

verses.esv: “Can you lift up your voice to the clouds, that a flood of waters may cover you?
verses.kjv: Canst thou lift up thy voice to the clouds, that abundance of waters may cover thee?
verses.bsb: Can you command the clouds so that a flood of water covers you?

verses.esv: Can you send forth lightnings, that they may go and say to you, ‘Here we are’?
verses.kjv: Canst thou send lightnings, that they may go, and say unto thee, Here we are?
verses.bsb: Can you send the lightning bolts on their way? Do they report to you, ‘Here we are’?

verses.esv: Who has put wisdom in the inward parts or given understanding to the mind?
verses.kjv: Who hath put wisdom in the inward parts? or who hath given understanding to the heart?
verses.bsb: Who has put wisdom in the heart or given understanding to the mind?

verses.esv: Who can number the clouds by wisdom? Or who can tilt the waterskins of the heavens,
verses.kjv: Who can number the clouds in wisdom? or who can stay the bottles of heaven,
verses.bsb: Who has the wisdom to count the clouds? Or who can tilt the water jars of the heavens

verses.esv: when the dust runs into a mass and the clods stick fast together?
verses.kjv: When the dust groweth into hardness, and the clods cleave fast together?
verses.bsb: when the dust hardens into a mass and the clods of earth stick together?

verses.esv: “Can you hunt the prey for the lion, or satisfy the appetite of the young lions,
verses.kjv: Wilt thou hunt the prey for the lion? or fill the appetite of the young lions,
verses.bsb: Can you hunt the prey for a lioness or satisfy the hunger of young lions

verses.esv: when they crouch in their dens or lie in wait in their thicket?
verses.kjv: When they couch in their dens, and abide in the covert to lie in wait?
verses.bsb: when they crouch in their dens and lie in wait in the thicket?

verses.esv: Who provides for the raven its prey, when its young ones cry to God for help, and wander about for lack of food? “Do you know when the mountain goats give birth? Do you observe the calving of the does?
verses.kjv: Who provideth for the raven his food? when his young ones cry unto God, they wander for lack of meat.
verses.bsb: Who provides food for the raven when its young cry out to God as they wander about for lack of food?

verses.esv: Can you number the months that they fulfill, and do you know the time when they give birth,
verses.kjv: Canst thou number the months that they fulfil? or knowest thou the time when they bring forth?
verses.bsb: Can you count the months they are pregnant? Do you know the time they give birth?

verses.esv: when they crouch, bring forth their offspring, and are delivered of their young?
verses.kjv: They bow themselves, they bring forth their young ones, they cast out their sorrows.
verses.bsb: They crouch down and bring forth their young; they deliver their newborn.

verses.esv: Their young ones become strong; they grow up in the open; they go out and do not return to them.
verses.kjv: Their young ones are in good liking, they grow up with corn; they go forth, and return not unto them.
verses.bsb: Their young ones thrive and grow up in the open field; they leave and do not return.

verses.esv: “Who has let the wild donkey go free? Who has loosed the bonds of the swift donkey,
verses.kjv: Who hath sent out the wild ass free? or who hath loosed the bands of the wild ass?
verses.bsb: Who set the wild donkey free? Who released the swift donkey from the harness?

verses.esv: to whom I have given the arid plain for his home and the salt land for his dwelling place?
verses.kjv: Whose house I have made the wilderness, and the barren land his dwellings.
verses.bsb: I made the wilderness his home and the salt flats his dwelling.

verses.esv: He scorns the tumult of the city; he hears not the shouts of the driver.
verses.kjv: He scorneth the multitude of the city, neither regardeth he the crying of the driver.
verses.bsb: He scorns the tumult of the city and never hears the shouts of a driver.

verses.esv: He ranges the mountains as his pasture, and he searches after every green thing.
verses.kjv: The range of the mountains is his pasture, and he searcheth after every green thing.
verses.bsb: He roams the mountains for pasture, searching for any green thing.

verses.esv: “Is the wild ox willing to serve you? Will he spend the night at your manger?
verses.kjv: Will the unicorn be willing to serve thee, or abide by thy crib?
verses.bsb: Will the wild ox consent to serve you? Will he stay by your manger at night?

verses.esv: Can you bind him in the furrow with ropes, or will he harrow the valleys after you?
verses.kjv: Canst thou bind the unicorn with his band in the furrow? or will he harrow the valleys after thee?
verses.bsb: Can you hold him to the furrow with a harness? Will he plow the valleys behind you?

verses.esv: Will you depend on him because his strength is great, and will you leave to him your labor?
verses.kjv: Wilt thou trust him, because his strength is great? or wilt thou leave thy labour to him?
verses.bsb: Can you rely on his great strength? Will you leave your hard work to him?

verses.esv: Do you have faith in him that he will return your grain and gather it to your threshing floor?
verses.kjv: Wilt thou believe him, that he will bring home thy seed, and gather it into thy barn?
verses.bsb: Can you trust him to bring in your grain and gather it to your threshing floor?

verses.esv: “The wings of the ostrich wave proudly, but are they the pinions and plumage of love?
verses.kjv: Gavest thou the goodly wings unto the peacocks? or wings and feathers unto the ostrich?
verses.bsb: The wings of the ostrich flap joyfully, but cannot match the pinions and feathers of the stork.

verses.esv: For she leaves her eggs to the earth and lets them be warmed on the ground,
verses.kjv: Which leaveth her eggs in the earth, and warmeth them in dust,
verses.bsb: For she leaves her eggs on the ground and lets them warm in the sand.

verses.esv: forgetting that a foot may crush them and that the wild beast may trample them.
verses.kjv: And forgetteth that the foot may crush them, or that the wild beast may break them.
verses.bsb: She forgets that a foot may crush them, or a wild animal may trample them.

verses.esv: She deals cruelly with her young, as if they were not hers; though her labor be in vain, yet she has no fear,
verses.kjv: She is hardened against her young ones, as though they were not hers: her labour is in vain without fear;
verses.bsb: She treats her young harshly, as if not her own, with no concern that her labor was in vain.

verses.esv: because God has made her forget wisdom and given her no share in understanding.
verses.kjv: Because God hath deprived her of wisdom, neither hath he imparted to her understanding.
verses.bsb: For God has deprived her of wisdom; He has not endowed her with understanding.

verses.esv: When she rouses herself to flee, she laughs at the horse and his rider.
verses.kjv: What time she lifteth up herself on high, she scorneth the horse and his rider.
verses.bsb: Yet when she proudly spreads her wings, she laughs at the horse and its rider.

verses.esv: “Do you give the horse his might? Do you clothe his neck with a mane?
verses.kjv: Hast thou given the horse strength? hast thou clothed his neck with thunder?
verses.bsb: Do you give strength to the horse or adorn his neck with a mane?

verses.esv: Do you make him leap like the locust? His majestic snorting is terrifying.
verses.kjv: Canst thou make him afraid as a grasshopper? the glory of his nostrils is terrible.
verses.bsb: Do you make him leap like a locust, striking terror with his proud snorting?

verses.esv: He paws in the valley and exults in his strength; he goes out to meet the weapons.
verses.kjv: He paweth in the valley, and rejoiceth in his strength: he goeth on to meet the armed men.
verses.bsb: He paws in the valley and rejoices in his strength; he charges into battle.

verses.esv: He laughs at fear and is not dismayed; he does not turn back from the sword.
verses.kjv: He mocketh at fear, and is not affrighted; neither turneth he back from the sword.
verses.bsb: He laughs at fear, frightened of nothing; he does not turn back from the sword.

verses.esv: Upon him rattle the quiver, the flashing spear, and the javelin.
verses.kjv: The quiver rattleth against him, the glittering spear and the shield.
verses.bsb: A quiver rattles at his side, along with a flashing spear and lance.

verses.esv: With fierceness and rage he swallows the ground; he cannot stand still at the sound of the trumpet.
verses.kjv: He swalloweth the ground with fierceness and rage: neither believeth he that it is the sound of the trumpet.
verses.bsb: Trembling with excitement, he devours the distance; he cannot stand still when the ram’s horn sounds.

verses.esv: When the trumpet sounds, he says ‘Aha!’ He smells the battle from afar, the thunder of the captains, and the shouting.
verses.kjv: He saith among the trumpets, Ha, ha; and he smelleth the battle afar off, the thunder of the captains, and the shouting.
verses.bsb: At the blast of the horn, he snorts with fervor. He catches the scent of battle from afar— the shouts of captains and the cry of war.

verses.esv: “Is it by your understanding that the hawk soars and spreads his wings toward the south?
verses.kjv: Doth the hawk fly by thy wisdom, and stretch her wings toward the south?
verses.bsb: Does the hawk take flight by your understanding and spread his wings toward the south?

verses.esv: Is it at your command that the eagle mounts up and makes his nest on high?
verses.kjv: Doth the eagle mount up at thy command, and make her nest on high?
verses.bsb: Does the eagle soar at your command and make his nest on high?

verses.esv: On the rock he dwells and makes his home, on the rocky crag and stronghold.
verses.kjv: She dwelleth and abideth on the rock, upon the crag of the rock, and the strong place.
verses.bsb: He dwells on a cliff and lodges there; his stronghold is on a rocky crag.

verses.esv: From there he spies out the prey; his eyes behold it from far away.
verses.kjv: From thence she seeketh the prey, and her eyes behold afar off.
verses.bsb: From there he spies out food; his eyes see it from afar.

verses.esv: His young ones suck up blood, and where the slain are, there is he.” And the LORD said to Job:
verses.kjv: Her young ones also suck up blood: and where the slain are, there is she.
verses.bsb: His young ones feast on blood; and where the slain are, there he is.”

verses.esv: “Shall a faultfinder contend with the Almighty? He who argues with God, let him answer it.”
verses.kjv: Shall he that contendeth with the Almighty instruct him? he that reproveth God, let him answer it.
verses.bsb: “Will the faultfinder contend with the Almighty? Let him who argues with God give an answer.”

verses.esv: Then Job answered the LORD and said:
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
verses.bsb: Then Job answered the LORD:

verses.esv: “Behold, I am of small account; what shall I answer you? I lay my hand on my mouth.
verses.kjv: Behold, I am vile; what shall I answer thee? I will lay mine hand upon my mouth.
verses.bsb: “Behold, I am insignificant. How can I reply to You? I place my hand over my mouth.

verses.esv: I have spoken once, and I will not answer; twice, but I will proceed no further.”
verses.kjv: Once have I spoken; but I will not answer: yea, twice; but I will proceed no further.
verses.bsb: I have spoken once, but I have no answer— twice, but I have nothing to add.”

verses.esv: Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind and said:
verses.kjv: ¶ Then answered the Lord unto Job out of the whirlwind, and said,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind and said:

verses.esv: “Dress for action like a man; I will question you, and you make it known to me.
verses.kjv: Gird up thy loins now like a man: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.
verses.bsb: “Now brace yourself like a man; I will question you, and you shall inform Me.

verses.esv: Will you even put me in the wrong? Will you condemn me that you may be in the right?
verses.kjv: Wilt thou also disannul my judgment? wilt thou condemn me, that thou mayest be righteous?
verses.bsb: Would you really annul My justice? Would you condemn Me to justify yourself?

verses.esv: Have you an arm like God, and can you thunder with a voice like his?
verses.kjv: Hast thou an arm like God? or canst thou thunder with a voice like him?
verses.bsb: Do you have an arm like God’s? Can you thunder with a voice like His?

verses.esv: “Adorn yourself with majesty and dignity; clothe yourself with glory and splendor.
verses.kjv: Deck thyself now with majesty and excellency; and array thyself with glory and beauty.
verses.bsb: Then adorn yourself with majesty and splendor, and clothe yourself with honor and glory.

verses.esv: Pour out the overflowings of your anger, and look on everyone who is proud and abase him.
verses.kjv: Cast abroad the rage of thy wrath: and behold every one that is proud, and abase him.
verses.bsb: Unleash the fury of your wrath; look on every proud man and bring him low.

verses.esv: Look on everyone who is proud and bring him low and tread down the wicked where they stand.
verses.kjv: Look on every one that is proud, and bring him low; and tread down the wicked in their place.
verses.bsb: Look on every proud man and humble him; trample the wicked where they stand.

verses.esv: Hide them all in the dust together; bind their faces in the world below.
verses.kjv: Hide them in the dust together; and bind their faces in secret.
verses.bsb: Bury them together in the dust; imprison them in the grave.

verses.esv: Then will I also acknowledge to you that your own right hand can save you.
verses.kjv: Then will I also confess unto thee that thine own right hand can save thee.
verses.bsb: Then I will confess to you that your own right hand can save you.

verses.esv: “Behold, Behemoth, which I made as I made you; he eats grass like an ox.
verses.kjv: ¶ Behold now behemoth, which I made with thee; he eateth grass as an ox.
verses.bsb: Look at Behemoth, which I made along with you. He feeds on grass like an ox.

verses.esv: Behold, his strength in his loins, and his power in the muscles of his belly.
verses.kjv: Lo now, his strength is in his loins, and his force is in the navel of his belly.
verses.bsb: See the strength of his loins and the power in the muscles of his belly.

verses.esv: He makes his tail stiff like a cedar; the sinews of his thighs are knit together.
verses.kjv: He moveth his tail like a cedar: the sinews of his stones are wrapped together.
verses.bsb: His tail sways like a cedar; the sinews of his thighs are tightly knit.

verses.esv: His bones are tubes of bronze, his limbs like bars of iron.
verses.kjv: His bones are as strong pieces of brass; his bones are like bars of iron.
verses.bsb: His bones are tubes of bronze; his limbs are rods of iron.

verses.esv: “He is the first of the works of God; let him who made him bring near his sword!
verses.kjv: He is the chief of the ways of God: he that made him can make his sword to approach unto him.
verses.bsb: He is the foremost of God’s works; only his Maker can draw the sword against him.

verses.esv: For the mountains yield food for him where all the wild beasts play.
verses.kjv: Surely the mountains bring him forth food, where all the beasts of the field play.
verses.bsb: The hills yield him their produce, while all the beasts of the field play nearby.

verses.esv: Under the lotus plants he lies, in the shelter of the reeds and in the marsh.
verses.kjv: He lieth under the shady trees, in the covert of the reed, and fens.
verses.bsb: He lies under the lotus plants, hidden among the reeds of the marsh.

verses.esv: For his shade the lotus trees cover him; the willows of the brook surround him.
verses.kjv: The shady trees cover him with their shadow; the willows of the brook compass him about.
verses.bsb: The lotus plants conceal him in their shade; the willows of the brook surround him.

verses.esv: Behold, if the river is turbulent he is not frightened; he is confident though Jordan rushes against his mouth.
verses.kjv: Behold, he drinketh up a river, and hasteth not: he trusteth that he can draw up Jordan into his mouth.
verses.bsb: Though the river rages, Behemoth is unafraid; he remains secure, though the Jordan surges to his mouth.

verses.esv: Can one take him by his eyes, or pierce his nose with a snare? “Can you draw out Leviathan with a fishhook or press down his tongue with a cord?
verses.kjv: He taketh it with his eyes: his nose pierceth through snares.
verses.bsb: Can anyone capture him as he looks on, or pierce his nose with a snare?

verses.esv: Can you put a rope in his nose or pierce his jaw with a hook?
verses.kjv: Canst thou put an hook into his nose? or bore his jaw through with a thorn?
verses.bsb: Can you put a cord through his nose or pierce his jaw with a hook?

verses.esv: Will he make many pleas to you? Will he speak to you soft words?
verses.kjv: Will he make many supplications unto thee? will he speak soft words unto thee?
verses.bsb: Will he beg you for mercy or speak to you softly?

verses.esv: Will he make a covenant with you to take him for your servant forever?
verses.kjv: Will he make a covenant with thee? wilt thou take him for a servant for ever?
verses.bsb: Will he make a covenant with you to take him as a slave for life?

verses.esv: Will you play with him as with a bird, or will you put him on a leash for your girls?
verses.kjv: Wilt thou play with him as with a bird? or wilt thou bind him for thy maidens?
verses.bsb: Can you pet him like a bird or put him on a leash for your maidens?

verses.esv: Will traders bargain over him? Will they divide him up among the merchants?
verses.kjv: Shall the companions make a banquet of him? shall they part him among the merchants?
verses.bsb: Will traders barter for him or divide him among the merchants?

verses.esv: Can you fill his skin with harpoons or his head with fishing spears?
verses.kjv: Canst thou fill his skin with barbed irons? or his head with fish spears?
verses.bsb: Can you fill his hide with harpoons or his head with fishing spears?

verses.esv: Lay your hands on him; remember the battle—you will not do it again!
verses.kjv: Lay thine hand upon him, remember the battle, do no more.
verses.bsb: If you lay a hand on him, you will remember the battle and never repeat it!

verses.esv: Behold, the hope of a man is false; he is laid low even at the sight of him.
verses.kjv: Behold, the hope of him is in vain: shall not one be cast down even at the sight of him?
verses.bsb: Surely hope of overcoming him is false. Is not the sight of him overwhelming?

verses.esv: No one is so fierce that he dares to stir him up. Who then is he who can stand before me?
verses.kjv: None is so fierce that dare stir him up: who then is able to stand before me?
verses.bsb: No one is so fierce as to rouse Leviathan. Then who is able to stand against Me?

verses.esv: Who has first given to me, that I should repay him? Whatever is under the whole heaven is mine.
verses.kjv: Who hath prevented me, that I should repay him? whatsoever is under the whole heaven is mine.
verses.bsb: Who has given to Me that I should repay him? Everything under heaven is Mine.

verses.esv: “I will not keep silence concerning his limbs, or his mighty strength, or his goodly frame.
verses.kjv: I will not conceal his parts, nor his power, nor his comely proportion.
verses.bsb: I cannot keep silent about his limbs, his power and graceful form.

verses.esv: Who can strip off his outer garment? Who would come near him with a bridle?
verses.kjv: Who can discover the face of his garment? or who can come to him with his double bridle?
verses.bsb: Who can strip off his outer coat? Who can approach him with a bridle?

verses.esv: Who can open the doors of his face? Around his teeth is terror.
verses.kjv: Who can open the doors of his face? his teeth are terrible round about.
verses.bsb: Who can open his jaws, ringed by his fearsome teeth?

verses.esv: His back is made of rows of shields, shut up closely as with a seal.
verses.kjv: His scales are his pride, shut up together as with a close seal.
verses.bsb: His rows of scales are his pride, tightly sealed together.

verses.esv: One is so near to another that no air can come between them.
verses.kjv: One is so near to another, that no air can come between them.
verses.bsb: One scale is so near to another that no air can pass between them.

verses.esv: They are joined one to another; they clasp each other and cannot be separated.
verses.kjv: They are joined one to another, they stick together, that they cannot be sundered.
verses.bsb: They are joined to one another; they clasp and cannot be separated.

verses.esv: His sneezings flash forth light, and his eyes are like the eyelids of the dawn.
verses.kjv: By his neesings a light doth shine, and his eyes are like the eyelids of the morning.
verses.bsb: His snorting flashes with light, and his eyes are like the rays of dawn.

verses.esv: Out of his mouth go flaming torches; sparks of fire leap forth.
verses.kjv: Out of his mouth go burning lamps, and sparks of fire leap out.
verses.bsb: Firebrands stream from his mouth; fiery sparks shoot forth!

verses.esv: Out of his nostrils comes forth smoke, as from a boiling pot and burning rushes.
verses.kjv: Out of his nostrils goeth smoke, as out of a seething pot or caldron.
verses.bsb: Smoke billows from his nostrils as from a boiling pot over burning reeds.

verses.esv: His breath kindles coals, and a flame comes forth from his mouth.
verses.kjv: His breath kindleth coals, and a flame goeth out of his mouth.
verses.bsb: His breath sets coals ablaze, and flames pour from his mouth.

verses.esv: In his neck abides strength, and terror dances before him.
verses.kjv: In his neck remaineth strength, and sorrow is turned into joy before him.
verses.bsb: Strength resides in his neck, and dismay leaps before him.

verses.esv: The folds of his flesh stick together, firmly cast on him and immovable.
verses.kjv: The flakes of his flesh are joined together: they are firm in themselves; they cannot be moved.
verses.bsb: The folds of his flesh are tightly joined; they are firm and immovable.

verses.esv: His heart is hard as a stone, hard as the lower millstone.
verses.kjv: His heart is as firm as a stone; yea, as hard as a piece of the nether millstone.
verses.bsb: His chest is as hard as a rock, as hard as a lower millstone!

verses.esv: When he raises himself up, the mighty are afraid; at the crashing they are beside themselves.
verses.kjv: When he raiseth up himself, the mighty are afraid: by reason of breakings they purify themselves.
verses.bsb: When Leviathan rises up, the mighty are terrified; they withdraw before his thrashing.

verses.esv: Though the sword reaches him, it does not avail, nor the spear, the dart, or the javelin.
verses.kjv: The sword of him that layeth at him cannot hold: the spear, the dart, nor the habergeon.
verses.bsb: The sword that reaches him has no effect, nor does the spear or dart or arrow.

verses.esv: He counts iron as straw, and bronze as rotten wood.
verses.kjv: He esteemeth iron as straw, and brass as rotten wood.
verses.bsb: He regards iron as straw and bronze as rotten wood.

verses.esv: The arrow cannot make him flee; for him, sling stones are turned to stubble.
verses.kjv: The arrow cannot make him flee: slingstones are turned with him into stubble.
verses.bsb: No arrow can make him flee; slingstones become like chaff to him.

verses.esv: Clubs are counted as stubble; he laughs at the rattle of javelins.
verses.kjv: Darts are counted as stubble: he laugheth at the shaking of a spear.
verses.bsb: A club is regarded as straw, and he laughs at the sound of the lance.

verses.esv: His underparts are like sharp potsherds; he spreads himself like a threshing sledge on the mire.
verses.kjv: Sharp stones are under him: he spreadeth sharp pointed things upon the mire.
verses.bsb: His undersides are jagged potsherds, spreading out the mud like a threshing sledge.

verses.esv: He makes the deep boil like a pot; he makes the sea like a pot of ointment.
verses.kjv: He maketh the deep to boil like a pot: he maketh the sea like a pot of ointment.
verses.bsb: He makes the depths seethe like a cauldron; he makes the sea like a jar of ointment.

verses.esv: Behind him he leaves a shining wake; one would think the deep to be white-haired.
verses.kjv: He maketh a path to shine after him; one would think the deep to be hoary.
verses.bsb: He leaves a glistening wake behind him; one would think the deep had white hair!

verses.esv: On earth there is not his like, a creature without fear.
verses.kjv: Upon earth there is not his like, who is made without fear.
verses.bsb: Nothing on earth is his equal— a creature devoid of fear!

verses.esv: He sees everything that is high; he is king over all the sons of pride.”
verses.kjv: He beholdeth all high things: he is a king over all the children of pride.
verses.bsb: He looks down on all the haughty; he is king over all the proud.”

verses.esv: Then Job answered the LORD and said:
verses.kjv: THEN Job answered the Lord, and said,
verses.bsb: Then Job replied to the LORD:

verses.esv: “I know that you can do all things, and that no purpose of yours can be thwarted.
verses.kjv: I know that thou canst do every thing, and that no thought can be withholden from thee.
verses.bsb: “I know that You can do all things and that no plan of Yours can be thwarted.

verses.esv: ‘Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge?’ Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand, things too wonderful for me, which I did not know.
verses.kjv: Who is he that hideth counsel without knowledge? therefore have I uttered that I understood not; things too wonderful for me, which I knew not.
verses.bsb: You asked, ‘Who is this who conceals My counsel without knowledge?’ Surely I spoke of things I did not understand, things too wonderful for me to know.

verses.esv: ‘Hear, and I will speak; I will question you, and you make it known to me.’
verses.kjv: Hear, I beseech thee, and I will speak: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.
verses.bsb: You said, ‘Listen now, and I will speak. I will question you, and you shall inform Me.’

verses.esv: I had heard of you by the hearing of the ear, but now my eye sees you;
verses.kjv: I have heard of thee by the hearing of the ear: but now mine eye seeth thee.
verses.bsb: My ears had heard of You, but now my eyes have seen You.

verses.esv: therefore I despise myself, and repent in dust and ashes.”
verses.kjv: Wherefore I abhor myself, and repent in dust and ashes.
verses.bsb: Therefore I retract my words, and I repent in dust and ashes.”

verses.esv: After the LORD had spoken these words to Job, the LORD said to Eliphaz the Temanite: “My anger burns against you and against your two friends, for you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it was so, that after the Lord had spoken these words unto Job, the Lord said to Eliphaz the Temanite, My wrath is kindled against thee, and against thy two friends: for ye have not spoken of me the thing that is right, as my servant Job hath.
verses.bsb: After the LORD had spoken these words to Job, He said to Eliphaz the Temanite, “My wrath is kindled against you and your two friends. For you have not spoken about Me accurately, as My servant Job has.

verses.esv: Now therefore take seven bulls and seven rams and go to my servant Job and offer up a burnt offering for yourselves. And my servant Job shall pray for you, for I will accept his prayer not to deal with you according to your folly. For you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has.”
verses.kjv: Therefore take unto you now seven bullocks and seven rams, and go to my servant Job, and offer up for yourselves a burnt offering; and my servant Job shall pray for you: for him will I accept: lest I deal with you after your folly, in that ye have not spoken of me the thing which is right, like my servant Job.
verses.bsb: So now, take seven bulls and seven rams, go to My servant Job, and sacrifice a burnt offering for yourselves. Then My servant Job will pray for you, for I will accept his prayer and not deal with you according to your folly. For you have not spoken accurately about Me, as My servant Job has.”

verses.esv: So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did what the LORD had told them, and the LORD accepted Job's prayer.
verses.kjv: So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went, and did according as the Lord commanded them: the Lord also accepted Job.
verses.bsb: So Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite went and did as the LORD had told them; and the LORD accepted Job’s request.

verses.esv: And the LORD restored the fortunes of Job, when he had prayed for his friends. And the LORD gave Job twice as much as he had before.
verses.kjv: And the Lord turned the captivity of Job, when he prayed for his friends: also the Lord gave Job twice as much as he had before.
verses.bsb: After Job had prayed for his friends, the LORD restored his prosperity and doubled his former possessions.

verses.esv: Then came to him all his brothers and sisters and all who had known him before, and ate bread with him in his house. And they showed him sympathy and comforted him for all the evil that the LORD had brought upon him. And each of them gave him a piece of money and a ring of gold.
verses.kjv: Then came there unto him all his brethren, and all his sisters, and all they that had been of his acquaintance before, and did eat bread with him in his house: and they bemoaned him, and comforted him over all the evil that the Lord had brought upon him: every man also gave him a piece of money, and every one an earring of gold.
verses.bsb: All his brothers and sisters and prior acquaintances came and dined with him in his house. They consoled him and comforted him over all the adversity that the LORD had brought upon him. And each one gave him a piece of silver and a gold ring.

verses.esv: And the LORD blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning. And he had 14,000 sheep, 6,000 camels, 1,000 yoke of oxen, and 1,000 female donkeys.
verses.kjv: So the Lord blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning: for he had fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand she asses.
verses.bsb: So the LORD blessed Job’s latter days more than his first. He owned 14,000 sheep, 6,000 camels, 1,000 yoke of oxen, and 1,000 female donkeys.

verses.esv: He had also seven sons and three daughters.
verses.kjv: He had also seven sons and three daughters.
verses.bsb: And he also had seven sons and three daughters.

verses.esv: And he called the name of the first daughter Jemimah, and the name of the second Keziah, and the name of the third Keren-happuch.
verses.kjv: And he called the name of the first, Jemima; and the name of the second, Kezia; and the name of the third, Keren-happuch.
verses.bsb: He named his first daughter Jemimah, his second Keziah, and his third Keren-happuch.

verses.esv: And in all the land there were no women so beautiful as Job's daughters. And their father gave them an inheritance among their brothers.
verses.kjv: And in all the land were no women found so fair as the daughters of Job: and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren.
verses.bsb: No women as beautiful as Job’s daughters could be found in all the land, and their father granted them an inheritance among their brothers.

verses.esv: And after this Job lived 140 years, and saw his sons, and his sons' sons, four generations.
verses.kjv: After this lived Job an hundred and forty years, and saw his sons, and his sons’ sons, even four generations.
verses.bsb: After this, Job lived 140 years and saw his children and their children to the fourth generation.

verses.esv: And Job died, an old man, and full of days.
verses.kjv: So Job died, being old and full of days. THE
verses.bsb: And so Job died, old and full of years.
